74672ee7b2b02678: Model yourself on someone who acted steadily from reason, kept an even temper, sought real understanding of every matter before deciding, and bore unjust judgment without protest.
3e9d0ce399b9ef9f: Be slow to act, deaf to slander, careful in judging people, plain in speech, content with little, and able to endure hardship and stay constant in friendship.

33168d4d2b21c35e: Rouse yourself from your habitual illusions, and once awake, view the things of the world with the same detachment you feel toward a dream you have just left.

6c287024613153e2: Do not treat harsh things as opposed to what you revere, but trace all of it back to its single source. Whoever has seen the present has seen everything, since all things are of one kind and are folded into one another in mutual agreement.
1ba499b3dd623e03: Things follow from one another by physical motion, by natural sympathy, and by the unity of all substance.
029ad3f5a331ff1f: Adapt yourself to the circumstances you have been given and genuinely love the people you must live among. A tool keeps working after its maker is gone, but in natural things the shaping power still lives inside them.

8050d40bd581bddd: If you count as good and evil only what depends on your own will, you will have no reason left to resent heaven or to be at war with anyone.
982161089f21e248: Everyone contributes to what the world is doing, even those who sleep through it and even those who actively resist, so the only real question is which sort of contributor you choose to be.
a97fb978f31e7b7f: The universe will make use of you whether you cooperate or not, so do not be its ridiculous part. Each thing has its own function, and the sun does not try to do the rain's work.
9357496123cb986c: Things that differ completely and have separate functions still work together toward a single end.
e5774b5eebf9b171: If a wise providence decided what happens to me, I should accept it, since wisdom would not harm me for no benefit; and if it decided only about the whole, I am still bound to accept what follows for me.

6353d308575f0bfc: What benefits you is what accords with your own nature, which is to act rationally and to be kind to your fellow members of the community; and since your city as a human being is the whole world, only what serves that community is genuinely good for you.
22e0aa8b9fad4f80: Whatever happens to any individual serves the whole. And when we say something is beneficial in the ordinary sense, we usually mean indifferent things like health and wealth.
32bf340d0fb18f52: The same spectacle repeated becomes tedious, and everything you will see in life is the same show from the same causes, so ask yourself when it will ever be enough.
1f182441505fc0f5: Keep constantly before you the deaths of people of every kind, from entertainers to philosophers to generals, kings, and geniuses, since all of them have gone the same way you are going.
1572f46f4f1918b9: Even those who mocked life's brevity are long dead, and those whose names vanished entirely are none the worse for it; the one thing genuinely worth doing is to deal truthfully, justly, and gently with dishonest and unjust people.
6fbe4b3738ea6217: When you need cheering, call to mind the specific virtues of the people you live among, because nothing lifts the spirit like living examples of goodness, so keep them always at hand.
7ed7de369eac9295: Complaining that your life is short makes no more sense than complaining that your body is not larger; accept your allotted span as calmly as you accept your allotted size.
33483231a0e1d350: Try to persuade people, but act as justice requires even against their will; if you are forcibly prevented, turn the same energy into patience and calm, and remember that you always wanted the outcome conditionally, never fixing your desire on the impossible.

bf55ae61cb7e200e: The ambitious locate their happiness in other people's applause, the pleasure-seeker in his own sensations, and the wise person in his own action.
165836e177147a88: You have the power to withhold your judgment about what happens, and grief disappears along with it, because external things cannot force any opinion on you by themselves.
bfbdd90b1782f7a5: When someone speaks to you, listen so completely that no other thought intrudes, as though your attention were fastened to their mind.
a8ea38439743442d: What harms the community cannot be good for the individual member of it.
c4dc49aa2841425f: People judge a service by whether it achieves its proper end, and nothing else.
e820890019da8c42: How many of the people born when I was born are already gone.

dd515bc63d02a8a1: No one can prevent you from living as your own nature requires, and nothing can happen to you that the nature of the whole does not require.
4a9b9aa1c5496ad7: Notice who the people are that the ambitious want to impress, what they want from them, and by what means, and notice how quickly time buries all of it.
140c2d1eb3ce3fee: Wickedness is nothing you have not already seen many times, so when something disturbs you, remind yourself that it is familiar, since everything that exists is both commonplace and short-lived.
485e4a19e1ff9f67: Your principles stay alive as long as you keep refreshing the impressions they rest on, and since you always have the power to judge rightly about whatever happens, nothing outside your own understanding has any claim on you.
e90c497f97290a14: Hold to this attitude and you will be on the right course.
515c89fc79eb0e25: You can give yourself right now what most people would beg to receive after death, namely to live again, since living again would only mean watching the same spectacle over: crowds and shows, quarrels and appetites, people scurrying like ants or jerked about like puppets.
5970307855e34ff8: Stand among these things steadily and without indignation, understanding that a person's worth is exactly the worth of the things they care about.
2976abdfea29c1bd: Take in what is said word by word and what is done purpose by purpose, asking what each thing truly means rather than accepting the meaning common usage gives it.
a5a9e3aee726c246: Ask whether your own reason is adequate to the task; if it is, use it as a natural instrument without seeking applause, and if it is not, either hand the work to someone better able or seek help, so long as the common good gets done.
e462dab99c71b895: Aim at what is good for the community rather than at praise, since praise and those who give it are quickly forgotten. Never be ashamed to accept help in doing what you must do.
a43ebc09d13bd01c: Do not abandon a worthwhile task, or pursue it with less spirit, merely because you cannot accomplish it alone.
e04dbd8ffdf1f2dc: Do not let the future disturb you, since when it arrives you will meet it with the same reason that makes the present bearable. Everything in the world is bound together, so nothing is alien to anything else.
0a7fa62b8fb7a543: All things are arranged into a single ordered whole, each keeping its place, and one reason and one law run through every rational being.
2c59c64077fefeab: Your body dissolves back into universal matter, your animating principle back into universal reason, and your reputation is swallowed by the sheer length of time.
67f03855dfe9f730: For a rational being, acting according to nature and acting according to reason are the same act.
c9d6211d5f582ab3: Rational beings are limbs of one body, not merely separate parts alongside each other. Until you feel this, your kindness rests on mere propriety rather than on the recognition that doing good to others is doing good to yourself.
a77408c945d8c41a: External events can only affect what is external; nothing harms you unless you judge it harmful, and that judgment is always within your power to withhold.
0ea8f29a45d01073: Be good for the sake of your own nature, not in reaction to what anyone else does or says, the way a thing of value simply keeps its own character.
1543ec1058581f58: The mind never troubles itself; fear and grief enter only through false opinions you supply, and the body's pain need not become the mind's pain.
8dd2c07a9da33369: Only the soul can truly feel fear or grief, and only through the opinions it accepts. The mind is sufficient to itself, so nothing can obstruct it unless it obstructs itself.
cf4df642008461c2: Happiness is simply having a good spirit within you. Recognize opinion for the uninvited intruder it is, and dismiss it without resentment.
3380072bfbf3c519: Fearing change is absurd, since nothing that exists came to be without it, and death is just one more instance of the change nature requires.
0e211009b2c45f8e: All particular bodies pass through the substance of the universe like a torrent, and time has already swallowed the greatest people who ever lived. Recall this whenever your mind fixes too earnestly on any person or affair.
5d9fbd2d2267208d: Care only that you never do anything contrary to your nature as a human being. You will soon be forgotten, so while you last, practice the thing most proper to a human: loving even those who wrong you, remembering they are your kin.
5f019d186455cb5c: People wrong you through ignorance and against their will, and both of you will soon be gone. Nor have they truly harmed you, since your own mind is not made worse by what they do.
9079929ae8e1306c: Nature endlessly reshapes the same matter into new forms, each lasting only briefly. If it was no hardship to be assembled, it is no hardship to be taken apart.
f75d7277335dc71a: Anger is contrary to both nature and reason. It is not enough merely to stop feeling it; you must understand why it is irrational, since a life without the comfort of a clear conscience is not worth continuing.
7299e8a742449146: Everything you see lasts only a moment, and nature perpetually breaks things down and remakes their substance into new things, so that the world stays forever fresh.
f86c6add59a330d1: When someone wrongs you, ask what he took to be good and evil when he did it. Once you see his mistake you will pity rather than resent him, since either you share his error and must forgive it, or you have outgrown it and can only be gentle toward a man who is confused.
6ee3410438062a9f: You cannot help being gentle toward someone who is simply mistaken.
758f94be7d27cc5b: Do not treat imagined future things as if they were present; instead, appreciate what you actually have by picturing its absence. But be careful not to prize it so highly that losing it would destroy your peace.
bef143d5447a4e6a: A mind that acts justly and thereby rests at peace with itself is fully satisfied and needs nothing else.
1be01769a16053bc: Strip away opinion, restrain your impulses, confine yourself to the present moment, examine what happens and break it down into its form and matter, and keep death in view. Leave other people's guilt where it belongs, treat everything between virtue and vice as indifferent, love humanity, and obey the divine order.
f489b8ebdea2e7a8: Everything happens according to an order, and death is only dissolution of one kind or another. Severe pain ends quickly and lasting pain is bearable, while the mind can shut off its sympathy with the body and keep its own tranquility intact.
9c1d750dda6e0c5b: Let the suffering body complain if it can. Before you crave anyone's praise, examine what sort of mind is praising you, and remember that everything is buried by what comes after, as new sand buries the old on the shore.
f1da429d7206fb2b: A mind accustomed to contemplating all of time and all of existence cannot regard this brief life as a great matter, nor death as a terrible one.
4305a2caf45dc0a8: It is noble to do good and be spoken ill of. It is shameful to take such care shaping your face while leaving your mind unshaped.
f264df4c407bebc4: Anger at circumstances is futile, since they cannot feel it and you only make yourself ridiculous. Life is cut down like ripe grain, and the right response is to endure without lamenting, so long as justice is on your side.
9c4a7f4879519082: Anyone of real worth does not treat life and death as the great danger, but asks only whether his actions are just, and holds the post he has taken up regardless of the risk.
af394bd70122ab67: Fear doing something shameful more than you fear death. True nobility and happiness lie somewhere other than in preserving your life or clinging to its length.
abf546a169c3b75f: Since no one escapes death, the only thing worth caring about is living as well and as virtuously as you can while you are here. Contemplating the vast motions of the stars and the transformations of the elements washes away the grime of ordinary earthly concerns.
8514689353af5dd7: Look down on human affairs as if from a height, seeing all the labors, ceremonies, and struggles at once, and notice how the jumble of contradictory things together makes up the beauty of the whole.
e8d3005943d00e8e: Past ages show the same patterns that future ages will repeat, so a man who watches the world for forty years sees no less than one who watches it for ten thousand.
1c6fdf16be25ec07: At death whatever came from the earth returns to earth and whatever came from above returns there, or else the atoms simply scatter. No remedy or charm can turn aside that necessity, however hard we struggle.
c3177a751b94ae7d: Another person's physical strength or skill is irrelevant to your worth; the only comparison that matters is whether he is more generous, more modest, more even-tempered, or more forgiving than you.
d7fbc0ad90257184: Where you can act according to the reason shared by gods and humans, there is no cause for grief, because the benefit of an action rightly undertaken is certain and no real damage can follow.
79462ab8d2d1feb5: In every place and at every moment it is within your power to accept what happens, deal justly with the people in front of you, and examine each impression carefully before letting it slip in unexamined.
59d2b93735a4a2ba: Do not peer into other people's minds; look straight ahead where nature leads you and do what your own constitution ordains. Lesser things exist for the sake of better ones, and rational beings exist for one another.
f3eac13a599a4eeb: The first duty of a human being is to intend the common good. The second is to refuse the demands of the body, since reason was made to rule the senses and appetites rather than serve them.
046296988e2a5d1a: The third duty proper to a human being is to avoid rashness and error. A mind that pursues these ends without distraction has reached its purpose, and with it, happiness.
f19e7c7e00dad5b0: Live as though you had already died, treating what remains as a bonus to be spent on virtue and loving whatever fate brings. When misfortune strikes, remember all the people who met the same thing with grief and complaint, and who are now dead and gone.

a41e398858a13a68: Look within, where the source of everything good lies; it is a spring that never fails so long as you keep digging deeper.

324eb85e54e7846b: Consider carefully what sort of minds belong to the people whose approval you crave, and what they pursue and avoid. Once you understand their opinions, you will neither resent their offenses nor miss their applause, since no one willingly gives up the truth.
95ff63a5750ed609: Understanding why people go wrong makes you far gentler toward all of them.
741d363fa5c6bc69: Pain is nothing to be ashamed of and cannot corrupt the mind that governs you, since it touches neither your substance nor your purpose. Remember that it is neither unbearable nor everlasting, provided you do not add opinion to it.
f724a8a4a4084de9: Many small discomforts that we never call pain, like restless sleep, heat, or loss of appetite, defeat us unnoticed because we never armed ourselves against them. When one of them makes you discontented, recognize that pain has beaten you and your courage has failed.
81126db0a3e63180: Never let yourself feel toward even genuinely wicked people the way ordinary people habitually feel toward one another.
ade8e1fc88c9fd2b: Outward achievements and impressive conduct tell you nothing reliable about whether a person was truly great.
9afcb770e50736bb: What matters is the quality of a person's soul: whether he sought only to act justly toward people and reverently toward the divine, neither tormenting himself over others' wickedness nor going along with it out of fear or friendship, and neither surprised nor overwhelmed by whatever befell him.

5db2434a332afce2: A person can be genuinely excellent and completely unknown, because true happiness depends on very few things. Failing to become a good logician or scientist is no obstacle to being generous, modest, kind, and obedient to the divine order.

022bf1b9a33db495: Even if the world is governed by chance, you can still exercise deliberate judgment over your own affairs, and that is enough.
9fe4a994609d241c: Everything, including the earth itself, is swept along in ceaseless change, and seeing this clearly makes worldly concerns look trivial.
abde1cb5f3fda34a: People who make a public show of seriousness and virtue are often hollow; do what your nature requires now, without worrying whether anyone notices.

3c227be2066ff136: Mentally rise above your life and survey the whole sweep of humanity — past, present, and future, in all its variety and simultaneity.

ddb0cff6e8725fea: Much of what distresses you exists only in your opinion of it, and you can simply drop those opinions.
f050b7baa2aecfb4: Every existing thing is brief compared to the infinite time before and after it, so dying old and dying young come to the same thing.
a6c0c6852bf8cd22: If you could see into people's minds, you would find their praise and their insults are equally products of mere opinion.
7280eefd1f2f7f80: Loss is nothing but change, which is what nature does and has always done; to call it evil is to condemn the whole universe as broken.
0e83429896a8811c: The things we prize most — fine stone, gold, royal clothing, even life itself — are, seen materially, just ordinary stuff arranged temporarily.

5441a64a1865bf7c: Nothing that happens to you is genuinely new, so examine whether its cause or its substance really warrants your complaint.
ec4c2f4a1979705b: Seeing the same pattern of things for three years teaches you as much as seeing it for a hundred.
78aa8ed0fa5d715b: Another person's wrongdoing harms them, not you — and you may be mistaken that they did wrong at all.

bbb300ae96ec84aa: Your mind is not the same kind of thing as your body, so do not assume it shares the body's fate.
cd621d407fda7176: If the gods can help at all, ask them not for external things but for freedom from the fear and craving that make you need those things.

92b0951722755f21: Instead of praying to get what you want, pray to stop wanting it; instead of praying to avoid loss, pray to stop fearing it.
7f203a3782499ffa: In illness, dwell not on the illness but on how the mind can remain undisturbed and happy even while the body suffers.

87f28ccae6889c65: Every craft demands full attention on the present task and the tool at hand.
d7bb3ce188861918: It is impossible for the world to contain no shameless people, so encountering one is not grounds for surprise or anger — this makes it easier to be gentle with each particular offender.
acdfcf50efe23160: For every vice you meet, nature has equipped you with a corresponding virtue; you can try to correct the person's error, and in any case their error does not damage you.

9b6135cf368dd0a9: Aim for a character that neither resents anyone for what they do nor gives anyone just cause to condemn you.
3d1884b9c3fc1765: Check any proposed act against your nature at each level — living, sensing, reasoning — and remember that being reasonable means being social.
c45dd471cf12fc3e: Whatever happens, you can either bear it or it will destroy you and end itself with you; and anything you can believe tolerable, you are in fact able to bear.

33479f8d1a58add5: The same chain of causes that produced you also produced everything that happens to you.
285884e170108b74: You are a part of a natural whole and kin to everyone like you; as a part, nothing that serves the whole can truly harm you.
731ed28932fd629e: Nature cannot produce and nurture what damages itself, so nothing that happens within it is grounds for resentment.
e2e1b285d504d253: Because you are kin to other humans, aim every deliberation at the common good, as a good citizen does.
641693d55d3c4984: Living that way is what a happy life consists of.
27419154d66976ed: Every part of the world must eventually change; if that change were truly harmful, the whole would be built to injure itself.

108fc19057e2ac1d: The universe either burns away and restarts or renews itself endlessly; and you are already not the matter you were born with, being like a river continuously replenished.

69911981a3e3c685: Once you claim titles like good, modest, and true, live up to them or lose the right to them — and if you slip, return at once.
783397db062c979b: Rising above pain, pleasure, reputation, and death as matters of indifference, without craving credit for it, remakes you into a new person.
2ce4b19bf770961c: Continuing a life of self-inflicted turmoil out of attachment to it is like a mauled gladiator begging to be sent back into the arena tomorrow.

e2445f88b3581e5e: Any departure from life should be calm and deliberate rather than passionate; what the divine asks is not flattery but that we become like it.
1b9b959bbcb3fffd: As a fig tree, a dog, and a bee each do what their nature fits them for, so a human should do what being human requires.
c453a205161bbc0a: Daily distraction and drudgery will gradually erase your principles unless you guard them, and much of what you observe you fail to actually use.

316b81c3f4920ae0: Aim to know each thing plainly: what it is made of, what it is for, how long it lasts, and who can give or take it.
fc426c5b2491b563: Conquerors preening over defeated nations are, in the end, doing what a spider does over a fly.
8840cbf820fffe06: Nothing produces greatness of soul so reliably as a practiced habit of seeing how all things change into one another.

a74b8f30c74af0db: His single occupation was to go straight where reason pointed.

db1f4d5900b4e09a: Acting rightly is happiness, since it is the only thing we can genuinely fail at.
a445fd7e638ec4a0: The person who follows reason is at once unhurried and quick, cheerful and serious.
ad538a0c4fe26e45: Doing good is its own benefit, so it is foolish to demand recognition or repayment on top of it.
51e21c93993ea265: Everything that happens follows from the ordering of the universe, and rational beings are the proper objects of whatever particular care it takes; holding this in mind brings calm.

283ed4da3e552dcf: Stop caring about reputation and live according to your nature, since clever argument, wealth, honor, and pleasure have already failed to make you happy.
1a227f5a64f03d96: Living well follows from holding true beliefs about good and evil: nothing is good but what makes a person just, temperate, courageous, and generous, and nothing is evil but what does the opposite.

4a779b333846ef17: Conquerors ruled only over external things and were enslaved by their own ignorance, while those who understood the nature of things had real mastery.

932568e95864591c: Say what is true kindly, modestly, and without pretense.
c015663ebc69149e: The universe does nothing but move things, change them, and take them away; nothing that happens is ever new, so nothing need be feared.

45346f908181f845: Nothing is truly yours to own — not your family, your body, or your life — and since you only ever live in the present moment, death can take nothing from you but this instant.
165aa543cacd7841: Everyone who once burned with rage, fame, or hatred is now forgotten, so the objects of such intensity were never worth it.
d9e6e692a60f9427: It is better to act justly and moderately in each situation than to chase what people strive for; and the worst conceit is being proud of your own humility.
b923fac896db319e: Belief can be justified by consistent experience of something's effects, even when the thing itself is never directly seen — as with the soul.
b9dc1c263f9c8e35: A good life consists in understanding the true nature of things, acting justly, and speaking truthfully, lived out as an unbroken succession of good actions.
5154bc4482f3776e: One substance, one life, and one intelligence underlie the world, only appearing divided because they are distributed among separate bodies.
2144a979a9237ca6: Non-rational things have no natural affinity for one another, but every rational mind is drawn toward others of its kind, and that bond cannot be broken by physical separation.
a760883dac14fefd: None of the specific functions of living — sensation, appetite, growth, even speech and reasoning — is worth wanting for its own sake; what matters is following reason, so grieving that death ends these things is irrational.
f3e96cd9aa9c6ada: Given how tiny a share of time, matter, and space each of us occupies, nothing matters except doing what your own nature requires and accepting what universal nature brings.
33a10b5b2946128f: Only the current state of your own mind is genuinely yours; everything outside your will has no real bearing on you.
46ed5ebe1b94826b: Death is not fearful to someone who counts as good only what nature makes timely, who cares about the quality rather than the quantity of his actions, and who knows the world offers nothing new with more years.
d73dee3a58ba0176: You have lived exactly as long as nature's terms allowed, so being dismissed from life is no injustice; a complete life is however long it happens to be.
c72860bf179376a9: The length of your life is set by the same power that made you, so accept your ending without complaint.
297d6095a7cf88c4: Your judgments, intentions, and desires are wholly in your control, while your body, possessions, and reputation are not; treating what is outside your control as yours guarantees frustration and blame.
048f4d2a65cab833: If you claim only what is genuinely yours, no one can coerce or harm you; but this freedom requires giving up ambition for external goods rather than trying to have both.
8e033ccce2a209bf: Meet every troubling impression by first testing whether it concerns something in your control, and if not, dismiss it as nothing to you.
d3d2d2ab3f0fcb95: Desiring what you cannot control guarantees disappointment, and fearing what you cannot avoid guarantees misery, so restrict aversion to your own faults and, until you are trained, suspend desire altogether.
b196c6396ff73ce9: Since desire for what is not yours must be disappointed, pursue and avoid only what you must, and do so with restraint.
0c5275f386a94560: Remind yourself of the true, perishable nature of what you love, so that losing it does not destroy you.
aaee680117b26f02: Before any undertaking, rehearse the annoyances it typically involves, and set your aim on acting well rather than on the outcome — then interference cannot upset you.
e4b1c6657868af40: People are disturbed not by events but by their interpretations of events, so blame for distress belongs to your own opinions; maturity moves from blaming others, to blaming yourself, to blaming no one.
aac1a17d13c6820b: Take no pride in qualities that belong to something you merely possess; the only thing genuinely your own is how you use your impressions.
3d663311d66111d1: Enjoy the goods life hands you lightly, keeping your attention on the fact that you will be called away, and never let attachment make you cling when the summons comes.
cf18bba7e5cbb998: Stop demanding that reality match your wishes; instead wish for reality as it is, and life goes well.
9ec97044f2722be4: Physical impairments obstruct the body, not the will, unless you let them; nothing that happens obstructs the real you.
d35a1d06d4709cb6: Every event arrives paired with a capacity in you suited to meet it — temptation with self-control, pain with endurance, insult with patience.
83e515deb854dd00: Treat every loss as a return of something you were only lent, and never as something taken from you; who takes it back, and by what means, is irrelevant.
0ad7d9a9904b5f6a: Peace of mind has a price in worldly outcomes, and paying it is worth more than the losses; practice on small ones, and accept that people over whom you have no control will not always do what you want.
b6738c767edd22e2: Letting another person's behavior disturb you harms you and does them no good either.
3ee9f00a5ea6b3e9: Progress requires accepting a reputation for foolishness, because you cannot simultaneously perfect your character and secure external success.
da3e6b246e45b1ee: Wishing that people and circumstances were other than their nature allows is folly; whoever controls what you want or fear controls you, so freedom means wanting nothing that depends on others.
56127e460a8b4b3f: Take what life offers as it comes to you — a moderate share, without grasping at what passes by — and the ability to decline it altogether is higher still.
65901319f73792eb: When someone grieves, recognize that his interpretation, not the event, is what wounds him; sympathize outwardly with him, but do not adopt his grief inwardly.
2ea29b894fdc4b0a: Your role in life is assigned, not chosen; your only task is to play whatever role you're given well.
8d295bbd6c75b893: No external sign or event has power to harm you, because you can always choose to extract some benefit from whatever occurs.
b55609797482887d: You become unbeatable by competing only in contests you control; so pursue freedom rather than status, and envy disappears.
2ef6c44aff6dc83d: No one insults you — your judgment that something is insulting does; buy yourself time before reacting and you will regain control.
ac5e750fd1047fe4: Keep death and other supposed catastrophes constantly in view, and you will neither grovel nor crave anything excessively.
5656f4c484bdfe96: Expect ridicule when you commit to a serious way of life, and persist without arrogance; wavering under mockery earns you contempt twice over.
8530f0fbf18b5de5: The moment you chase external validation you have abandoned your way of life; it suffices to be what you are in your own eyes.
9216e693a892acc0: Obscurity is not an evil, since another person cannot make you bad any more than he can make you base; being significant in what is actually yours is what counts.
1588804cadab96d8: You cannot give others what you do not have, and acquiring money or influence at the cost of your integrity trades a real good for a false one — a trustworthy friend is worth more.
632a8e66c1ef6f2f: Serving your community does not mean providing it material things outside your role; supplying it with one more honorable citizen is a genuine contribution.
5a200a0c3f5ea957: You cannot serve your country by sacrificing the integrity that makes you worth having as a citizen.
9e6135f08f39db9b: Honors and invitations have a price paid in flattery and servility, so it is unreasonable to resent those who paid it while you refused; you keep what you did not spend.
4f2b2e29786540e0: Someone who obtains a benefit by paying its price has gained nothing over you, since you retain what you declined to spend — your freedom from flattery and indignity.
c66a0493022e0fd7: Declining to flatter and being spared humiliation are themselves worth having.
d07f3035bef0daeb: Notice how calmly you accept the same misfortune when it happens to someone else, and hold yourself to that same standard when it happens to you.
470f931de4d0c095: Evil is not a real feature of the world, only a failure to hit the mark, just as a target exists for hitting, not missing.
774dbe79d4fe5a31: You would resent someone handing over your body to a stranger, yet you hand over your mind to anyone who insults you; and before undertaking anything, count its full costs, or you will quit in shame once they appear.
e53fce31fb83fa12: Any serious pursuit demands hardship and may still end in defeat, so reckon the full cost before committing; otherwise you are only imitating what impressed you, like a child at play.
e849b6a7eae23305: Whoever adopts pursuits on impulse abandons each as soon as its novelty wears off, and does nothing in earnest; first examine what the thing requires and what you can actually endure.
b3d4e6ff5de725b0: People are suited to different things, and philosophy demands real sacrifice — of appetites, company, status, and dignity — so undertake it only if you would trade all that for peace of mind.
80d1b00d286dc59c: You cannot cultivate your inner character and external success at once; choose one and be consistent, because a divided life amounts to nothing.
6a8879a95a8114ad: Your obligations follow from your relationships, not from the other person's merit — a bad father is still your father — and no one can injure you unless you assent to being injured.
0effa02efb439b77: Every social role you occupy — neighbor, citizen, subordinate — carries duties you can derive directly from the relationship itself.
a877a6bcc849291c: Genuine piety means holding true beliefs about the divine and willingly accepting whatever happens; this is only possible once you locate good and evil solely in what you control.
2f1241d974868d77: If you locate good and evil in external things, you will inevitably blame whoever deprives you of them, since no creature can love the source of what it believes harms it.
a47ec4a045baeea7: People drift toward temptation deliberately, choosing the hours and places where they are least likely to be seen.
591281fc377c451b: Temptation is restless and public, waiting wherever people pass rather than staying quietly at home.
17115ec910d7c839: Seduction often arrives wearing the appearance of piety and moral obligation fulfilled.
5f9bff0cd71ef469: Flattery works by convincing you that you were sought out specifically, and that elaborate preparations were made for you alone.
d746bdb34ecc1cf9: Temptation promises pleasure without consequence by pointing out that no one who matters is watching.
8a44ef16d8bb048c: Persuasive speech can compel a person more effectively than force.
807209bff7a053f0: A person walking into ruin usually goes willingly and calmly, unaware that the cost is his life.
a29c8052c6cdd7e4: Avoiding destructive paths requires refusing them at the level of desire, before your feet ever turn that way.
e7079433de4dd069: Many strong people have been destroyed by this, and the path leads reliably to death.
6f2e0a607236b46c: Wisdom is not hidden; it calls out publicly, in the busiest and most visible places.
eb0042f7a23a6fe0: Wisdom addresses everyone, and especially those who currently lack it.
020cd0dee76402c0: What wisdom says is true, and it has no use for wickedness.
cc7ca686b1c1e698: Wisdom's teachings contain nothing crooked, and they are obvious to anyone willing to understand them.
aa3dbd2b18fcb13f: Knowledge and instruction are worth more than any amount of money, and more than anything else a person could want.
565461c305bd71ef: Wisdom lives alongside practical good sense, and to revere what is good is to hate arrogance, corruption, and deceitful speech.
20441d0b46bcf1d3: Sound judgment is the real source of power, and every legitimate authority governs by means of it.
78751cc7ef65241b: Those who seek wisdom early find it, and it brings honor and a lasting kind of wealth.
c35ce086d9f5f195: What wisdom yields is better than gold, because it leads a person along the path of justice.
e124789f5ddb7e30: Wisdom enriches those who love it, and it existed before anything was made.
e059fd21e95ac41e: Wisdom predates the world itself, established before the seas and the springs existed.
65fae33683fe6fb1: Wisdom came into being before the mountains, the earth, and the fields.
65b588098d94573f: Wisdom was present at the making of the heavens, the clouds, and the deep.
b69c3ec5d264d8e4: Wisdom was there when the sea was given its limits and the earth its foundations.
135752ca665cbf49: Wisdom is the delight of creation itself, and it takes its own delight in human beings.
4ba4180e37b48eae: Those who follow wisdom's ways flourish, so accept instruction rather than refusing it.
b8fca3057bfa6a5b: Whoever pursues wisdom persistently and daily finds life itself.
744d862ef3a0cc3d: To act against wisdom is to harm yourself; those who hate it are choosing death.
c06d76d67470f2db: Wisdom has prepared a generous feast and built a house to hold it.
70baba984ba31c31: Wisdom actively invites the inexperienced and the uncomprehending to come and learn.
469ae484b451c7ef: Choosing understanding means abandoning foolish company, and that choice is what it means to live.
c31801903d5bfdbd: Correcting a contemptuous person earns you only insult, while correcting a wise person earns you their affection.
b1a29da721a1bd91: A wise person grows wiser from instruction, and reverence for what is good is where wisdom begins.
4ee3bb042aa4236e: Wisdom lengthens your life, and both its rewards and the costs of scorning it fall on you alone.
f39b1ae3a1dc7f7d: Folly is loud, ignorant, and positions itself in plain sight to call out to people already headed somewhere.
9d9422f85d4426f5: Folly's whole appeal is that what is stolen or secret tastes sweeter than what is earned.
48b0efe5697d7f8c: The person accepting folly's invitation does not know that everyone who came before is already dead.
dd59f953493d5f52: Wisdom in a child gladdens a parent while foolishness grieves them, and wealth gained by wrongdoing is worthless while integrity saves a life.
023d8d1fb176e350: The upright are sustained while the corrupt lose what they have, and laziness makes a person poor while diligence makes them rich.
a8e66e094b4716f4: Working when the work is available is wisdom, and sleeping through it is shameful.
2c90733f8112d4a5: A good person is remembered well while a wicked one's name decays, and the wise accept guidance while the babbling fool collapses.
055f9dfeca6db40b: Honest conduct is a secure way to walk, while crooked conduct is eventually exposed.
c28301d3d68ea3dd: What a good person says gives life to others, and while hatred provokes conflict, love covers over wrongs.
91fe6591e65ac6f5: Wisdom is found in the speech of the discerning, and the wise store up knowledge while fools talk themselves into ruin.
724bc8403a28934b: Wealth protects the rich while poverty is what destroys the poor, and honest work leads toward life.
59c9c883eade2379: Whoever accepts correction stays on the path of life, and whoever rejects it goes astray.
36b98465d2b39ce5: The more a person talks, the more likely they are to do wrong; restraint in speech is wisdom.
643526067e6132f9: What good people say nourishes many, while fools die from lacking sense.
2b16557858044e1f: A fool treats doing harm as entertainment, while a person of understanding takes pleasure in wisdom.
ad56dce5eb6b140d: The wicked pass like a storm and are gone, while the upright endure as a permanent foundation.
b5bb065967252ac4: Reverence lengthens life, and the hopes of good people end in joy while the expectations of the corrupt come to nothing.
f59492fc52cc2b31: The same path that strengthens the upright destroys those who do wrong.
2a9a2575a95096ba: Wisdom comes out of an honest person's mouth, and the honest know instinctively what is fitting to say.
bf151c21517aca65: Dishonest dealing is detestable and fair dealing is delightful, and pride brings disgrace while wisdom comes with humility.
25691483e9b1966c: Integrity guides a person while duplicity destroys them, and wealth is useless in a crisis while integrity saves a life.
007bbd56acf79407: Uprightness clears a person's path, while the wicked are brought down by their own wickedness and trapped by their own schemes.
1ac9631f5ed782d9: When a wicked person dies, everything they were counting on dies with them.
c7989246a2d6cb73: A hypocrite can destroy a neighbor with words, but knowledge protects the honest; a whole city celebrates when the upright do well.
dde1c62ba65b399b: A city rises through the influence of its honest citizens and is torn down by the speech of its corrupt ones.
27203d9cb99194da: A gossip exposes secrets while a trustworthy person keeps them, and a people without counsel falls while many advisors bring safety.
e4cf6a45b981f185: Guaranteeing a stranger's debt brings suffering, and refusing to do so is safety.
581f78f389fba965: Real sincerity never needs to be announced; it shows itself immediately in a person's face and voice, as unmistakably as a smell.
2c0dc90ca78f980c: Pretended sincerity is contemptible and treacherous friendship is the most shameful thing of all; genuine goodness cannot be concealed anyway, because it shows in the face.
f9a5a4dce3f579e5: Happiness is the mind's power to stay indifferent to things that are in themselves neither good nor bad, since external things impose no judgment on us — we manufacture the judgments ourselves, and we can decline to form them or erase them once formed.
ab077e98c5da32b7: Your vigilance is required only for the short span of a life, so welcome whatever accords with nature and pursue your own good without worrying how it looks.
72c239fb19ca8525: Consider of everything where it came from, what it is made of, and what it will change into, recognizing that nothing is harmed by changing.
5f26bc0a978fc477: Other people's faults need not disturb you: the universe is governed rather than random, worse things exist for the sake of better, and people act as they do because they are driven by the opinions they hold.
e86b5dc055cbf760: No one goes wrong willingly, so wrongdoers act against their own will out of ignorance — which is why they resent being accused of injustice — and you commit many of the same faults yourself.
a7f862763bdc9a5d: Not committing a wrong is not the same as being free of the disposition to it, since fear or vanity may be all that restrains you; and you often cannot actually tell whether another person has done wrong, because judging an action requires knowing far more than you do.
fe2b4c312c9804f7: Life is momentary, so grief and outrage are out of proportion; and what disturbs you is not another's wrongdoing but your own opinion that it is grievous, so drop the opinion and the anger goes with it.
80d4587f506e7874: Anger and grief usually cause more damage than the things that provoked them, and genuine gentleness — not the performed kind — cannot be defeated.
c1efe40d2b857e68: No one can sustain hostility against a person who stays kind and calmly explains that harming others harms the one who does it, since creatures made for cooperation do not injure their own kind.
cc94313e7256813a: Correct people privately and gently, never with mockery or for an audience; and flattering them is as uncharitable and damaging as raging at them.
364bdbee382cb6aa: Anger is not strength but a wound and a surrender to weakness, while gentleness is both more human and more powerful, because power lies in freedom from passion.
4ca942bbefb46434: It is insane to demand that no wicked people exist, and tyrannical to accept their existence in general while refusing to tolerate any wrong done to you.
bcc8e8df9bd6d29f: Watch your thoughts for four faults — being unnecessary, being unkind, merely echoing someone else, and letting your reason be enslaved to bodily appetite — and correct each one as you notice it.
61d8c2e28ab8df5d: Even the elements accept the place the universe assigns them against their own tendency, so it is shameful that your reason alone refuses to keep its place.
8005a5ff1b1f9ddb: Every movement of the mind toward injustice, indulgence, sorrow, or fear is a departure from its own nature, and so is resenting what happens by providence.
ca0ef2995f5bc77f: Justice and reverent acceptance are duties we owe as social beings, and without them we cannot live together — they are the source of all just action.
557bb8b16d540c9e: You cannot remain one consistent person without a single lifelong aim, and that aim has to be the common good rather than any privately conceived good.
9b7ad06b6c6139e9: Directing all your private purposes toward one shared end makes your actions coherent and keeps you the same person throughout.
4c9742407da1d679: Luxury bought at the price of constant fear is worse than a poor but peaceful life.
c0b25e2172e3bc71: Popular opinion is a bogeyman that frightens only those who have not grown up.
7f110d45847b94b3: Give strangers and guests the comfortable place and take whatever is left for yourself.
3f96ff97f7def07c: The worst fate is to receive a kindness you have no way of repaying.
c8085c259f333b5b: Keep some admirable person constantly before your mind as a model.
32a5388617bde3ac: Begin each day by looking at the stars, which do their work without deviation, in order, and with nothing hidden — and be like them.
868899891e941262: Dignity does not depend on how you are dressed; the embarrassment at outward poverty belongs to the onlooker, not to the person.
eb493259560a26de: Living well has to be taught, just as reading has to be taught; untaught, you are simply a slave to your senses and impulses.
ae265b1fdefc278f: You can smile inwardly at abuse, since people will find vicious words even for virtue itself.
42cefdac5ed0c664: Longing for children before you have them is as futile as longing for fruit out of season.
c59817a6330bdd77: Remind yourself when you embrace someone you love that they may die tomorrow; there is nothing ill-omened in naming a natural change, which turns a thing not into nothing but into what it is not yet.
2f5c76d9cd399168: Nothing can rob you of your own will; so practice careful judgment about what you agree to, keep your desires proportionate and provisional, avoid craving altogether, and reserve aversion for what actually depends on your choice.
3c7f3125e38fd8c2: What is at stake in philosophy is not trivia but whether you will be as mad as the crowd or genuinely sane; and people do not work at acquiring sound reason because they assume they already have it.
df635c591a459f0f: You can possess right now what you hope to reach later, by letting go of the past, entrusting the future to providence, and giving the present entirely to accepting what happens and to acting and speaking justly.
b4f710c604325fa5: Do not let other people's wickedness or opinions, nor your body's comfort, turn you aside; the thing to fear is not that you will one day stop living but that you will never begin to live as your nature requires.
b9e5e074e92d6ba8: Then you will stop feeling like a stranger in the world, stop being astonished by ordinary events, and stop anxiously depending on things outside your control.
56b6daa221827670: What is really you is your mind, stripped of body and possessions; disregarding your body, clothing, and dwelling frees you from an enormous amount of baggage.
8deeb0cd2c395dec: Only your mind is properly yours, so detach from it whatever others say and do, whatever you did in the past, anxiety about the future, and everything outside the reach of your own will.
4043d2e36c384e0d: A mind kept whole and free of entanglement — acting justly, accepting what comes, speaking the truth, and reckoning with no more life than the present moment — can spend the rest of its days untroubled.
6c37bc3d1df5c961: Though each of us loves himself most, we care more about our neighbors' opinion of us than our own, though we could not bear a single day of saying our thoughts aloud.
189a2a7d6f823fa6: If good people die and are never restored to life, that is not an oversight in the order of things; had it been fitting otherwise, it would have been arranged otherwise.
bb43cb645410b461: If the powers governing the world are just and good, then nothing in its arrangement can be unjust or unreasonable — and your very freedom to argue with them is evidence of their goodness.
8e0c773fe33f49af: Practice makes possible what you first despaired of, as an unused hand becomes stronger than the practiced one at a task it has been trained in.
99f9711f59228739: Regularly meditate on what kind of person death should find you being, on the brevity of life, on the vastness of the time before and after you, and on the frailty of every material thing once its outward disguise is removed.
d258a1d35e41d5f3: Every person is the sole cause of his own peace and cannot truly be obstructed by anyone else, because everything is opinion; hold your principles like a wrestler who cannot be disarmed rather than a swordsman who can lose his weapon.
3cf7635c752033b2: Analyze anything you encounter into its matter, its form, and its purpose.
1bd1bbe097a7f8ad: It is a remarkable good fortune that a person need do nothing but what accords with the divine order, and can accept contentedly whatever it sends.
4847a79b60e599c1: Blame no one: not the gods, who cannot err, and not people, who err only through ignorance and therefore against their will.
c2974a853a1eb133: It is absurd to be astonished at anything that happens in the ordinary course of nature.
6ec3b635e56c9fa9: Whether the world runs on unavoidable necessity, on a responsive providence, or on sheer chance, none of these gives you cause to complain — you cannot fight the first, you can earn the help of the second, and under the third you still possess a reason with which to govern yourself.
96211eead138eb51: The current can sweep away your body and circumstances but not your mind, and truth, justice, and self-control need not stop shining in you while you still exist.
61db2935e50c0177: When someone appears to have done wrong, ask whether it really was wrong, whether he has not already condemned himself, and remember that a corrupt person acting corruptly is as natural as a fig containing moisture — so pity him rather than rage at him, and correct him if you are able.
073e9d6c30c805fc: Do nothing that is not right and say nothing that is not true, and keep your intentions free of compulsion.
ef96d9d5e93da5b6: Take apart whatever confronts you into its form, its matter, its purpose, and the span of time it is allotted.
f1f6f24f45531f2e: Something in you is higher than your passions and appetites; act only with a deliberate purpose, and let that purpose be the common good, since everything you now see will shortly be changed and replaced.
2ef713a1ac79ffe0: Everything is opinion, and opinion is under your control; remove the opinion and you reach an immediate calm, like a ship arriving in a sheltered harbor.
8a0c1f0eced95688: An activity coming to an end suffers no harm from ending, and neither does the person who performed it — so a life ending is no harm either, and its ending is set by nature rather than by us.
2e064d923684cb75: The world stays perpetually fresh because its parts keep changing, and what serves the whole is always best and timeliest; death is not shameful, not within our choice, and serves the whole, so it cannot be an evil.
7b6f5d8ad4adab75: A person whose will moves in step with the order of the world is carried along by that order itself.
2f2872a86ea9b6ee: Keep three things ready: that your own actions be neither aimless nor unjust; that whatever befalls you comes either by chance or by providence, and it is unreasonable to blame either; and an awareness of what the body is compounded of and what it dissolves into.
f716e72300d1233f: Follow the example of good people, and you will hold a secure and lasting place in the world.
022744b5b83e5fee: Those who live wickedly eventually lose their place in the world.
651e0b16d2bddef6: Holding on to sound teaching lengthens your life and brings you peace.
3138a180e2a035ad: Keep loyalty and honesty so constantly that they become part of you, and you will earn the regard of both people and the divine.
f4d0ff4580741c75: Trust something larger than yourself instead of leaning only on your own judgment, and never assume you are wise enough on your own.
af21c7a7aeec50c4: Living rightly makes you well in body; give back the first and best of whatever you earn.
629ecc34c841a893: Generosity returns to you as abundance, and you should not resent being corrected.
cd9facb8ac7e68cb: Correction is an expression of love, not rejection, and the person who finds wisdom is fortunate.
81ad66631b017211: Wisdom is worth more than silver, gold, or anything else you might want.
8a1e0c3f8995a607: Wisdom brings long life, wealth, honor, and peace together.
3b289ce411dda63a: Wisdom sustains whoever holds on to it, and it is the principle by which the world itself is ordered.
3fdd7df9e9a8148a: Knowledge underlies the natural order; keep wisdom and discretion in sight and they will sustain your life.
08579d0afc4871fb: Living wisely lets you move through life without stumbling and sleep without fear.
a4b1666aec9c2840: You need not fear sudden disaster, because confidence in something greater keeps you from being caught.
f6ed00cda1178d33: Do not withhold from someone the good you owe them when you have the means to give it now.
97e751bc3e7d03ff: Do not scheme against people who trust you or quarrel without cause, and do not envy or imitate those who get ahead by force.
c0003f696e0a93d5: Crooked people are detested while honest ones are trusted with what matters, and ruin settles on a wicked household while blessing rests on a just one.
76328b6d65cc1bd4: Arrogance meets scorn and humility meets favor; the wise inherit honor while fools inherit disgrace.
b03d88c375db87c4: Listen to the instruction passed down by your parents, because it is sound.
27b0c948c19bf318: Hold on to what you have been taught, and above all acquire wisdom and understanding.
392b83bbe8b3e864: Never abandon wisdom and it will protect you; whatever else you acquire, acquire understanding.
ffb615546ed94ba9: Honor wisdom and it will raise you to honor in turn.
974119c6a73a10d1: Being taught the right way of living lengthens your years.
c26330c15d0a09e2: Wisdom lets you move freely without stumbling, so hold on to instruction as though your life depends on it.

6c9a01db3d966804: People bless or curse the gods according to whether events serve their own interests, so learning to govern your desires and aversions is what makes genuine reverence possible. 7ab4c0f4fad408b4: Consult a diviner only about the unknown outcome, never about whether a thing is good or bad, since whatever lies outside your control is neither, and you retain the power to use it well.

d6b4ba7da9f74ea8: Decide in advance what character you will hold to and keep it whether alone or watched. Speak little and briefly, avoid trivial talk and especially talk that praises, blames, or compares other people, and if you cannot steer the conversation upward, stay silent.

839b0aa452c88495: At public shows, refrain from cheering, jeering, and rehashing it afterward, since visible excitement proves the spectacle has mastered you. Before any difficult encounter, especially with someone above you, ask how a person you admire would handle it.
67f7402027e04f07: Before approaching someone powerful, rehearse every possible snub in advance, and if the visit is still your duty, bear what comes without complaining it wasn't worth it. Don't dwell on your own exploits and dangers, which are far more enjoyable to tell than to hear.

7b1a97d173889515: Even when a pleasure seems justified, the consciousness of having mastered yourself is worth more than the pleasure would have been.

88f9f04ca1974f1b: Just as you watch your step to avoid injuring your foot, watch each action so as not to damage your own capacity for judgment.

321ff7da297c5a25: Girls learn early that they are valued only for being desirable, so they stake everything on their appearance; they should instead be shown that they are honored for their conduct and character.

8ed6456bd14e31a2: Whoever wrongs you acts on what seems right to him, not to you, and if his view is false he is the one deceived and thereby harmed; recognizing this lets you bear insult calmly by saying simply, "that is how it looked to him."

6c79bd05da6ee25d: You have genuinely begun the work when being told you know nothing no longer stings. Like sheep, who turn grass into wool rather than displaying what they ate, show the actions your learning produces rather than the learning itself.

0d75d21b04251e00: The ordinary person expects all help and harm to come from outside himself, while the philosopher looks only to himself for both.
