1412ca0aff4692a6: Do not let careless speech lead you into wrongdoing and then excuse it as a mistake; amid many empty words and fantasies, hold to reverence.
2745ca0f83523bb4: Do not be shocked to see the poor oppressed and justice perverted, because officials protect one another up the hierarchy, yet everyone ultimately depends on the land.
e8872212c27f881c: Whoever loves money is never satisfied by having more of it, and as wealth grows so do the people consuming it, leaving the owner only the sight of it.
a6c590e29374d565: A laborer sleeps well regardless of how much he eats, while the rich man's abundance keeps him awake; hoarded wealth harms its owner and can be lost entirely, leaving nothing for his heirs.
7110bf90ba46d0ea: We leave the world as naked as we entered it, carrying none of what we earned, so laboring only to accumulate is laboring for nothing.
be38157e768125c4: Deep teachings are conveyed indirectly, and only those already prepared to grasp them will understand.
33ddb288fb0b14f1: Understanding compounds: those who already have insight gain more, while those without lose even what little they had.
90716f7c3eb08bbe: People can hear and see something without ever perceiving its meaning.
c73c0cf334a379f0: People close themselves off from understanding because grasping the truth would require them to change.
ad895f2ff0a19c92: Those who actually perceive what is in front of them are fortunate, since many have longed for such insight and never received it.
a4c8a6e4a095f6ec: A truth heard without being understood is quickly lost.
0f9e4ffc49568e22: Something accepted with enthusiasm but without deep roots collapses at the first hardship.
155fb02a46793163: Anxiety and the lure of wealth crowd out what you have learned and leave it unproductive.
020042964b5d7cc0: A truth that is both heard and understood becomes fruitful, sometimes many times over.
ceee577d63d38731: Corruption is quietly introduced alongside the good while no one is paying attention.
f820db61f31b20ef: The difference between the genuine and the counterfeit only becomes visible once both have matured.
80527879569856f5: Trying to eliminate what is bad too early destroys the good along with it.
70d17bc81b1bec61: Let good and bad develop side by side; separation is possible only at the end, when the distinction is clear.
cae4561f3426b0b9: What is ultimately vast can begin as something almost negligible.
b5fc5e59e2728b1d: The smallest beginning can grow into something large enough to shelter others.
ea2516c0b97e7507: A small hidden influence gradually transforms the entire mass it is placed in.
bcea83d9b5b60054: Indirect speech can reveal things that have never been said openly.
90f09d94b58716f7: What is genuinely good in the world originates from a good source.
581c9e6ffe34a4af: The world contains both good and corrupt elements, sown from opposite sources, and they will be sorted only at the end.
888746a0bf3aad11: At the end, everything harmful and every wrongdoer will be gathered out and destroyed.
ea31d572aa60888b: Wrongdoing ends in ruin and anguish, while the good finally shine plainly for what they are.
535ea1a7e4c54231: When you discover something of supreme value, giving up everything else to obtain it is worth it, and a genuine seeker recognizes it when found.
d4e4db85cbac5dbe: The one thing of ultimate worth is worth everything you own, and life gathers all kinds of people indiscriminately.
618d8fcd2f49f1ca: In the end what is worthwhile will be kept and what is not will be discarded, and the good will be separated from the wicked.
97ba764bab257ac5: Wrongdoing ends in destruction and misery, and people often claim to understand teachings they have only just heard.
76945b5bcdc01eec: Someone truly educated draws on both old and new knowledge rather than only one.
7bb647985174c343: People are astonished when wisdom and power appear in someone they thought they knew.
b60929f4c749b589: Familiarity with a person's ordinary origins makes people refuse to credit their extraordinary qualities.
29dcc23c6040be09: A person is least honored where they are best known, and disbelief in them limits what they can accomplish there.
6e68cab832e3e851: Greatness is not what the ambitious imagine it to be.
9ee04a62d22308fd: Whoever makes themselves lowest, with a child's lack of status, is in truth the greatest.
3e423dbcc81dd9b4: Welcoming the powerless honors what is highest, and harming them is the gravest of wrongs.
0e608f02e710d33a: Corrupting influences are inevitable in the world, but the person who causes them still bears the guilt.
2401d7d7e958d03a: It is better to lose something valuable to you than to let it lead you into ruin.
c0c985357c382cc4: Sacrifice even what you most cherish rather than let it destroy you.
29f6d7db7e63b2ad: Never treat the insignificant with contempt; they matter supremely, and rescuing the lost is the whole point.
f2193cad63b68c78: The one who is lost deserves more attention than the many who are safe.
de800332fe1ac89b: Recovering what was lost brings more joy than what was never in danger, and no one should be written off.
6229121da65fda72: Confront someone who wronged you privately first, and widen the circle only if they refuse to listen.
6fb266a26ffc21bd: Someone who refuses correction from everyone eventually places themselves outside the community.
8b61560abfb24872: Decisions made by a genuine community carry weight beyond themselves.
19882ed4b4f3fc85: Genuine agreement among even a few people has real power, and what is sacred is present wherever they gather.
8d67abf013ead9fe: Forgiveness should have no limit or countable end.
7fcb5bf23a986360: Everyone stands under an obligation far larger than they can ever repay.
90f513d85b11c85c: Facing ruin over an unpayable debt, a person can only beg for mercy they cannot earn.
f31d97246fce8061: A person who has been forgiven an enormous debt may still ruthlessly demand a trivial one from someone else.
78d8e214e5cf90e4: People refuse others the very patience they themselves were shown.
b02a92369a00518f: Refusing mercy after receiving it is a wickedness that others see and condemn.
3f7bd1769e30d34a: Having been shown compassion obliges you to show it, and refusing forfeits the mercy you were given.
0a085acd3b026ae1: Mercy withheld from others is mercy forfeited for yourself, and forgiveness must be sincere rather than formal.
ac8cd0cf07b50a5c: Wisdom means preparing for a delay you cannot predict; foolishness means bringing only what suffices for the present moment.
2f41b26912ed0c9c: When the long-awaited moment finally arrives without warning, the unprepared discover their resources are already spent.
de4202253b37fced: Some preparation cannot be borrowed or acquired at the last moment, and by the time you seek it the opportunity has closed.
5a82434956f377c3: An opportunity missed cannot be reclaimed by pleading, so stay ready, since you never know when the decisive moment comes.
8870e1b36ae4343c: People are entrusted with resources in differing amounts, each according to their own capacity.
fadb7282052248d4: Those who use what they are given increase it; those who fear losing it hide it away and produce nothing.
5a9d2313c691e96e: Eventually everyone is asked to account for what they did with what they were given.
4e7a41d8522b032b: Proving trustworthy in small responsibilities earns larger ones and genuine reward.
9b8cc3adf147fa36: Someone given less can still double what they were given.
fdd47b41d1828523: Faithfulness is measured against what you were given, not against what others were given, so a smaller yield earns the same praise.
de6d9e99df2e6af8: Honest criticism from someone who cares about you is more valuable than affectionate flattery from someone who does not. What you value depends on your need: when you are satisfied even good things seem worthless, and when you are desperate even bad things seem acceptable.
f4567f3a8a141a8a: A person who abandons the place where they belong loses their bearings. Sincere advice from a friend is a genuine comfort.
7290d4c1bf7090ad: Maintain long-standing friendships rather than trading them for new ones, because in a crisis someone close at hand helps more than family who are far away.
1798c8ee7b52e37a: A child who lives wisely vindicates the parent against critics. Prudent people see trouble coming and take cover, while the naive walk into it and suffer for it.
9b648049ae81d1e6: Demand security from anyone who guarantees a stranger's debt. Praise that is too loud and too eager is heard as mockery rather than as a compliment.
6b628aaac16a29e8: Constant complaining wears a person down the way steady dripping does, and it cannot be contained or concealed any more than wind can be held.
d10d56fbb9d42e74: People sharpen and improve one another through direct contact. Whoever tends something faithfully receives the benefit of it.
fd09e94f92ca8f91: People recognize themselves in one another because human hearts work alike. Human desire has no natural limit and is never satisfied.
c843196043f20c35: A person's character is revealed by how they handle praise, the way heat reveals the purity of metal. Foolishness is not cured by punishment, however severe.
e8d9f4f4050e14dc: Know the actual condition of what you own and manage it attentively, because wealth and position are never permanent.
c8ba1c19ca667bc1: Resources come in seasons and must be gathered when they appear. What you raise and tend is what clothes you and buys what you need.
9d53594f84790303: Careful management of what you have provides enough to sustain everyone who depends on you.
c4cbd7e0535d40e7: Guilt makes people fearful without cause, while integrity makes them confident. A corrupt society churns through many rulers, but one capable leader can give it stability.
a9164bfad9b4dbf9: A person with little power who exploits others still ruins them completely. Those who abandon principle end up defending wrongdoing, and those who keep it end up resisting it.
02719370b191f80b: People committed to wrongdoing cannot understand justice, while those seeking what is right come to understand it fully. Poverty with integrity is better than wealth with corruption.
38fc9601facaa45e: Living by principle honors your family, while running with dissipated company shames it. Wealth built on exploitation is eventually inherited by someone who uses it generously.
c16025f5e4c9c0aa: Someone who refuses to listen to moral instruction cannot expect their own appeals to be taken seriously.
bde1609c7d785e4e: Anyone who leads good people into wrongdoing falls into the trap they set, while the upright keep what is genuinely good.
7aef7aefa1b37516: Wealth convinces people they are wise, but a discerning person without money sees through them. When decent people hold power a society flourishes openly, and when corrupt people hold power people go into hiding.
7437bf98d56c9138: Concealing your wrongdoing keeps you stuck, while admitting it and abandoning it makes you forgivable. Staying alert to your own capacity for error protects you, and hardening yourself against correction destroys you.
12f3a02fb44dae09: A corrupt ruler over powerless people is simply a predator. Leaders without understanding become oppressors, while those who refuse to be bought last.
7f6376021eb46ec7: Someone guilty of killing will be a fugitive for life. Integrity carries a person through, while crookedness brings sudden collapse.
2755fa840c4630dd: Working steadily at your own real work produces enough to live on, while chasing empty pursuits produces nothing but poverty.
8e68537c54313ddc: Reliability accumulates real advantages, while rushing to get rich requires cutting corners. Showing favoritism corrupts judgment, since a person who does it will betray fairness for almost nothing.
81538aa570b5da09: The urgency to get rich blinds a person to the ruin they are walking toward. Honest correction eventually earns more gratitude than flattery does.
a1f3b692627c8f8c: Someone who takes from their own parents and tells themselves it is not really wrong is no different from an outright vandal.
0fe68ae53e9e2cc0: Arrogance generates conflict, while trusting something beyond yourself brings abundance. Relying entirely on your own judgment is foolish, and living wisely is what saves you.
c7553c1785276df9: Giving to those in need does not impoverish you, while deliberately looking away earns you widespread resentment. People go into hiding when the corrupt gain power and flourish again when they fall.
873a5f2e3ab558fe: Someone corrected again and again who refuses to change will break suddenly and beyond repair. People thrive under good leadership and suffer under bad.
f471732255a69dc9: Loving wisdom brings your family joy, while dissipation drains your resources. A ruler builds a country through fair judgment and destroys it by taking bribes.
aac25ac7bbcfa61a: Flattering someone sets a trap for them. Wrongdoing ensnares the person who commits it, while those who live honestly are free to enjoy their lives.
d7813587426a24f2: Decent people care whether the powerless get justice, while the corrupt make sure not to find out. Contemptuous people inflame a community, and wise people defuse its anger.
50f26f796c476538: Arguing with a fool never reaches resolution, whether he responds with rage or with laughter. Violent people hate those who live honestly, while the just protect them.
533308e463b5d112: A fool says everything that comes into his head, while a wise person holds it back until the right moment. A leader who listens to lies ends up surrounded by liars. The poor and the deceitful share the same nature and the same origin.
7dc7fcf38966be8c: A ruler who deals fairly with the powerless secures their own position. Discipline and correction produce wisdom, while a child left entirely to themselves brings the family shame.
5cfa688be6a2144e: The more wrongdoers there are the more wrongdoing spreads, but it does not last. Correcting your child is what eventually gives you peace.
1a7df8cdcfec97d4: Without a guiding vision people come apart, while those who live by a shared standard do well. Some people will not be changed by words alone, even when they understand perfectly.
911c0ede0ac3159b: A person who speaks without thinking is a worse prospect than an outright fool. Indulging a subordinate from the beginning ends with them claiming they are your equal.
1420302200b4b78a: Anger breeds conflict and multiplies the harm a person does. Pride brings a person down, and humility is what earns them real standing.
b8fe6cf79e01bd7c: Going in with a thief destroys you, because you hear the truth and stay silent about it. Fear of what people think is a trap, while trusting something larger keeps you safe.
2825ee809bcd27d9: Many people court those in power, but what a person actually deserves is determined elsewhere. The just and the unjust each find the other repellent.
e84006470278dfa8: A person can begin by admitting they understand less than anyone else.
a056d0a0b5de85e4: Admitting you have neither learned wisdom nor grasped the sacred is itself a starting point.
f4bd606f09eb56e4: No human being has done or could do what made the world, and whatever did is beyond human naming or comprehension.
e05352d73089c20e: What is genuinely true needs no additions, and anyone who adds to it makes themselves a liar.
3430a737f768401a: The two things worth asking for are freedom from falsehood and enough to live on, neither destitution nor excess.
d9666bfd74a040fc: Too much makes people forget what sustains them, and too little drives them to steal. Do not accuse a subordinate to their superior, because the accusation will land on you.
8117c42242dacffe: Some people despise the parents who raised them, and some consider themselves pure while remaining filthy.
b2299cd32f9c0d84: Some people are consumed by their own arrogance.
b12b9bf0cb3289f9: Some people live by devouring the poor and the defenseless.
b54f41e10dafa556: Greed only ever asks for more. Some appetites can never be filled.
0b68d13e13be8fe7: Death, barrenness, parched ground, and fire share the same nature: they consume without ever reaching enough.
297de1449607fba1: Contempt for the parents who raised you ends badly for the one who holds it.
4e3b17f22ae6daf6: Some things remain genuinely beyond understanding, however closely you look at them.
96558eea8615208f: The most remarkable movements leave no trace of how they happened, and attraction between people is one of them.
8ae1cb70cdbdbd5f: People can do real harm and sincerely believe they have done nothing wrong. Some situations a society simply cannot absorb.
f53020ec3cbb2840: People raised suddenly above what they can handle become intolerable, whether through power, plenty, marriage, or inheritance.
c1a2f15818e3b419: Wisdom does not require strength; small creatures show it by preparing in advance for what is coming.
db880878bd39e3af: Weakness is compensated by shelter, by coordinated action without a leader, and by persistence that reaches the highest places.
028b5de8bda0f431: Some things carry themselves with a bearing that does not flinch or give way.
3434208edb9c6d54: Those who are spiritually empty and those who grieve are not cursed but favored, and will be filled and comforted.
5ba5a7094e771f11: Gentleness, a deep craving for what is right, mercy, and inner purity are each answered in kind.
101ee2aedfd18d57: People who make peace, and people who suffer for doing right, hold the highest standing.
4d7977faf096fd67: Being slandered and attacked for your convictions places you among those who came before you, so treat it as cause for gladness rather than despair.
b9da48b58e753038: A thing that loses the quality that made it valuable is worthless; and anyone who genuinely has that quality cannot stay hidden.
bca52cdaf95b11e0: Do good openly, so that others see it and credit what it points to rather than the person doing it.
83b96c926d4e2a92: A new teaching completes an old moral law rather than abolishing it, and not the smallest part of that law lapses before its purpose is served.
b562755b067ec35d: How you treat even the smallest obligations, and what you teach others about them, determines your worth.
d3e19a450604f247: Merely meeting the letter of a rule, as the professionally religious do, is not enough; the old prohibition punished killing itself.
acdff31bea904774: Contempt and unwarranted anger toward another person carry the same moral weight as violence against them.
aa4d53195749df16: Repair a broken relationship before you attempt any act of devotion.
52f0a994dac3c790: Settle a dispute early, while you still can, before it escalates past your control.
1230ece9104d4c4c: A debt is exacted to the last coin; and the old prohibition forbade adultery.
f67e2054cd6ee63a: Wanting someone in your mind is already the betrayal, whatever your body does.
e72b6077daf75acf: Cut away whatever part of your life leads you to ruin, since losing a part is better than losing the whole.
0c6433aa70642122: Sacrifice whatever destroys you, however useful it seems; the old custom allowed a man to dismiss his wife with a certificate.
3d6ce729a4feaf6b: Discarding a spouse for any reason but infidelity implicates both parties in the wrong that follows.
b2318045045f01ad: Swearing by sacred things adds nothing, because none of them are yours to pledge.
39bb57ef07500746: You cannot control even the color of your own hair, so let a plain yes or no be your whole word; anything beyond it comes from bad faith.
fa72b1df546bcae4: Do not answer injury with injury; when someone strikes you or takes from you, offer more rather than retaliate.
b0e2785a8d9460cd: Exceed what is demanded of you and give to whoever asks; the old rule said to love your own and hate your enemies.
6acd20c3927bdc79: Love your enemies, treat well the people who hate you, and wish good on those who mistreat you.
5d2617edc112b17e: Nature's gifts fall on good and bad alike, and loving only those who already love you is no achievement at all.
051e72903f2d1e81: Kindness to your own circle is unremarkable; the standard is a goodness as complete and impartial as nature's.
956fad26b7dbddaf: Doing good in order to be seen doing it cancels its worth.
4435a9c344dbb818: Advertised generosity buys only the admiration it seeks; give so quietly that you barely register it yourself.
0782b2bcc3842299: Let your giving be hidden, and what sees in secret will answer it.
bfe9169252df0877: Piety performed for onlookers is paid in full by their attention and nothing more.
e0be81c91e843529: Go alone, shut the door, and speak in private to what is itself unseen.
9faa403b0e00d0d4: Piling up words does not make you heard, since what you need is already known before you ask.
ac8b5081d50a5489: Ask simply: that a greater will be done, that today's needs be met, and that you be forgiven exactly as far as you forgive others.
c0f9cd09d77b54b7: Ask to be spared from harm; and know that forgiving others is what opens forgiveness to you.
31883aaaa4ac70af: Refuse to forgive and you close off forgiveness for yourself; and self-denial paraded before others is repaid only by their notice.
6d4882278b3138b1: Conceal your self-denial behind an ordinary face, keeping it between you and what you do it for.
dcb5893e766ba2c0: Invest in what cannot decay or be stolen rather than in what can.
23441df76b56a4af: Your heart follows whatever you treasure, and a clear, undivided way of seeing fills your whole life with light.
3eb77c0214eb5a1d: A corrupted way of seeing darkens everything, and when the very faculty meant to give you light is dark, the darkness is total.
123f31a9f2b1af42: You cannot devote yourself to two masters with opposed claims; wealth and a higher good will not both hold you.
0dee4c7312a8f4cd: Stop anxiously calculating about food and clothing, because life is worth more than the things that maintain it.
e7554061fa7ed8fa: Wild creatures are sustained without storing anything up, and no amount of worry adds a single inch to you.
03c8bcda09173094: Wildflowers, which do no work at all, outshine the finest king in his robes.
9bb101c87f9a1233: If even grass that burns tomorrow is clothed beautifully, then anxious questions about food and clothing betray a failure of trust.
3d565b5b4b4e07fe: Frantic pursuit of material needs is what people without trust do; aim first at what matters most and the rest follows.
f66d74c80569d456: Don't carry tomorrow's worries today; each day brings enough trouble of its own.
acaf8cb3c13e5ddd: The standard you use to judge others is the one that will be applied to you, and you fixate on their small faults while ignoring your own enormous ones.
8482e54488f06a52: Clear your own far greater faults first; only then can you see well enough to help anyone else with theirs.
e5592a15f145d14d: Don't offer what is precious to those who will only trample it and turn on you; but ask, search, and knock, and you will be answered.
0392301b4d0f06a9: Everyone who persists in asking receives, and no decent parent hands a hungry child something harmful instead of food.
4e6a239084188da0: If flawed people know how to give their children good things, the source of everything gives far more readily; so treat others exactly as you would want to be treated, since this sums up all the rest.
0e94079745bc94e3: The way that leads to ruin is broad, easy, and crowded, while the way that leads to life is narrow and few ever find it.
c17708b6fe975860: Deceivers arrive disguised as what they prey on, so judge them by what they actually produce, since a thing's nature determines its output.
c6a574ebb7f0f5e5: Nothing can produce fruit contrary to its own nature, and what yields nothing good is eventually discarded, so results reveal character.
9efa9df7501bc792: Professing allegiance counts for nothing; only actually doing what is asked does.
f4f1fcedc48dcb71: Impressive works performed in a cause's name mean nothing if the person doing them lives corruptly, and such people were never truly known by it.
1e0819641fa28bea: Whoever hears these teachings and acts on them builds a life on rock, which stands when the storm hits.
47066089c7feef95: Whoever hears these teachings and fails to act on them builds a life on sand, and its collapse under pressure is total.
cc5e2306ef6e2de0: People were astonished because the teaching carried its own authority rather than borrowing it from established experts.
7eafdd39b3bef31d: Truth is taught through ordinary stories, and it is scattered broadly like seed, without regard for where it will land.
efd1d9f380234266: Some of what is sown is snatched away at once, and some lands where there is no depth to sustain it and springs up immediately.
17aca41bd52c3340: What has no root withers under the first heat, and what is crowded by competing growth is choked, while what falls on good ground yields many times what was sown.
a9986be2c72de341: A life consumed by bitterness wastes itself; the good available to a person is to eat, drink, and take pleasure in the fruits of their own work during their brief life.
3b116dac3c66513c: Being able to actually enjoy what one has earned is itself a piece of good fortune, and a person absorbed in that enjoyment scarcely notices life passing.
bcd5ddaacb210fe0: It is a common misfortune for someone to acquire everything they wanted and yet lack the capacity to enjoy any of it, so a stranger consumes it instead.
ee93ccb5a3e8e496: A long life full of children and years is worth nothing if the person never finds satisfaction in it; one who was never born is better off.
a5d38f76349e7f15: Someone who never existed has more peace than a person who lives thousands of years without ever enjoying anything, since everyone ends in the same place; all our labor feeds an appetite that is never filled.
69367b6006e4f62d: The wise hold no lasting advantage over the fool, and it is better to enjoy what is in front of you than to chase what you merely imagine. Nothing about human nature is new, and no one can contend against what is stronger than they are.
779d66c834bb38a3: The more we accumulate, the less it helps; no one knows what is genuinely good in a life this brief, nor what will come after they are gone.
06eec9a65c593c30: A good reputation outlasts luxury, and grief teaches more than celebration because it forces the living to confront their own end. Sorrow can improve a person in a way amusement cannot.
2ac162279cddc478: The wise are at home with mourning while fools seek constant amusement; honest criticism is worth more than flattering entertainment, which burns loud and fast and leaves nothing behind.
fde72ae059b9c0f4: Oppression can drive even a sensible person to madness, and a bribe corrupts judgment. Patience is better than pride, and quick anger is the mark of a fool.
c50d1debf5c009a5: Asking why the past was better than the present is not a wise question. Wisdom protects a person the way money does, but unlike money it also gives them life.
26db2d317d6961b0: No one can straighten what reality has bent; enjoy good times and reflect during hard ones, since both are set against each other so that no one can predict what comes next.
d6b9c204db17ef54: Good people sometimes perish early while the corrupt live long; do not be excessively righteous or excessively clever, since it can ruin you.
4bb21320ece8af7a: Do not be excessively wicked or foolish either, since that shortens your life; grasp both warnings and let go of neither. Wisdom defends a person more than a city's warriors do.
f6da364e6f3211b0: No one on earth does good without also doing wrong. Do not listen closely to everything said about you, since you know you have said the same things about others.
ec6b8afc2a4e34cf: The deepest truths cannot be found out, no matter how hard a person searches for wisdom and for an understanding of folly and madness.
995b704ad57a618e: A seduction whose affection is a trap and whose embrace is a snare is more bitter than death, and only the disciplined escape it.
d37bce9a04dd8078: A truly upright person is almost impossible to find; human beings were made straightforward, but they have complicated themselves with endless schemes.
f9d2ffd6bccd187d: Wisdom transforms a person's very bearing. Keep your commitments to those in power, and do not rush out of favor or dig in on a bad position, since they will do as they please.
56ebf4b169263a01: A ruler's word carries unquestionable force, and the wise sense the right time and manner for everything. Because timing decides so much and we control it so little, people suffer.
45d568ad012796c8: No one knows the future or when it will arrive. No one controls their own breath or the day of their death; death is the one war with no discharge, and no amount of scheming buys escape from it.
26518ae8f78d0005: People sometimes hold power over others only to their own harm, and the corrupt are buried with honor and quickly forgotten by the very city they wronged.
792ce5db25d38f4d: Because punishment for wrongdoing is delayed, people grow bolder in doing wrong; even so, in the long run it goes well for those who live with reverence.
f01bb74ad4c970b1: It will not finally go well for the person who lives without reverence, and their days pass like a shadow.
0f11c98f00529084: Good people often meet what the wicked deserve, and the wicked meet what the good deserve, and this is absurd.
61344ffe9ccbdf19: Since nothing better is on offer, the sensible response is to eat, drink, and be glad, because that is what stays with a person through the work of their life.
f333259927c8590d: Trying to grasp everything that happens on earth costs a person their sleep and yields nothing.
b4a9e415481c83b8: No one, however hard they work or however wise they are, can comprehend the whole of what happens in the world.
ae5a1f112a19b40c: The fates of the good and the wise are out of their own hands, and no one can tell from what befalls them whether they are loved or hated.
22a538771e2bcc8a: The same fate arrives for everyone — the decent and the corrupt, the devout and the indifferent alike.
6bcfc771bf42a741: That one fate comes to everyone is the great wrong, and it fills people with madness while they live. Still, being alive carries hope, and the lowliest living creature is better off than the greatest dead one.
15e2f6964449b94f: The living at least know they will die; the dead know nothing, are forgotten, and their loves, hatreds, and envies have vanished from the world entirely.
51db8e9ec19000be: Eat your food gladly, drink with a light heart, and take pleasure in small comforts, for this way of living is accepted.
78aae4e2bc16800b: Live joyfully with the person you love through your brief and fleeting days, for that companionship is the share allotted to you in return for all your work.
389175073c34449b: Do whatever is in front of you with your full strength, because after death there is no work, no planning, no knowledge, and no wisdom.
addc1d7bb170efab: The fastest do not win the race, the strongest do not win the battle, the wise do not eat best, and the skilled are not favored; timing and chance decide it all.
a6bebb1bb1a5f9ce: People do not know when their moment will come, and disaster catches them suddenly, the way a net catches fish or a snare catches birds.
d8d9e788d5f8f544: A poor but wise man once saved a besieged city by his intelligence, and afterward no one remembered him.
f6145ec161d9c6fe: Wisdom is stronger than force or weapons, yet the wisdom of a poor person is dismissed and goes unheard, while quiet words of the wise carry further than a ruler shouting among fools. One wrongdoer can destroy a great deal of good.
969d22d56a02e108: A little foolishness spoils an otherwise honored reputation, the way a few dead flies ruin perfume; a fool announces his foolishness with every step he takes.
66c2f5ec7ba17a5e: When someone in power turns against you, stay composed rather than react, since composure defuses even serious offense. It is a visible wrong when fools are elevated and capable people are placed beneath them.
69b364feeac5374a: Every action carries its own risk back to the one who takes it, and working with a dull tool costs more force. Foresight is what makes effort effective.
d57cb95267334fd8: A snake bites before it can be charmed, and the compulsive talker is no better. A wise person's words earn goodwill, while a fool's words devour him, beginning in nonsense and ending in destructive madness.
f7bc5bdee222ead5: A fool talks endlessly, though no one can know what comes next. Foolish labor exhausts the fool because he does not know the simplest way to get where he is going.
cac0af33d199467d: A society suffers under immature, self-indulgent leaders and thrives under disciplined ones who take what they need for strength rather than pleasure. Neglect quietly collapses whatever it touches, and while feasting brings laughter, money is what actually gets things done.
a6bef856c5447c35: Do not speak against the powerful, even privately or in your own thoughts, because word travels in ways you cannot trace.
599fde1040a7d16b: Give away what you have without expecting immediate return, and spread it widely, because you cannot know what disaster is coming.
fd94bcbc10c94bf8: Nature takes its own course regardless of us, and the person who waits for perfect conditions never plants and never harvests.
37f94a30acdeffa6: Since you cannot explain how life takes shape in the womb, you cannot expect to understand how the whole of the world works.
9c623db772bed024: Work in the morning and again in the evening, because you cannot know which effort will succeed, or whether both will.
d2e7e3bf2e29ddf1: However many years you enjoy, remember that the dark days will be many; everything that comes is fleeting.
e444c8b774c35e88: Enjoy your youth and follow your heart and your eyes, but know that you will be held to account for all of it. Put grief and harm away from you, because youth passes quickly.
c1cc34a6fd760cec: Turn toward what matters most while you are young, before the hard years arrive and pleasure drains out of everything.
8108174fd9296146: Old age arrives as the body fails: the hands tremble, the legs weaken, the teeth are lost, and the eyes go dim.
cb9ba0d758841fff: In old age a person withdraws from the world, sleeps lightly, wakes at the first bird, and can no longer hear music well.
c3bc98d9e3cd0f8e: The old grow afraid of heights and of the road, hair turns white, small burdens become heavy, and desire fades — the person is moving toward death while the mourners gather.
51870d3f51fcdc63: Eventually the body's fragile mechanism breaks: dust returns to the earth and breath returns to its source. Everything is fleeting.
888b21b7fe84112b: A wise teacher went on teaching, weighed and arranged many sayings, and searched for words that were both pleasing and true.
a1d1a7e592a10451: The words of the wise prod like a goad and hold like driven nails, all coming from one source; beyond them, books multiply without end, and too much study wears out the body.
e3ff98b3a97eda8b: The sum of it all is to live in reverence and keep your obligations, since that is the whole of a person's task, and every act, including the hidden ones, will be brought to account.
5d011ff8ce578328: Fear of a harsh judge makes people protect what they were given rather than risk it, so they produce nothing.
8ce26a0e9f876ad3: Fearing the person you answer to is no excuse for inaction; if you truly believed the stakes were high, you would have made at least some effort.
3ff9800c8f7b6058: Advantage compounds: those who already have gain more, while those with little lose even what they hold.
b7f783c108d15c9f: Those who make nothing of what they were entrusted with are cast out, and a reckoning eventually arrives for everyone.
7a5ee45b1cbfac71: In the end people are sorted into two kinds, and the division is made by someone who can see what they actually are.
5de7c965fc3d788c: Those who fed the hungry, gave drink to the thirsty, and welcomed strangers are the ones who inherit what was prepared for them.
a4e57f5e4c629550: People who genuinely do good do it without realizing they are serving anything greater than the person in front of them.
e16956524f7d8ff6: How you treat the lowest and least powerful person is how you treat what is most sacred.
54175b6d415aceae: Those who saw need and gave nothing bring their own condemnation on themselves.
99bea6bb7fbf2c5e: People who withhold help do not notice they are withholding it; the neglect feels like nothing at the time.
e1adbd95dd4287ff: Failing the least person is failing the highest, and the consequences of that failure are lasting.
7f619445e661d8b5: Rule-keepers object when hungry people meet their basic needs in a way that breaks the letter of a sacred law.
831d7f78df034ec0: Even revered figures broke ritual rules when they were hungry, and the tradition honors them anyway.
2172f91bb156b61d: Sacred rules exist to serve human need, not the reverse, and a person may stand above the rule.
1cf709388d5bf280: Legalists watch a good person closely, hoping to catch him doing good at a forbidden moment so they can accuse him.
858d77a8671f9386: If a rule forbids you from doing good or saving a life, you have misunderstood the rule.
9ae00a4d2b186b34: Doing obvious good in defiance of rigid convention enrages the people who enforce it.
accb81f70a441644: Withdraw and reflect at length before choosing the people who will carry your work.
1b844fefaab8012b: Any chosen circle, however carefully selected, will contain someone who betrays it.
2024c2927aada7ef: People travel from far outside their own regions to hear a teacher and to be healed.
0ac302fbbcb6b2f1: Something goes out of a person who heals others; and the poor, not the comfortable, are the ones who possess what matters.
1247ef965bd7be07: Those who go hungry and grieve now will be filled and will laugh, and being hated and slandered for a worthy cause places you in good company.
2e7e2d672efe4233: Rejoice when you are persecuted, because the reward is deferred; the rich, by contrast, have already collected theirs.
4f9a586f8fd13ba2: Present fullness, laughter, and universal approval are all borrowed against a future reversal; and the right response to enemies is love and active kindness.
7d13a27aba5dc5de: Answer hostility with blessing, and when someone strikes or takes from you, do not retaliate or resist.
bda31c87d4a041c6: Give to whoever asks and let go of what is taken; treat others exactly as you would want to be treated.
00720bec90ba3114: There is no moral credit in loving, helping, or lending only to those who will return the favor, since even the worst people do that.
a12fcb579f84d30a: Give and lend expecting nothing back, imitating a generosity that extends even to the ungrateful and the wicked.
1f1764bf633f3b8a: Refrain from judging and condemning, and forgive, because you will be treated as you treat others.
3295bf0debecee7d: Give generously and generosity overflows back to you; the standard you apply is the standard applied to you.
b98d12d2c0c27211: Someone who cannot see cannot guide others, and a student can at best only equal the teacher, never exceed him.
fb5a3b9de57cbafe: People notice trivial flaws in others while remaining blind to enormous ones in themselves.
23601bb29662f839: You cannot help correct someone else's fault until you have removed the larger one obstructing your own vision.
1495cd42185a4c7d: Character reliably produces its own kind, so a person is known by what they actually produce, not by what they claim.
f1efe991b60c7309: What a person has stored inside them is what comes out in their speech and actions, so professing loyalty without acting on it is empty.
bc8160c8c2d017de: The person who both hears and acts on what they hear is fundamentally different from one who only hears.
0c25c3ae40d960fd: A life built slowly on solid ground holds when crisis strikes it.
6269f887b3e8fc71: Hearing without acting builds on nothing, and when crisis comes the collapse is total.
5aa62076a6921924: Hypocrisy works like yeast, spreading invisibly through a whole group, so guard against it from the start.
f5fd61e9d98f5e19: Nothing stays concealed; whatever is whispered in private will eventually be announced publicly.
52d5468b1a572cae: Do not fear those who can only destroy your body, since their power ends there; fear only what can destroy what is deeper.
4756e626ac344500: Nothing, however small, goes unnoticed or forgotten, so you have no reason to be afraid.
88b02ff0e325e593: Publicly standing by what you believe, or publicly disowning it, determines how you are finally recognized.
cfcdcd34f2c48f4f: Most offenses can be forgiven, but rejecting the very source of truth cannot be, because it removes the means of forgiveness.
a865fe33311fbfb6: When you are hauled before authorities to defend yourself, do not rehearse; the words you need will come when the moment arrives.
d540d149fdfecf2f: Refuse to be pulled into other people's disputes over money and inheritance; it is not your role to arbitrate them.
e22c4d8402788c13: Guard against greed, because a person's life does not consist of the abundance of what they own.
84f78bc511498dc5: A man whose success outgrows his storage responds by building bigger storage.
a56b070355cb25dc: You can stockpile enough for many years and lose your life the same night, leaving everything you accumulated to someone else.
c5a9d4b44b1a71c9: Hoarding for yourself while remaining spiritually empty is folly; life is more than food and the body is more than clothing.
ef8ab527d7458e85: Wild birds neither sow nor store yet are fed, and no amount of worry can add a single inch to your height.
c5c9d35957f2baff: If anxiety cannot accomplish the smallest thing, it is pointless to apply it to the rest; wildflowers outshine kings without laboring at all.
46423ac5e4a26504: If even grass that lasts a day is clothed beautifully, anxiety about your own provision reveals a failure of trust.
64d53b51f77239a4: Everyone chases food and drink, but what you actually need is already known; pursue what is highest and the rest follows.
3b109dc27cce9587: Sell what you own and give it away, storing value where it cannot be stolen or decay, because your heart inevitably follows whatever you treasure.
78df5ac4f47f755c: Live like someone expecting a return at any moment, ready to respond the instant it comes.
9893563e711e1007: Those found alert when the reckoning arrives are rewarded, and the one they waited for serves them in turn.
84b0f3b23403fdf8: Since no one knows the hour, readiness cannot be timed; if the householder had known when the thief would come, he would have stayed awake.
84e507c1b0580cba: Be ready always, because the decisive moment comes precisely when you do not expect it.
c7ff1bfeabaa08f8: The trustworthy person is the one found doing their assigned work faithfully at whatever moment they happen to be checked.
83745c8d157161fd: People entrusted with authority who believe no one is coming to check on them begin to abuse those under them and indulge themselves.
616b0baabffb75d7: A reckoning arrives at the moment one least expects it, and those who betrayed their trust end up sharing the fate of those who never had any.
5220344c5d28b50a: Someone who understood what was required of them and did not do it deserves harsher punishment than someone who did not understand.
b924d2f2aa174d8d: Ignorance reduces culpability, so those who did wrong without knowing better are punished less. The more a person has been given or entrusted with, the more is rightly demanded of them.
7ef018b4eb0bf022: A message that demands real change sets the world on fire rather than calming it, and it brings division rather than peace. The one who carries such a message lives under strain until it is finished.
09129c0eef077970: A message that demands real change splits even a single household into opposing camps.
33d24c0da48c7172: Deep conviction divides people along the lines of their closest family ties, setting parent against child.
99c13b842a7f185b: People become skilled at reading the signs of ordinary, practical things and predicting what will follow.
18f81f343f02abf6: It is inconsistent to interpret everyday signs expertly while refusing to interpret the moral significance of your own moment, when you are perfectly capable of judging for yourself what is right.
785286a485fd630e: Settle a dispute with your opponent early, while you still can, rather than letting it escalate into a formal judgment you cannot control.
fd98746bfdf70b78: Once a judgment is handed down, the full price must be paid; there is no partial escape.
5faaf591152b5554: Those looking for a reason to condemn you watch closely for the moment a human need collides with a rule.
f5eb5b947636c2b3: When asked directly whether mercy is permitted, those who enforce rules against it fall silent, because they cannot defend the position out loud.
3510093a2ef0ee51: People suspend their own strict rules the moment their own interests are at stake, which shows the rules were never applied consistently.
6bea39960769eb39: Do not claim the most prominent position for yourself, because someone more deserving may arrive.
06c03e012db5abd4: Being publicly demoted from a place you claimed for yourself is more humiliating than never claiming it.
8ca744d8c8ec4973: Take the lowest place, and any honor you receive will come from others rather than from yourself, which is the only kind that carries weight.
1963cca7fb249da3: Those who raise themselves up get brought down, and those who lower themselves get raised.
7df471bf00b26845: Giving to people who can pay you back is a transaction, not generosity, because you have already collected your reward.
9c41d0cc7449a302: Real generosity is directed at those who have no way of repaying you.
a851a547e28c7b0e: A genuine invitation is extended widely, to far more people than will accept it.
2ec2da5321fbb5ec: People decline what matters most by pleading obligations to their property.
b505f19560afe98d: People decline what matters most by pleading obligations to their work and their families.
f169a52bcf497b8a: When those with standing refuse an invitation, it passes to those who had none.
76c7cd5256349331: Generosity keeps reaching further outward until nothing is left unfilled.
c81fd287f17aff54: Those who make excuses forfeit the thing they were offered, and it is not held open for them.
0b8d6b5e049aa149: A commitment that comes second to your family ties and your own life is not a real commitment. Anyone unwilling to carry the cost personally has not actually taken it on.
18965b2a7b53b5fa: Before starting something, calculate whether you have the resources to finish it.
fddc8f58f3089469: An abandoned project invites ridicule, so any serious undertaking begins with an honest assessment of whether your strength matches what you are up against.
21e069c1ba1aae24: If you cannot meet the cost, negotiate rather than pretend, and if you are unwilling to give up everything the thing requires, you cannot have it. Something that has lost the quality that made it valuable cannot get that quality back from outside.
5319ade331f50831: A thing that has lost its essential quality is useless for every purpose and gets thrown away.
4486e5825601a877: Welcoming disreputable people as equals reliably scandalizes the respectable.
3b67a8c146ae33fc: A single lost member of a group matters enough to justify leaving the secure majority and going after it.
c2363311452e757c: Recovering what was lost is a joy meant to be shared rather than kept private.
6373cfd09e365aae: Recovering one person who went wrong produces more joy than the continued presence of many who never went wrong.
d4de5ee770861e4d: What is genuinely valuable to you is worth an exhaustive search, however small it is.
9b7f2f0511a641e5: Finding what was lost calls for celebration, and the recovery of one person who went wrong is met with joy.
b4902bb9bd3fcbd8: The young take what they are owed, leave, and squander it.
4a725db4e7ff3fcc: Squandered resources leave a person exposed when circumstances turn hard, and dependent on strangers for degrading work.
caff5365a3ba8593: Hitting bottom is often what makes a person see their situation clearly and recognize what they left behind.
076d1fed2f2b2490: Genuine repentance means returning, admitting the wrong plainly, and claiming no entitlement to be restored.
36b913136d596fbc: Real forgiveness runs to meet the returning person before the apology is finished, and does not wait to be asked.
758f58a054f8fe7c: Forgiveness restores a person to their full former standing rather than granting them some lesser, probationary status.
351de4872fc3912c: The return of someone thought lost is a genuine resurrection worth celebrating, though those who stayed behind may not understand what the celebration is for.
6defaef0aad13521: The dutiful resent mercy shown to those who did not earn it, and refuse to join the celebration.
a82f961a673e818e: Long obedience can curdle into a sense of entitlement and a grievance that faithfulness was never rewarded the way failure now is.
d1240febed4c2a27: Those who resent another's forgiveness overlook that they possessed all along, freely, everything they thought they were owed.
1a5880fdb364b14d: Restoring what was lost is worth celebrating even when it seems unfair to those who never left.
715b02a9acde4ae4: Anyone managing what belongs to another will eventually be required to account for how they handled it.
4c4a138e653ba5a4: Faced with losing his position, a person plans shrewdly for what comes after, using what little leverage he still has.
db36b19382bbbea1: A person about to lose power uses it, while it lasts, to put others in his debt.
e7735c0961cb4dcf: Reducing what others owe you buys goodwill that outlasts the money.
8abdad1de16be340: People who pursue worldly ends are often more clear-eyed and resourceful about achieving them than good people are about theirs.
ebe0a120958c1125: Use money to build relationships rather than to accumulate, since it will not last. How a person handles small matters reveals exactly how they will handle large ones.
1f178a37791c9b2d: Someone who cannot be trusted with money, or with what belongs to someone else, will never be given anything genuinely valuable or genuinely their own.
f5a8fb6507f6558f: No one can serve two ultimate loyalties, because devotion to one becomes contempt for the other, and the pursuit of wealth competes directly with the pursuit of goodness.
42ab0a0047cdba67: People construct justifications for themselves in public while their actual motives remain visible to anyone who can see inward. What a society admires most is often what is worth least.
8ae69d23b9629dc2: An old order gives way to a new one that people press eagerly into, yet the underlying moral law outlasts the physical world.
930e344380023384: Discarding a spouse to marry another is a betrayal, and so is marrying someone who was discarded. A person can live in daily luxury while remaining entirely unmoved by what is outside his door.
3e7381d1d93d7caa: Extreme suffering can sit at the very threshold of extreme comfort and go completely unnoticed.
69c665769a345f63: Fortunes reverse after death: the neglected poor are comforted and the comfortable rich are not.
8d07598a1ad81646: In torment, the once-powerful must beg relief from the very people they ignored.
cc10901a2375eff3: Those who take all their comfort in this life have no further claim on it afterward.
56f70654df863c8a: After death a person's condition is fixed, and no one can cross from one side to the other.
729a0c19588fe87b: The guidance needed to live rightly is already available to anyone who will listen to it.
663c7991da447e83: People who ignore ordinary evidence will not be persuaded by miraculous evidence either, because refusal to believe is a matter of will rather than proof.
bdb1a9dcc1b1a73f: Real love holds steady to the end, even when you know the end is coming.
a2fded66d01c9cf5: Betrayal takes root quietly inside the closest circle, and knowing your own standing lets you meet it without flinching.
59a07d30aea878b7: The person with the highest standing is the one who can afford to take on the lowest task.
e977293d8aaac371: An act of service can be baffling while it is happening and understood only afterward.
d12b2d83c946e4be: Refusing to let someone serve you is pride, and it shuts you out of the relationship.
13405ec44de792c4: Someone already fundamentally sound needs only small ongoing correction, not repeated wholesale renewal.
a6f2a1a2448c667a: If the person at the top serves, no one below can treat service as beneath them.
5a98d0080d1ce5be: An example counts only when it is copied; knowing what is right does you no good until you do it.
07f5dc37ac95a905: Betrayal by an intimate is foreseeable, and saying so in advance turns the shock into confirmation.
183c09ee6e2cfc5d: How you treat someone's representative is how you treat that person.
9b7fbce01029b9b3: Within a close group, a betrayer is indistinguishable from the loyal.
203709ec6267fcad: The betrayer is identified by an act of intimacy rather than by exposure.
74a2b98d6ba68819: Once a person has settled on betrayal, delay only prolongs it.
94ee2c147df9cd99: Those closest to a decisive turn routinely misread it while it is happening.
c2acc51b5d392965: What looks from outside like defeat can be the moment of greatest honor.
316ff7fc4ddc3da5: Love one another in the same way you yourselves have been loved.
537fe7f60842ab10: A community is recognized by how its members treat each other, not by what it claims; and some paths cannot be walked until you are ready for them.
89398e36568b04e3: Confidence in your own courage outruns your actual capacity for it.
e77c05b31c9a8f70: There is room enough for everyone, so do not let your heart be troubled.
199057e1e33923ea: People often already possess the knowledge they insist they lack.
1b9ab6e9e51cef19: The way, the truth, and the life are not separate destinations but one and the same, reached through what stands concretely in front of you.
efae753570f8db84: The invisible source is fully visible in what it does and produces.
97f4dfbf7c8448eb: If someone's words do not convince you, judge them by their works instead.
142a59bb2409ace6: A genuine teacher's students go on to do more than the teacher did.
1797111e4398a8fb: Love shows itself as obedience rather than sentiment.
336403af47b849fd: Truth is invisible to those who have never known it from the inside.
b8c6c4651ea46102: A life shared with its source outlasts the source's visible presence.
5795942232dac912: What you actually do reveals whom you love, and that love is what opens deeper knowledge.
bc0f42d73698c0bd: Love makes a dwelling place, and the teaching a true teacher gives was never his own to begin with.
8ce9872f45b61c09: What you were once taught is recovered later by an inner teacher that brings it back to mind.
40dad09a1054dbe9: Real peace does not depend on circumstances the way ordinary peace of mind does.
e62bbd8c7fdce5b4: To love someone is to want their good even when it costs you their presence.
441bc6d62d049646: Nothing external can get a grip on a person who has nothing inside for it to grip.
65472fac5b79e514: What bears fruit gets cut back so it will bear more; what bears nothing is removed entirely.
1bf51b17af685dc3: Nothing bears fruit once it is cut off from its source.
fc8b54c12721b2ed: When your desires stay rooted in the source, what you ask for is what you receive.
b0cdd99eeef42aad: You remain inside love by continuing to act according to it.
dce5104537a13625: The highest form of love gives up its own life for the sake of friends, and the point of it is shared joy.
a5d11951a9c1cbe6: Withholding information keeps people servants; telling them everything makes them friends.
aaee6da66bb22c52: You did not do the choosing, and the purpose of being chosen is to produce something that lasts.
ab53b5fc6c1d44ac: Any group loves its own, so being genuinely different from it guarantees hostility.
f599e225ae16beae: Expect to receive the same treatment your teacher received.
f4ea8bca92edf50b: Once the truth has been made plain, ignorance is no longer an excuse.
6a6d6d9160fa94b8: Hatred often has no cause behind it at all.
b1c3c23332607eec: Firsthand presence from the beginning is what qualifies a person to testify.
3abf8c4207eb5fef: Offer your whole living self rather than a ritual substitute; that is the only response that makes sense.
9317e2e5b0bbfd6b: Do not let the surrounding culture shape you; be changed instead by remaking the way you think.
c69f6a306c50af29: Assess yourself soberly, not more highly than you actually are.
151cf0c21dbcddbc: People differ in function but form one body, each belonging to the others.
ab63d92a8d05404b: Do the thing you are actually gifted for, in proportion to the gift you have.
f3f377a4557fdb3a: Carry out your role wholeheartedly and in the spirit it calls for, and let your love be genuine rather than performed.
205010be140d4cc9: Prefer other people's honor to your own, stay diligent in work, and stay warm in spirit.
8855525907d3888b: Bless those who harm you, enter into other people's joy and grief, and never think yourself above anyone.
90808a06e7da2b85: Never repay evil with evil or take revenge; live at peace with everyone as far as it depends on you.
5e780d672157d3ef: Meet hostility with practical kindness rather than retaliation; evil is defeated by good, not by more evil.
e3cb644b53bfeac7: Eloquence without love is meaningless noise.
9af0ccd3c3391a1e: Insight, knowledge, and conviction are worth nothing in a person who lacks love.
29e043a049e8ef9d: Even total self-sacrifice gains nothing without love. Love is patient and kind, and free of envy, boasting, and self-importance.
9d2aae1a502ded14: Love is not rude, self-seeking, irritable, or resentful; it takes no pleasure in wrongdoing but delights in truth, and it bears, trusts, hopes, and endures without limit.
d63a89f08587198f: Love outlasts everything else, because all human knowledge and insight is partial and will eventually be superseded.
da4ae102c7ae3c4b: Understanding matures as a person matures, and our present grasp of things is indirect and incomplete compared with seeing them fully.
0129cc9909b177b2: Of faith, hope, and love, love is the greatest.
4eb17aa399826e43: Difficulty is worth welcoming, because being tested is what produces endurance.
2f8d9bc294188b08: Let endurance finish its work and you become complete. Wisdom is freely available to anyone who genuinely asks for it.
46c31c0130147f5a: Ask with settled conviction rather than divided intent, because a person at odds with himself is unstable in everything he does and gains nothing.
ff6a43086746e76d: Ranks are reversed in the end: the lowly are raised up, and the rich are brought down because their standing is as short-lived as a wildflower.
ba343e9afcd88132: Wealth withers as fast as grass in the heat, and the rich man disappears in the middle of his pursuits.
d059aaf597dffa64: Enduring a trial is rewarded. But no one should blame the divine for tempting him, since what is wholly good neither feels temptation nor supplies it.
2b4d129643503ba9: Temptation comes from a person's own desire; desire once indulged produces wrongdoing, and wrongdoing fully grown produces death.
4e06a04386090d7a: Everything genuinely good originates in an unchanging source, which brings us into being through truth.
7e2b3a7168899f9a: Be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to anger, since human anger produces nothing righteous. Strip away corruption and receive teaching humbly.
1a487129661f92b9: Hearing a teaching without acting on it is self-deception, like glancing at your reflection and immediately forgetting what you look like.
f6d1b65f1b190527: The one who keeps attending to the law and acts on it is blessed in what he does. Religion that leaves the tongue unchecked is worthless.
87c0235e986ecf09: Genuine religion is caring for the vulnerable and keeping yourself uncorrupted by the world.
