744fc30928e24414: Work and study should feed each other: whoever holds an office should study in his spare hours, and whoever finishes his studies should take up an office.
1105409d183baf04: Grief should be felt to its full depth and then allowed to end.
84ca79c95fe1d966: A person can accomplish things nearly impossible for others and still fall short of being genuinely good.
b03f03a0d14f40e0: An impressive manner in another person can make it harder, not easier, to practise virtue alongside them.
8438e775cc92a9ec: People rarely reveal everything that is in them, except in grief for a parent.
50d5f684593b42bd: Ordinary acts of devotion to a parent are within anyone's reach; leaving a dead father's people and policies untouched is the part almost no one manages.
c6e8061dd3513f9a: When you establish someone's guilt, the right response is grief and pity for people whom their rulers have failed, not satisfaction at your own competence.
7e820267888ca152: A notorious person's actual wickedness is usually smaller than the name attached to him, which is why one avoids occupying the low position into which all the world's blame drains.
518f9a104db213d7: A good person's faults are as visible as an eclipse, and so is the correction, which is why everyone looks up to him again afterwards.
52bcfbf15aea202b: Wisdom survives scattered among ordinary people, the large principles held by some and the small details by others, so anyone willing to learn from everyone has no need of a single master.
1047107983ce90ec: A modest person's worth is visible over a low wall, while a great person's is hidden behind a high one, so most people, never finding the door, will underrate the greater and overrate the lesser.
d12500ba66f62342: Ordinary talent is a hill anyone can step over, but true greatness is like the sun and moon, and abusing it injures no one but the abuser, who only reveals the limits of his own understanding.
64f51f95fd655bc1: A single sentence can mark a person as wise or as a fool, so speak with care.
fdd058ad180fa3b9: Truly good governance shows in results, when people settle where they are placed, follow where they are led, thrive, live in harmony, and mourn the ruler bitterly when he dies.
31e28c9c018ac219: Authority is held on condition of holding to the mean and keeping the people from want, and a real ruler takes his people's offences onto himself rather than pushing his own onto them.
8ba068a971906421: A ruler should prize proven virtue over blood relation, absorb the people's blame rather than deflect it, and win their hearts by honest standards, sound law, restored institutions, and the recall of the overlooked.
605bdbb559120982: Governing well means attending first to food, mourning, and sacrifice, and it is generosity that wins people, sincerity that earns their trust, effort that achieves things, and fairness that makes them glad.
c219bd17cb6a8a67: Good government means being generous without extravagance, demanding work without resentment, ambitious without greed, and dignified without either pride or harshness.
4bead7c2fd5c9d8c: You benefit people by improving what already benefits them, avoid resentment by assigning work that fits, escape the charge of greed by wanting only good government, and hold dignity by showing disrespect to no one, whatever their number or size.
c7c50093c2bab642: Punishing people you never taught is cruelty, demanding finished work you never warned them about is oppression, and issuing an order casually then enforcing it severely is injury.
598ba53786348361: Paying people grudgingly reduces a leader to a mere functionary. Without recognising what is given, knowing how to conduct oneself, and understanding the weight of words, a person cannot mature, hold a stable character, or read other people.
a4bda564a2893d1f: The same source that made human beings taught them what they did not know, yet people turn insolent as soon as they feel self-sufficient in wealth, forgetting that everything returns to where it came from.
4730a9e009136a75: Whoever obstructs another's devotion, calls the truth a lie, and turns away is nonetheless seen, and the right response is not to follow him but to keep to your own worship.
7090f6865afd1620: Stand up and warn people, keep yourself and your surroundings clean, avoid what is corrupt, give without expecting return, and wait patiently.
33ff74dca76fb55f: Wealth, family, and a smoothed path can breed an appetite that never closes and a deliberate, calculating hostility to truth, which is what actually ruins a person.
92de57a1511f720e: Dismissing a message as mere magic or a mortal's invention brings its own consequence, and the strange details of a warning function as a test, confirming those who already believe and unsettling those who do not.
d1fb1cfd9f24aeb1: The very same sign strengthens some people's faith and gives others grounds for doubt, and no one can fully account for how guidance and misguidance are distributed.
259ea3a3b7ce06ee: Every soul stands as security for its own deeds, and those who fail name their own reasons: neglecting prayer, ignoring the poor, arguing for the sake of arguing, and denying that a reckoning would ever come.
05571d675bc3ce63: People bolt from a warning like animals from a predator and demand private proof delivered to them personally, but the real obstacle is that they do not take the future seriously.
bfbd39e5d653999c: The message itself is warning enough for anyone willing to receive it, and that willingness is not entirely self-generated.
1b4b5d0c1d048020: Give the night to deliberate, unhurried prayer, because that is when the heart is most receptive and speech most collected, while the day is consumed by business.
3e419e4e10c90ddb: There is one source of everything, so take it as your protection, endure what people say without retaliating, and withdraw from them gracefully rather than fighting.
135d0085304f8264: Warnings have been delivered before and ignored before at great cost, and each person is free to take the path or refuse it.
99c0acd9155fec4f: Obligations are calibrated to human limits, since some people are ill, some are travelling, and some are at war, so do what you can manage rather than what would break you.
052f44a948392a53: What you do for others is stored up on your own behalf, and forgiveness remains available to those who ask.
3987a40f1e46b400: A difficult stretch is not abandonment; someone who was once orphaned, lost, and poor was already being provided for, and what comes will be better than what came before.
976f99159a62ad95: Because you were once helpless and were helped, do not wrong the orphan, do not turn away the person who asks, and speak openly about what you were given.
09bd5457eb2e2ba9: Relief does not follow hardship so much as accompany it, so when one burden lifts, take up the next task rather than stopping.
f8f398263e964ce0: Take refuge in the source of things from the harms latent in creation itself: the dark, malicious craft, and the envy of others.
bf6b6fbe61e2a4ba: The most dangerous harm is the whisper that slips into the heart and withdraws, whether it originates in unseen forces or in other people.
2be0be666368ee49: Praise belongs to the merciful lord of all worlds and judge of the reckoning; we worship and seek help from that alone, asking to be kept on the straight path walked by those who neither provoke anger nor go astray.
2b6bb239b4ff04bc: Where belief is fundamentally incompatible, worship cannot be merged, and each side keeps its own.
0a56deb61dc1b973: The divine is single and eternal, neither begetting nor begotten, and nothing is comparable to it.
02caa6720174264c: Wealth and standing will not save someone who sets himself against the truth, and his ruin extends to those who help him do it.
5c3cf4f500deaf4c: What you have been given abundantly should be answered with prayer and sacrifice, and the one who hates you is the one who ends without descendants.
1017641c67fe4b06: Slandering others and hoarding wealth in the belief that it makes you permanent ends in a fire that reaches the heart.
f9ff37b4a934c486: The righteous fear divine judgment, restrain their appetites, keep their promises and trusts, and testify honestly.
e95958f8994d6b6f: Those who maintain regular devotion will be honored, while scoffers who crowd around mocking have no claim on reward.
fe2c0c398c71ea6e: Those who reject truth can be replaced by better people, and they will one day face the reckoning they were warned about in shame.
02d1bbd346077fc5: The same power that gave humans speech set the universe in balance, and people are therefore obliged to deal fairly and not cheat in their weighing.
c63eb8c0ce33114f: The abundance of the natural world is a gift, and different kinds of beings were made from different substances by a single source.
47286c8efab6bde1: Everything that exists will pass away except the source of existence itself, which is continually at work sustaining all things.
36f06b33b562f839: No created being can escape the limits set for it except by permission of the power that set them.
c2e55cb7e2dcfcc4: Wrongdoers are recognizable by their own marks and reap suffering, while those who hold the divine in awe receive abundance.
f02ec8eaaa31581c: The reward of good can only be good.
d001216d8b3193c7: What awaits the faithful is described as gardens, fountains, fruit, and beauty beyond any earthly enjoyment.
7144b8b37e79632c: The name of the divine, full of majesty and glory, is to be blessed.
a9c9cfae23ef4614: People dismiss even clear signs as trickery and follow their own appetites, but the outcome of things is already fixed.
20ff6db6060e581d: Those who mocked warnings will come out shamed on the day of reckoning, just as earlier generations mocked the messengers sent to them.
eb506f1b0cf37abd: A great flood destroyed those who rejected the warning, and the story remains as a sign, though few take heed.
92f8c0fa8ff2dbcb: Warnings have been made easy to understand, yet people ignore them and are destroyed, as an ancient people was destroyed by a devastating wind.
d650dc7ad1c35c76: People reject a messenger simply because he is an ordinary person like themselves, calling him arrogant, but time reveals who the liar really was.
58f5885ca7251bed: A people were tested with a simple rule about sharing water; they broke it violently and were destroyed.
6f783e537a1f740f: A people who dismissed warning as a lie were destroyed, while the one who believed and his household were spared, because gratitude is rewarded.
818947dc2572d6b3: No group is exempt from consequence, and those who treated all evidence as fraud were overtaken by ruin.
0335865589bce2e5: Confidence in numbers and alliances is no protection; wrongdoers are lost in confusion, and everything unfolds by a fixed order.
cab5e2fdcf46f457: Every action, small or great, is recorded, and the peaceful end belongs to those who live rightly.
873950993b02ea0d: Live rightly and seek forgiveness now, because the appointed time comes and cannot be postponed once it arrives.
5414ab432c1bfe09: People stop their ears against uncomfortable truth and grow proud, though forgiveness and flourishing are freely offered to those who ask.
09ee1a85018788ba: The one who made you in stages, raised the heavens, and grew you from the earth is worth trusting for good.
9312a98d0cc5db92: Humans return to the earth and are brought forth again, and the world was made a spacious place for them to live in.
256abdd1a92ff4ba: Attachment to inherited idols leads people astray and ruins them, and they discover too late that nothing but the divine could help.
38b696f588c1124d: Seek forgiveness for yourself, your parents, and all who share your faith.
ddb975ba87963494: Humans are made from nothing they remember, given senses and shown the right way, and are then free to be grateful or ungrateful.
64e4b9158856a9b7: The just keep their promises and give food to the poor, the orphan, and the prisoner, seeking neither payment nor thanks.
d97a827ca9f08fa9: Those who endure faithfully are spared from suffering and given lasting joy and ease.
1c3a912066ba8bb2: The reward for the faithful is described as a place of pure drink, radiance, and boundless dignity.
d0e0e4fbf4ef5c5d: Be patient, keep to your practice, ignore the corrupt, and don't be like those who love the passing moment and forget what is coming.
2bf8978fa659b999: Those who wish can take the path toward the divine, but even that willing depends on a will greater than their own.
0803df3ed0934cfc: There is one source of life and death, and messengers are sent as a mercy to warn people.
a5bb7c55b7ece7f1: People beg for relief when disaster strikes and promise belief, but they had already turned away when the warning came clearly.
4f9f5c2aaefb6b41: Relief from suffering does not reform people; they simply relapse into their old ways.
f77246424c8d5aa4: An oppressor who exalts himself and pursues the innocent destroys himself in the pursuit.
7eed5ae892113a71: Tyrants lose everything they enjoyed and no one mourns them, while the people they degraded are lifted up.
14b10df064fb845e: Denying that anything follows death is a failure to see that the universe was not made as a game but for a serious purpose.
77ec28830852968e: On the day of separation no one can be rescued by rank or relationship; only mercy helps.
ba39efed7cc24d64: Those who thought themselves mighty and honorable are humbled, while the humble and pious rest secure.
7ed4631cb6fd7157: The teaching has been made plain and accessible in ordinary language so people can take it to heart.
227e0ad5691f29b9: People find the idea of returning after death absurd, but nothing is lost and everything is accounted for.
370836df1d24ec25: The flawless sky, the spread earth, and the rain that revives dead ground are all evidence available to anyone willing to look.
e473411ca78fe301: Just as rain brings a dead land back to life, so the dead are raised; every people who mocked their teachers met the consequence.
c967b4c55cb20d0b: If the first creation was not exhausting, a second is no harder; the divine knows even what your soul whispers and is nearer to you than your own jugular vein.
46ab7fc279119d8f: Death removes the veil and makes clear what a person spent life avoiding.
332b48adab1b96f3: Those who blocked good, transgressed, doubted, and worshipped false things face judgment, and blaming others will not help them.
c2577e583698f457: Suffering is never satisfied, but peace is brought near to those who turned back with a humble heart and revered the divine in private.
d141059152256188: Mightier generations than yours have vanished; the evidence is there for anyone with a heart, an ear, and open eyes.
16795af26c21c977: The one who gives life and death will gather all back, and this presents no difficulty.
68eb1add184d0246: You are not to force belief on anyone; only warn and let people choose.
ade1ee852004dd90: Some people have become so closed that warning them makes no difference.
9d679ea9aa9c88a7: Warning only reaches the person who is already willing to listen and reveres the divine in private; everything a person does and every trace they leave behind is recorded.
30018227a3701e90: People dismiss messengers by saying they are only ordinary humans like themselves.
0ee86d176c9c4017: Those who blame others for their own bad luck are the erring ones; trust the teacher who asks nothing from you in return.
1f59d6ef3796394a: It makes no sense to worship what cannot help you when trouble comes, rather than the one who made you.
328000897f8b95f6: A man wished his mocking people could see how graciously he had been treated after death, but they were swept away in a moment.
27bde2030cb33597: The reviving of dead earth into gardens and grain that people eat is a sign worth gratitude.
27fe961c73591cba: Everything is made in complementary pairs, including things beyond human knowledge, and night, sun, and moon all move by fixed decree.
3fbe55eedb0cd876: The regularity of the natural world — earth, mountains, the two sexes, sleep, night and day, the sky — is evidence of deliberate design, and the truth people argue over will eventually become undeniable to them.
633e9bd0c5df7f07: The same power that sustains life through sun and rain has set a fixed end for the world, at which the present order will come apart.
bcdaf8cb53ef8cff: Those who refused to face the fact of accountability, and dismissed the evidence as lies, receive a punishment exactly proportionate to what they did, because every act was recorded.
8867b630f31c22a0: Those who lived in awe of what is greater than themselves receive lasting peace, free of empty talk and deceit. In the final reckoning no one gets to argue their case; only what is true may be spoken, and only with permission.
0dec6a23f5263990: The day of reckoning is certain, and anyone who wishes may still turn back toward it. On it each person simply sees the deeds he has already sent ahead of himself, and the one who refused will wish he had never existed.
c6feea2034987e34: In the end people divide into two conditions: those exhausted and tormented by what they made of themselves, and those at ease, pleased with the work they did.
bb795fa0d269a8cf: The structure of clouds, sky, mountains, and earth is there to be looked at and reasoned from. One who carries a message can only deliver it; he cannot compel anyone to accept it.
7087eabdb9d484c7: No one has coercive authority over another's belief; those who turn away are answerable not to the messenger but to God, who alone settles the account.
15137b91fb875246: The great powers of the past — magnificent, monumental, seemingly untouchable — all destroyed themselves through excess.
db887dc59b00c65a: People misread their circumstances: they take prosperity as proof they are favored and hardship as proof they are despised, when both are tests. The real measure is whether you cared for the orphan and urged others to feed the poor.
429e95e3fe651422: Greed for inheritance and love of wealth blind a person until the moment of reckoning, when he finally understands himself — but understanding arrives too late to be of any use.
c648cd8edd06d155: The soul that has come to rest returns to its source willingly, and is welcomed.
712dd9400145e801: People deny that they will be reconstituted after death not because the evidence is against it, but because they prefer to go on doing as they please.
6716c870fde08fed: When the account is finally read there is nowhere to escape to, and no defense is possible, because a person is the clearest witness against himself.
46ed2cdc5b4b4f13: People love what is immediately in front of them and neglect what is lasting, and so their faces are radiant or stricken at the end according to which they chose.
04065ee112b938a9: At the moment of death no remedy or charm can hold a person back; he goes to his reckoning as the man he actually was — one who denied the truth, refused to pray, and followed the arrogant.
39a37ed745a9482f: A person imagines he is answerable to no one, forgetting he began as an embryo; whatever power brought him into existence from nothing can plainly do so again.
e5f5670ad1bfb2b4: It is corrupt to demand full measure when receiving and give short measure when paying; the double standard is a form of theft, and it is written down.
af35f10760c9f7be: People dismiss the truth as old fables not from reason but because their own accumulated deeds have taken over their hearts, and the consequence of that self-blinding is being cut off from what they refused to see.
b0e271e32f66bf9a: The record of the good is kept in a high place, and they live in visible delight, their faces bright with it.
880217abca1e4810: The reward of the good is of a finer kind than anything worth chasing here. Meanwhile the corrupt make sport of the faithful, winking and joking at their expense and calling them deluded.
0b4b800676d89a8d: Those who mock others were never appointed to judge them; in the end the positions reverse, and everyone is repaid exactly according to what they did.
618c5120b739078d: Civilizations that treated the day of reckoning as a fable were swept away by forces they could not resist, and left nothing behind.
44026f448981f4a4: Tyrants and cities that defied the truth were destroyed, and their destruction was preserved as a warning for anyone with an ear willing to retain it.
9596a61b0143a23e: When the world is finally undone, everything a person kept hidden is brought into the open.
184d33dbd450d1b6: The one who lived expecting to answer for his life meets the account gladly, while the one who never expected it wishes for annihilation and finds that his wealth and power are gone and cannot help him.
4089515d9e1eb611: What condemns a person in the end is not poverty but refusing to acknowledge God and never bothering to feed the hungry — and then there is no friend left to intercede.
98b2e7875d501b77: This message is not poetry, and not the trance-speech of a fortune-teller; it comes from the source of the world itself.
8a210fec5231727d: Had the messenger invented these words, he would have been destroyed for it; the message is certain truth, a warning to those who take it seriously and a source of despair to those who do not.
c91cdfbf243e87f6: The reckoning is coming, however contradictory and confused people's accounts of it are; those who turn from the truth are turned away by their own choosing.
d495242c1fd6b115: The people who mock by demanding the punishment arrive early get exactly what they asked for. Those who feared God slept little, prayed through the night, and sought forgiveness at dawn.
f814e3e9de0aaae7: The good give a rightful share of their wealth both to the one who asks and to the one too ashamed to ask. The evidence for all this is written in the earth and in your own body, if you would only look.
75ccd36eafee375f: Hospitality to strangers is met with unlooked-for blessing, and what seems biologically impossible is not impossible to the power that made biology.
54df8ffaa39c8c79: When a society is destroyed for its excesses, the few who lived rightly are brought out of it first, and the ruin left behind serves as a sign to those who take warning.
2be483c1ea4c36ba: Pharaoh dismissed truth as sorcery and madness and relied on his armies instead; the armies did not save him.
5e2d782696abd215: Nation after nation was destroyed for defiance. The heavens were built and the earth spread out, and everything was made in complementary pairs — so reflect on it, turn to the one source, and set up no rival to it.
7d89bf9687daf258: Every messenger in history has been called a sorcerer or a madman, as though the insult were handed down as an inheritance; the sameness of the response reveals it as reflex, not judgment.
cd3cd0fe4106555c: Conscious beings exist in order to worship, and God needs nothing from them in return — no offering, no sustenance, no support.
90146778c9638f51: A reckoning is coming that cannot be postponed, and it falls on those who called truth a lie and treated serious questions as material for idle argument.
ba4cd210112010a9: In the fire, patience and impatience make no difference — you receive exactly what your actions earned. Those who lived in awe of God are spared it and rest in what they were given.
c80f223e36fcecab: Every person stands as security for his own deeds and gets exactly what they deserve, defrauded of nothing. Those whose children followed them in faith are reunited with them.
0e940380494aa924: Looking back, the saved say they once lived in anxiety over their families' fate; the anxiety was answered by mercy, not by their own effort.
42a6bb513cf8f30e: Opponents wait for a truth-teller's fortunes to turn, treating that as refutation; but a change in his luck would prove nothing about what he said.
302158f42c5cce31: The charge of forgery is a cover for prior unbelief: let the accusers produce its equal if they are honest. They did not create themselves, nor the heavens and the earth — so on what standing do they judge?
876890df386f3561: Those who reject the message have no evidence, no secret knowledge, no access to the divine council, and no alternative god — only assertion. And they explain away even a sign falling from the sky as ordinary weather.
beae2ae42ab8b0af: Leave the deniers to the day their schemes come to nothing, and be patient in the meantime, praising God at dawn, through the night, and as the stars set.
b605d2fdfd07efbc: When the end comes suddenly no one will be able to deny it; it lowers those who were high and raises those who were low, and humanity is sorted into its true divisions.
75ca50958e5345c3: Those who were foremost in doing good remain foremost, and they are brought nearest to God.
45417773d3687513: The reward of the good is proportionate to the labor they gave, and its distinguishing quality is that nothing empty, accusing, or false is spoken there — only peace.
54c63b1be0473ed0: Whatever people were reaching for through pleasure exists in a lasting, unfailing, unforbidden form — and it belongs to the good, drawn from every generation.
ffe86c0ac3928ab0: The condemned were the ones who had every worldly advantage, persisted in serious wrongdoing, and mocked the idea that they and their ancestors could ever be raised again.
f202a092407d8cfb: You did not create the seed of your own life, and it is not you who decreed that you must die; so why assume you know what comes after?
f0fa93442ce82e01: The one who made you can replace you, or remake you in a form you cannot imagine. You already have the evidence of your first creation — reflect on it.
0c006848de233921: You do not make the crop grow, or send the rain, or make the water fresh rather than salt, or grow the wood you burn; the whole basis of your life is given, so be grateful.
e624fdc52a9cfc28: This is genuine revelation, preserved and not to be handled carelessly; it should not be treated as something to deny out of daily habit.
ad36f6330d32bc41: When a dying person's life is leaving him and you stand right there watching, you cannot call it back — which shows how little control you actually have.
3edb9975c1c9e341: The one who draws near to God finds rest and peace at death; the one who called truth a lie finds the opposite.
e41d785678ba5887: A mocker demanded the punishment arrive at once, and no one can prevent it. What people regard as impossibly distant is in fact close; endure the wait with dignity.
fc3ab45b441e1787: On the day of reckoning no one can ransom himself, not with his children, his spouse, his brother, the kin who loved him, or everyone on earth combined.
98433901c1023792: Human beings are impatient by nature: frantic when misfortune strikes and tight-fisted when good fortune comes. The exceptions are those who pray constantly, who set aside a fixed share of their wealth for the beggar and for the one too proud to beg, and who take the reckoning seriously.
ec5e7af128e1788f: The cosmos runs on a fixed order in which nothing collides or overtakes its bounds, and the safety people enjoy within it — carried across dangerous waters — is a granted mercy that could be withdrawn at any moment.
4033ba3c9e865131: People who reject warning turn away from every sign put in front of them, and rationalize refusing charity by arguing the needy would be provided for anyway if they deserved it.
499d93969ca8e80b: Those who demand to know when the reckoning will come will be caught by it mid-argument, with no time to settle their affairs or return home.
abf7e690d528191e: At the final accounting no one is wronged in the least: each is repaid exactly according to what they did.
cb77fff511871ca4: The line between peace and torment is drawn by whether a person served what they knew was hostile to them or what they knew was right, and the warning was given in advance.
20b51c5b35a7444f: On the day of accounting a person's own hands and feet testify against them; denial is useless because the record is the body itself.
e481f6bf80dea35c: Everyone whose life is lengthened is bent by age — a standing lesson for anyone willing to understand it. The message given to humanity is plain warning, not art or poetry.
7620164e2188ab50: People live off provisions and creatures given to them, yet credit powerless substitutes instead, and argue against their own origin from a drop of fluid.
3abd01749bdf108f: Whoever brought a thing into being from nothing can obviously bring it back, so the objection that the dead cannot live again is incoherent — as incoherent as denying that fire can come from a green tree.
9adb2d6bfe0feb27: Creation requires nothing but a will that a thing exist; all things are in that power's hand and return to it.
11a637edf525fe04: A discourse of genuine truth can convert even those predisposed against it, who then recognize that the foolish among them had spoken unjustly about the divine.
51a382483a324900: It is naive to assume no one would lie about ultimate things, and those who seek refuge in occult powers only compound their folly.
7db8b7f4bb29f5dc: No one can know in advance whether what befalls them is meant as harm or as guidance, and there is nowhere to flee from the power behind it.
74fb943945d3acb4: Whoever surrenders to the truth follows the way of truth, and whoever turns from it burns; abundance itself is a form of testing.
da378d624e9f4a45: Devotion belongs only to the source, never to the messenger, who has no power to harm or benefit anyone and no refuge of his own.
fb0ec3cd6290789a: A messenger's sole task is to deliver the message; even he does not know when the reckoning comes, for that knowledge is not divulged.
d0d3b930bea92d24: Hidden knowledge is disclosed only to those chosen to carry it, and even their faithfulness in delivering it is watched and counted.
4a4c135ec5dad79d: Death and life exist to test who acts best, and the created order is without flaw — look twice and your eye returns exhausted, having found nothing.
8726fb76fa1dbb02: Those who ignored warnings will be asked whether no warner ever came to them, and they will have no answer that helps them.
af47f4cf2d7c4560: The condemned admit that a warner did come and they called him a liar; had they listened or understood they would not be where they are, while those who revere in private are rewarded.
d9f001088269e9a1: The one who made you knows the innermost recesses of your thoughts, and the solid ground you walk on is a provision that could quake beneath you.
5f9a4d8d2ebeaded: Nothing sustains you that you control: the birds are held aloft, the supplies are given, and no army can substitute for the power that grants them.
e33ff951bb87a60a: If provision were withheld, no one could supply it, yet people persist in pride. Whether a person grovels face-down or walks upright on a straight road reveals whether they are guided.
1c0d709052279d46: No one knows when the reckoning comes; those who demand it now will recognize it soon enough on their own faces when they see it near.
d7595fe118bf3ab3: Trust belongs to mercy, and error becomes obvious in hindsight; the water you take for granted could sink away, and no one else could bring it back.
6d013c19372732de: Signs are shown to those carried by a higher power, and scripture is given as guidance so that people take no other guardian; gratitude is the mark of a true servant.
f828c9ac43bf7c9c: A people warned they would twice fall into arrogance and crime did exactly that, and consequences arrived each time, followed by restoration to power.
53348661ab797759: Good and evil rebound on the doer alone: if you do well it is for yourself, and if you do harm it is against yourself, and repeated wrongdoing brings repeated ruin.
505bb6234701c9a7: Guidance points to what is most upright, but humans are hasty and pray for their own harm as eagerly as for their good; night and day are set apart so that time itself can be measured and everything made distinct.
11fe600e6721b874: Every person's fate is bound to them and their own record is what condemns or clears them; no one carries another's burden, and no one is punished before being warned.
69bb7eb739c2fe7b: When a society's affluent turn corrupt, the whole society falls with them. Whoever chooses the fleeting life gets exactly that and no more.
1e48b91a1c1dd2e6: Whoever strives for the lasting good, striving as it deserves, has that striving honored; gifts are denied to no one, but the later life is ranked far higher.
e816d23abba5315d: Honor only what is ultimate, and show your aging parents tenderness and respectful speech, remembering that they reared you when you were small.
27e00ea248d700e9: Give kin, the poor, and the traveler their due without squandering, since waste is a kind of destruction; and if you have nothing to give, at least answer kindly.
da8c11b02c871cac: Be neither closed-fisted nor recklessly open-handed, or you will end up in rebuke or in beggary. Do not kill your children out of fear of poverty, and do not commit adultery.
ecc7c4a9599c6458: Do not take life except with just cause, and even the avenger must not overstep the bounds of retaliation. Do not touch an orphan's property except to improve it, and keep your covenants, for they will be asked about.
e86a869d6b08fabd: Measure fully and weigh honestly, do not assert what you have no knowledge of, and do not walk the earth in arrogance — you cannot split the ground or match the mountains in height.
16313d61c1c48a54: Do not project onto the divine the preferences of your own culture; repeated warning only makes those determined to flee it flee further.
d3b2c0a1b38059e0: Everything that exists praises its source in a language humans do not understand, while those who refuse to hear have a veil over their hearts and a heaviness in their ears.
ec38010f2a4cba09: When people are confronted with a message they do not want, they invent explanations — calling the speaker bewitched — that expose their own confusion rather than his falsehood.
5f3ca4ca33a7129e: Whatever made you the first time can remake you, however impossible it seems, and the whole span of a life will look, in retrospect, like a brief while.
341db9aaa0f2e03b: Speak in the kindest way, because discord is exactly what division feeds on; you are not the guardian of anyone else's belief.
9d6403090c3832a4: The things people appeal to for rescue have no power to lift or even shift their trouble — those very things are themselves striving for nearness to their source.
14d454195cbecaf7: Miracles are withheld because earlier peoples called them lies; signs strike terror rather than persuade, and to the determinedly wicked they only increase wickedness.
77ff9f807015ea47: Refusing to bow to someone you consider beneath you is the root of malice, and whoever follows that malice shares its recompense.
577ab8ba3403a07f: Corruption works through persuasion, wealth, children, and promises that turn out to be empty, and it has no power over those who cannot be bought.
8fdfa079847b66ae: In real danger people call only on what is real; brought safely to land, they turn away again — ingratitude is the human default.
ae086c3c78208664: Human beings have been dignified and provided for beyond most of creation, yet each will be summoned with the leaders they chose to follow, and whoever was blind to the truth here stays blind and further lost.
925eba1e3ff17353: Pressure to soften or invent a more agreeable message would have won you friends, but yielding to it would have cost you in life and in death.
102096a770ae7b1e: Those who drive truth-tellers out of the land do not long outlast them; this pattern repeats and does not change.
b1a6d2a1ddf12c38: When truth arrives, falsehood disappears — vanishing is simply what falsehood, by nature, does.
be097917c0d0c913: When things go well people withdraw and forget, and when things go badly they despair; each acts according to their own nature, and human knowledge is only a little.
6c8df45e21864906: Everyone acts according to their own disposition, and no collective effort could produce what genuine revelation is; illustrations of every kind are met with refusal and escalating demands for spectacle.
77a038b3b0429f80: The demand for miraculous proof is endless and always escalating, and the honest answer is that the messenger is only a human being.
4a5f562783cb92be: What actually stops people from accepting guidance is that it arrives through an ordinary human being rather than something more impressive.
b95cff8eb991ce9c: The power that made the heavens and the earth can plainly make your like again; a fixed term is set for everything, though the resistant reject even that.
043e35a30806ed1a: Confronted with clear evidence, a tyrant dismisses the messenger as deluded and destroys himself along with his followers.
03f44a8c51e83909: A message is delivered piecemeal, in slow stages, so that it can be absorbed rather than dumped all at once.
40c1c767c9f723bc: Those who already possess knowledge recognize truth when they hear it and are humbled, not puffed up, by it. Names for the ultimate are many, and prayer should be neither loud nor silent but on a middle path.
4238106375dc21e9: The ultimate has no offspring, no partner, and no protector propping it up out of weakness.
842bb87677b23e4b: A single creator made everything and alone sustains it, so there is no other patron to appeal to; a message warning of this deserves reflection rather than dismissal as invention.
171c6571d723add7: One power governs all things, forming humans from base material and giving them hearing, sight, and understanding, yet people rarely return thanks for it.
fa6b2c4c188bdf3e: People doubt they can be remade after death, but when they finally see the truth they beg to return and live rightly — which means the time to act rightly is now.
fd22911e2b4af92d: Those who ignore their reckoning are ignored in turn; genuine belief shows itself as humility rather than disdain.
0c81ac2390b72b35: Those who pray with both fear and longing and give away what they have receive a reward beyond imagining, because the good and the wicked cannot be treated alike.
f94b2f2501167b83: Wrongdoers face consequences they cannot escape, though lesser suffering comes first as a chance to turn back; the worst offence is to be warned clearly and turn away anyway.
d69476d1772ea9a3: Guidance has been given to earlier peoples through scripture and through leaders who emerged from endurance and conviction, and their disputes will finally be settled.
a3b209512cdc4c21: The ruins of vanished civilizations and rain reviving dead land are evidence people walk past without seeing; belief that arrives only when consequences do comes too late.
4a6d16c40c63ee09: Withdraw from those who reject you and wait, since both sides are waiting on the same outcome.
357cd4efdd58af9e: The natural world — living creatures, the turning of night and day, rain that revives dead ground, the shifting winds — is evidence of a creator for anyone willing to look.
c5a5f1a3ecc0a28f: Someone who hears evidence and meets it with arrogance or mockery rather than attention harms only himself.
815ee3ed720ee3f8: Neither accumulated wealth nor the powers people substitute for God can protect them; even the sea is made navigable so that humans may prosper and be grateful.
023458f0bd13f57e: Everything in existence is given for human use and stands as evidence for those who reflect, and each person's good and evil rebounds onto themselves.
58bd9e34a32b973f: Earlier peoples were given scripture, wisdom, and prophecy, and fell into division not from ignorance but from mutual envy after knowledge had reached them.
1959720197acc932: Follow what you know to be true rather than the wishes of the ignorant, and do not imagine that the just and the wicked will meet the same end.
aded0328225d260b: The world is arranged so that each person is repaid for what they do; someone who makes a god of his own desires becomes deaf and blind and can no longer be guided.
64601c7b2fe0c115: Those who claim this life is all there is and that only time destroys us are stating a conjecture, not knowledge.
e0be2b4a0b02d1cf: At a final reckoning every nation is judged against its own record, in which everything it did has already been written down.
3d1e0b9cbb458d3d: People who scorn warnings and dismiss the reckoning as mere speculation will find their own deeds rising up to surround them.
67f647e28e7c4a3d: Those who treat the truth as a joke and let this life deceive them will be abandoned exactly as they abandoned it.
4e10b29885f75a3c: Greatness in heaven and earth belongs to the creator alone.
cc89e6f812a4ffde: Scripture guides those who pray, give, and trust in a life to come, while some purchase idle distraction in order to lead others away from it.
930259cc2e41310e: Those who go deaf to truth suffer for it while those who believe and act well flourish, and the pillarless sky, the mountains, the animals, and the rain are all signs of a single creation.
1a572b3a547fefd7: Nothing besides God has created anything, and gratitude benefits the grateful person rather than God, who needs nothing.
2c6f575fb945b6b1: Honour your parents, who bore you at real cost, but do not obey them when they ask you to affirm what you have no knowledge of.
70ea3da01e52c3cd: Nothing is hidden, however small; so do right, forbid wrong, endure what comes, and avoid arrogance.
5cf2e689f71887f4: Keep your bearing modest and your voice low, and recognize that rejecting truth because your ancestors believed otherwise is following custom rather than reason.
d55222bafb3f8613: Whoever surrenders to God and does good has taken hold of something secure; do not let other people's unbelief grieve you, since what they do is known and answered for.
c4eb36477be7e973: Even those who reject God concede that he made the world, yet fail to follow through on what that implies; the words of God exceed anything that could be written down.
e7d1fa09907a8724: Creating and reviving all humanity is no harder than doing it for one person, and the ordered turning of night, day, sun, and moon shows that God is real and what people worship besides him is empty.
f536fe75d29d849f: People call on God sincerely when danger surrounds them and waver again the moment they are safe.
cab500b61abc592b: No one can pay another's debt, not even a father for his son; and knowledge of the future — the hour, what a womb carries, what tomorrow brings, where you will die — belongs to God alone.
6d10800f51f7a53a: The world was made in truth and for a limited term, and those who worship other things can point to nothing those things created and no revelation they ever gave.
ef822bb0b95c6c3d: Calling on things that cannot answer is the deepest error, and at the reckoning those things will disown the people who worshipped them.
96349db3e5a03d3e: A messenger claims only to pass on what is revealed to him and to warn openly; if he had invented it, nothing could save him.
dcb5a40765c32ce0: Rejecting a message because other people believed it first, or because it sounds like an old story, is pride rationalizing itself rather than judgement of truth.
b9382b80ad538ac9: This revelation confirms the earlier law given to Moses, and those who say "our Lord is God" and go straight toward him have nothing to fear or grieve over.
045bf9d46e4dd873: Show kindness to your parents, who carried and bore you in pain, and on reaching maturity ask to be made grateful and to do good with what you were given.
94fc22293ed97d50: The grateful have their best works accepted and their failings passed over, while those who mock their parents' faith and call resurrection an ancient fable dismiss what they cannot disprove.
6ac4ea508bace319: Everyone is ranked exactly according to what they did, with no unfairness; those who squandered their gifts on pleasure and pride are left with nothing.
859d0379683e8958: A warner can only deliver the message he was sent with; he does not control when the consequences arrive, and demanding a timetable reveals ignorance rather than doubt.
7f81ceb5ab6e66d4: A people mistook approaching disaster for a rain cloud, and their power, senses, and understanding could do nothing for them once they had denied the evidence in front of them.
24124557aaf4cc52: Cities destroyed before were warned in varied ways so they might return, and the gods they trusted abandoned them at the end.
35bdc7da773101e5: Those who hear truth and recognize it carry it back to their own people as a warning.
823a536dda3e3b32: A power that created the heavens and the earth without tiring can certainly restore the dead; so bear up with patience and do not try to hurry the outcome.
aa207e178de74bef: When the end arrives an entire lifetime will seem to have lasted only an hour, and only those who transgress are destroyed.
8345a8e4495c8a49: People fractured into sects only after clear evidence reached them, though all that was ever asked of them was simple: sincere worship, prayer, and giving.
ba3aea84f8bd5a50: Those who reject the truth are the worst of creatures and those who believe and act rightly the best, and the latter receive lasting reward and mutual delight with God.
c18f24f32d3f8992: Everything in existence praises its creator, who made both believers and unbelievers, gave humans their good form, and sees everything they do.
8f52ebc44eb41765: Nothing is hidden from God, not even the secrets of the heart; earlier peoples who scorned messengers because they were mere men suffered for it, though God loses nothing by their refusal.
248121051e46c001: The dead will be raised and shown what they did, and believing while acting rightly cancels a person's evil deeds.
c95c5f7e9030db5f: Nothing happens without God's permission, and whoever trusts him has his heart guided; the messenger's only duty is plain preaching, so refusal is no fault of his.
0ff3b51e97346406: Wives, children, and wealth are a trial that can pull you away from what matters, though pardon and leniency toward them are better than resentment; those saved from their own greed prosper.
473de3bc5d423fa9: What you give generously is returned to you multiplied and forgiven.
9f12286b38c087f3: A messenger was raised from among ordinary people to teach them scripture and wisdom and purify them, and this is given freely rather than earned.
945385e55f33e09c: Someone given a law who will not live by it is like a donkey carrying a load of books; those who claim to be God's chosen would welcome death if they truly meant it.
902e5e1b91907d6c: The death you flee will meet you regardless, and when the call to worship comes you should set your business aside for it.
24fefb5aeabf24af: After worship, disperse and seek your living, but keep remembering God, because what he offers is better than amusement or trade.
67f6da64b9d55175: Those who obstruct the way of God have their works come to nothing while those who believe have their sins cancelled, because one group followed vanity and the other followed truth.
ecd06b343637593b: In war against unbelievers fight decisively, then take captives to be freed or ransomed once the fighting ends; God could take vengeance himself but instead tests people through one another.
6c0b754f0dcd09ec: Those who live in mutual acceptance with the divine receive lasting peace and flourishing.
e7ca25a1c1a20eb4: Success is granted rather than seized, and it comes with forgiveness, guidance, and an inner calm that deepens conviction.
ab8bd92e4a65999d: Faithfulness earns lasting reward and the erasure of past wrongs, while hypocrisy and suspicion of the divine bring their own cycle of ruin.
07f9566077860e08: A pledge made to a rightful leader is a pledge made to God; breaking it harms only the one who breaks it, while keeping it brings great reward.
63f46c65032bb6b6: People who abandon a shared duty invent excuses their own hearts know to be false, and no excuse alters what is actually known about them.
1912aee38caf9979: Those who refuse the risk cannot later claim the reward, and their protest that this is mere jealousy only reveals their poor understanding.
070dffc038dc3110: Those who avoid a hard duty will be tested by a harder one, though the genuinely incapable bear no blame.
6124808caca85459: Sincere commitment, made when its cost is real, is met with inner steadiness and eventual success.
6aeb87b0e1b1fa31: The same restraint that holds back your enemies also holds back you, and this underlying pattern of things never changes.
d647ce919c53b363: Punishment is withheld when the innocent are so mixed among the guilty that striking the guilty would harm them too.
29291d5f05fb9a77: Where others are seized by the rage of ignorance, calm and steadiness are given instead to those able to receive them.
8a306fa23cd4a3b9: A living truth outlasts every rival system, and those who hold it are unyielding toward its opponents yet tender toward one another.
2181d09013a4922e: A community that grows strong from small beginnings provokes the anger of its enemies by that growth alone.
8aa2a729e0a48af2: Do not forbid yourself what is permitted merely to please others, and do not treat a rash oath as binding.
7a809d95e36593fd: Turning back after going astray is always open, but conspiring against someone under genuine protection is futile.
248ef6986f773a7e: Guard yourself and those in your care from ruin, and let genuine repentance rather than excuses be your response to wrongdoing.
a73a6a29da346cd3: Closeness to a good person does not save someone who betrays what that person stands for.
748625a3b0eaa499: Integrity can survive even inside a corrupt household, and purity of devotion is possible under any circumstance.
791ab7e4e5650394: Do not secretly court the goodwill of those working to destroy what you believe, because concealed loyalties are never truly concealed.
d7fbbe122a73917a: Those hostile to you will harm you when they can, and neither family nor children will stand between you and the consequences of your own actions.
b846013fdb9c0cfd: Renouncing a wrong allegiance must be complete, and even a good person's promise made from affection is not a model worth copying.
84446317dd52b3d0: Ask not to be made a spectacle for those who would use your failure against you, and remember that today's enemy may become tomorrow's friend.
7f6947dbe98bd357: Deal kindly and fairly with those who have not attacked you; only those who have made war on you and driven you out are unfit for friendship.
6e5073c6e3f035fc: Those who seek refuge on grounds of conscience should be received in good faith, with any material obligations to those they left settled fairly on both sides.
2c155922151cd593: Losses from broken households should be equitably compensated, and a sincere commitment to live justly should be met with acceptance rather than suspicion.
d481be2344b5454b: Do not seek intimacy with those who have given up on any meaning beyond this life.
8349d0c0bf0ea9cb: When success finally arrives, answer it with gratitude and a plea for pardon rather than with triumph.
30432192b1a1901c: Do not run ahead of legitimate authority, and lower your voice before those you claim to follow; irreverence quietly undoes your work.
947db50c827bde5b: Shouting for someone's attention shows less understanding than waiting for it, and news from an unreliable source must be verified before you act on it and regret it.
af09773a507de04f: A leader who yields to every demand will lead people into error; genuine guidance makes goodness feel desirable and wrongdoing repellent.
c13b98158e59101a: When members of one community fight, reconcile them; if one side is the aggressor, oppose it until it returns to justice, then make peace impartially.
1a6db7ac14517b60: Do not mock, insult, or nickname one another, since the one you scorn may be better than you. Avoid suspicion, prying, and speaking behind someone's back, which is as repellent as eating the flesh of your dead brother.
f5a320569e1abb8f: Humanity is divided into peoples and tribes so they may come to know one another, and worth is measured by conscience rather than by birth. Outward profession of a faith is not the same as faith having entered the heart.
1c208024d8f1a44b: The sincere are those who believe without wavering afterward and who commit their wealth and their persons to what they believe. Being guided is a gift received, not an achievement to hold over the giver.
13c85ea47c047107: Nothing hidden anywhere in existence escapes divine knowledge.
09f4bb8c7c63e9b1: See everything that exists as pervaded by the divine; enjoy what is real by renouncing possessiveness, and covet no one's wealth.
9291e21e7e735429: Spend your whole life doing right action; performed this way, action leaves no stain on you, and there is no other path.
0f4d78b476089775: Those who deny or destroy their own true self pass into worlds of blinding ignorance.
dc1f0927d5fd141a: The ultimate reality is motionless yet outruns the mind, unreachable by the senses because it is always already ahead, and it is what sustains all living things.
e2a0d7bcbc9a2872: Ultimate reality moves and does not move, is far and near, inside everything and outside it.
32bafc82954e0219: One who sees all beings within the self, and the self within all beings, never turns away in hatred from anything.
b621ebaa0e6389cd: For one who perceives all beings as the self, oneness leaves no room for delusion or grief.
689ec0828bc5afaa: The self is bodiless, pure, all-pervading, and self-existent, and it has ordered all things rightly from the beginning.
606ff4e7b7877a60: Those who pursue ignorance alone enter darkness, and those who pursue knowledge alone fall into a deeper darkness still.
7fd7fc13f6e06e23: Knowledge and ignorance each lead to a different destination.
e60d05061f184469: One who understands both knowledge and ignorance together passes beyond death through the one and reaches the deathless through the other.
e688e021e1ee00ae: Those who worship only the unmanifest enter darkness, and those who worship only the manifest fall into a deeper darkness still.
00db87dc5b8ead52: Worship of the unmanifest and worship of the manifest each lead to a different destination.
f710b4a242d1c72e: One who understands both the perishable world and its unmanifest source passes beyond death through the first and reaches the deathless through the second.
8f4610ceefef3b4a: Truth's face is hidden behind a dazzling brightness, and the seeker must ask for that brilliance to be drawn back before truth can be seen.
4d833bac1358213b: When the blinding glare is withdrawn, the seeker recognises that the being dwelling within the highest reality is his own self.
d3f21b0205dcac20: At death the breath returns to the universal life and the body to ashes, while what remains is the record of what one has done.
547ec19c4b9426c3: Ask to be led along the good path and to have evil and delusion removed, offering yourself again and again.
88567aca245cdae4: Someone hoping for reward in the next world may give away everything he owns.
2f34dea1aa18ab6d: Genuine faith can enter the heart of the young while their elders are performing empty ritual.
030ef5e5ca3bd70a: A gift that costs the giver nothing, made of what is already used up and worthless, earns no merit and leads only to joyless ends.
06cfffd38d52c05a: Pressed by a child's persistent question about what he is truly worth, a man may in anger give away what he most values.
98477988f551811d: The young see clearly that they are neither the best nor the worst, and ask what anyone actually gains by sacrificing them.
952e4d71f6c57a40: Look at those who came before and those alive now: mortals ripen and fall like grain, and like grain they spring up again.
568f7b3bf8504f07: Those who commit themselves to a cause receive clarity and steadiness in return, while those who turn away from it find their efforts amount to nothing.
21198968eb97f83d: History shows that peoples who lived only for appetite came to ruin, while those who held to conviction and right action endured.
42255cb87b39718d: Even the strongest cities fell, and a person guided by clear understanding is not the same as one whose own desires have made their wrongdoing look good to them.
613d7e8023d9a6e9: The end awaiting the faithful and the faithless are utterly unlike; those who listen and then mock in private have hardened themselves and follow only their appetites.
b9587e1c43a41b53: Those who accept guidance are given more of it, while those who wait for undeniable proof will find that by the time it arrives, warning is useless.
7afd9ce1ca7651dc: People who eagerly demand direction lose their nerve when the direction turns out to be costly; honest obedience would serve them better than enthusiastic words.
1d9abe5d995c690c: Those who will not reflect have locked their own hearts, and people who quietly agree to compromise with what they know is wrong are already sliding backward.
8ab8cd6119bf81aa: Whoever loves what is corrupt and resents what is good wastes their own life's work, and their hidden malice eventually betrays itself in how they speak.
303c96486b7d8d33: Adversity is what reveals who is actually steadfast; those who obstruct the truth damage nothing but their own record.
a8524000a345ec37: Do not lose heart or sue for peace when you hold the advantage, since this life is brief play, and what you are asked to give is far less than what you have been given.
bd8f83d7fdb0ab97: If everything you had were demanded of you, your grudging would show; withholding harms only the one who withholds, since what you cling to was never really yours.
ddaed6dc35b23b69: It is deeply wrong to profess what you do not practise, and a cause is served by people who stand together like a solid wall; those who wear down their own guide end up losing their way.
a6a7e3324b0e19be: Each teacher confirms the one before and points to the one after, yet people dismiss clear evidence as trickery, and the worst dishonesty is inventing falsehood about what you were called to.
e375ac758ba636bf: Truth cannot be blown out like a candle by those who hate it; trading your wealth and effort for something worth living by is the only bargain that saves you.
2748f02284164e4c: Committing your resources and your person to what you believe is the best investment you can make, though you may not recognize it as such.
01eef9ae4670e836: Be an actual helper of the cause, not a spectator; when a community splits over conviction, those who commit are the ones who endure.
19e328ef63fbb886: The whole of existence testifies to a single origin that is first and last, visible and hidden, present wherever you are and aware of everything you do.
6ea9b0f9f7fcdc13: Night and day turn by a power that knows even the secrets of the heart; give away what you were only ever left as heir to, and it returns to you.
eb692fe7e9753bbf: Clear teaching exists to bring people out of darkness into light, and those who gave and struggled before the victory, when it cost the most, stand higher than those who joined once it was safe.
08b606a28129ba0e: Whatever you lend generously is returned to you multiplied, and the generous walk by a light that goes ahead of them.
2ceb6d3756a14aa0: Those who only pretended to believe discover too late that they cannot borrow the light others earned; hesitation, doubt, and craving comfort were what deceived them.
df0e5154a59b6e58: Nothing can be paid to buy your way out at the end, so soften your heart to the truth now, before a long comfortable life hardens it beyond reach.
e4390e4e05a1e179: Life returns to dead ground, and so it does to people; those who give freely are repaid double and stand as living witnesses to what they believed.
18281ae37927f4ce: Worldly life is a game of display, rivalry, and the piling up of wealth and children — it flourishes briefly like a crop after rain, then yellows and turns to stubble, an illusion of fulfillment.
058e09577e583fb0: Compete for forgiveness and for a good as vast as the heavens and the earth; and nothing that befalls you, in the world or in yourself, was outside the pattern already written.
1632df7ea89ebb3a: Understanding that outcomes are not yours to command keeps you from despairing at what you lose or exulting over what you gain. Teaching, scripture, and the standard of justice were given so people could deal fairly, and iron was given too, holding both great harm and great use.
e741fc66161ee16d: Insight has been handed down through generations, and many who received it went wrong; monastic withdrawal was a human invention never asked for — only sincerity of devotion was.
3b5a4b06dc636360: Integrity and trust bring both mercy and a light to walk by, and no group can claim ownership of grace, which is given freely and not by inheritance.
44e45ee42917d94c: End a marriage according to proper timing and procedure, without driving the woman from her home, since reconciliation may still become possible.
7e31bad3d1174a09: When the waiting period ends, either keep the marriage with kindness or part with kindness, before honest witnesses; whoever acts with integrity finds a way out and provision from where they never expected.
1c4b49ff16abe011: Waiting periods vary with circumstance, and for a pregnant woman it lasts until she gives birth; acting conscientiously makes what is required easier to bear.
4943df4a2fa4b8e9: House and support a divorced woman according to your means without crowding or distressing her, and pay her for nursing your child; no one is obliged beyond their capacity, and hardship is followed by relief.
39806ec19ba21636: Communities that turned from what they knew were ruined by the natural consequence of their own conduct; a warning comes precisely to lead the thoughtful out of darkness into light.
cb0824ce003f8538: Belief joined to right action leads to a good that lasts.
5686fa39bb9310bf: The layered order of the cosmos is evidence of a power and a knowledge that encompass everything within it.
3cf49d8b4e54576c: People who trusted their fortresses for safety were undone from a direction they never watched, tearing down their own houses with their own hands — an example for anyone with eyes to see.
4df4347f81fb5ecf: Exile was itself the punishment for setting themselves against the truth, and even the destruction of their property served to expose wrongdoing.
61a2ca726f979084: Wealth that comes without your own labor belongs to the community — to kin, orphans, the poor, the stranded — so that it does not circulate only among the rich.

9cfc0fb6aa89253b: What is natural is easy to obtain and what is useless is hard, and simple inexpensive habits produce health, freedom from anxiety, and readiness to meet whatever fortune brings.

1e87eeb283cb2283: The wise person owes little to luck, because reason secures the greatest goods and keeps them available for a lifetime.
66d26e45e76683d7: The just person is the least troubled of all, while the unjust one lives in constant anxiety.
6f836959bf632a16: Once the pain of lack has been removed, bodily pleasure cannot be increased, only varied.
088d6e9699544395: The deepest peace of mind comes from thinking clearly about the things that frighten us most.
4c57395793c507ae: Measured by reason rather than duration, a finite life holds as much pleasure as an endless one.
c6cf5d2c7bc00750: Only if the body could feel unlimited pleasure would eternity be worth wanting.
3eb0d99dca64ea0d: Understanding that the body ends releases us from fear of eternity and makes a finite life complete, so that a person in this state can leave life without loss.
1a883c67ba5067c8: Whoever knows the limits of life sees that what ends want and completes life is easily obtained, so nothing hard-won is needed.
091ab8cb10ebb496: We must hold our conception of life's ultimate goal to the same standard of evidence we use for everything else, or all judgment dissolves into confusion.
8f85afe6943d04ad: Reject the senses altogether and you destroy the only standard by which you could have judged them false.
7304dee145bacdb1: Dismissing one sense without distinguishing raw sensation from the inferences built on it corrupts the others and leaves no criterion of truth at all.
19dac49d14a17ffa: Treating unverified inferences as equal in authority to immediate certainties guarantees error, because it mixes doubtful opinion with reliable knowledge.
9c62d2c1496ff627: If we do not measure each action against life's ultimate aim, our conduct will contradict what we claim to believe.
d42aab1c24a84f45: Of everything wisdom provides for a happy life, friendship is by far the most important.
ca47d097601e571a: Understanding life's limits shows that no evil lasts long and that friendship is the surest protection available within a lifetime.
6f68c6c643055a58: Desires divide into those that are natural and necessary, those natural but unnecessary, and those rooted in nothing but vain opinion.
6fab1689cb62532b: A desire whose frustration causes no pain is unnecessary, and can be silenced whenever satisfying it would be difficult or harmful.
273d7ce5d995345f: When a desire is violent even though frustrating it would cause no pain, its intensity comes from illusion rather than from nature.
c7cda52237f61d80: Justice is a mutual agreement not to harm and not to be harmed.
566e90320b112bf3: Beings and groups incapable of making such an agreement have neither justice nor injustice.
857f666a8cc450dc: Justice has no independent existence; it arises wherever people actually agree not to harm one another.
39f413a08a4a7441: Wrongdoing is not bad in itself, but only because of the fear of being caught and punished.
853fbc92d641cb28: Someone who secretly breaks the social agreement can never be confident of escaping detection, no matter how often he has escaped before.
2c59febec909656c: Justice is everywhere the same in principle, since mutual society is advantageous, but its particular content varies with circumstances.
efee5994f7df8cba: A law becomes genuinely just the moment it proves useful to people's dealings with one another, whether or not everyone recognizes this.
c99f6e09a1419ac5: A law that does not serve mutual advantage is not just, and one that stops being useful stops being just, though it was truly just while it served.
ce8c20ce217d6fa6: A rule that conflicts with reason was never really just, and a rule that circumstances render useless ceases to be just at that moment.
8995b154e445cb01: To live free of fear of others, make friends where you can, avoid making enemies where you cannot, and keep your distance from those you can do neither with.
77be91bb6422f308: The happiest people are those with nothing to fear from anyone near them; they live in mutual trust and full friendship, and do not grieve a friend's early death as a calamity.
57314a969d59a447: Some things operate on a scale so vast that ordinary experience provides no measure for them.
b4e561d5d00d4272: Perspective is relative: what looks immense from below looks trivial from far enough away, and nothing can be supported by a medium too shallow for it.
ad11894eb1b2a132: Great undertakings require correspondingly great conditions, and small creatures, judging by their own capacities, find such undertakings absurd.
fe068bb93b7d9cb2: Preparation must match the scale of the journey, and small knowledge cannot encompass great knowledge any more than a short life can imagine a long one.
d55d0ef2834784ac: Every span of life seems long or short only relative to the observer, and even the longest is envied by those with less.
3e67a369cdfa0ee0: Some beings exist on a scale to which ordinary comparisons simply do not apply.
28ca3f1c1404c9df: The small mock what they cannot conceive, judging the purposes of others by the limits of their own reach.
cb2d8c464d02553e: Someone who fills a small role well takes his own narrow scale as the measure of everything, just as the quail measures the great bird by its own short flight.
1198cdf226966c87: A person is admirable when the world's praise cannot inflate him nor its blame shake him, because he can tell what is intrinsically his from what is merely conferred; but even this falls short of true freedom.
378fdfb73d11b900: Even extraordinary powers leave a person dependent on something outside himself.
2cf621ee0ef21d4e: Complete freedom would depend on nothing at all, and belongs to the person who has let go of self, of striving, and of reputation.
47d7e6276de07f99: Once a person passes into that condition, worldly joys and sorrows lose their hold on him.
78edf0b02e3a18a3: It is wasted effort to keep doing what someone else could do better, so one should yield the task to them.
6a1e404e4d5ff010: Reputation is only the shadow of the reality, and a person needs no more than what his nature actually requires, so a role he has no use for is worth nothing to him.
576e6692f05a02ca: People dismiss as nonsense whatever falls outside their own experience.
81dce393f73c4efc: Descriptions of a life freed from ordinary needs strike the ordinary listener as unbelievable.
95fe1145e2e7ba55: There is a blindness of the mind as well as of the body, and those who suffer from it dismiss what is greatest and would drag it down to trivial work.
21fe01d49287621d: What is merely incidental to the greatest people is the entire substance of ordinary ones.
8f499cb290ac4759: What is precious in one setting is useless in another, and whoever sees through the worth of worldly possessions ceases to want them.
f7540f484b6be8fe: Once a person has let go of himself, he has nothing left to gain from the world.
16cb8fd716b299b3: When something seems too large to be useful, the failure lies in the user's imagination rather than in the thing.
08b57c324b14bc27: People who use a thing only in the way they inherited will trade away its greater value for a small immediate gain.
8e3004856cd451a1: The same thing yields a trivial or an enormous return depending entirely on how it is applied.
6df607ef860bacde: What conventional standards call worthless may simply be waiting for an unconventional use.
85f1d7c255404be0: Something dismissed as big and useless may only lack a purpose its critics are able to imagine.
6841e97d6f79b019: The very usefulness that makes a thing valuable exposes it to danger, while apparent uselessness preserves it.
d805439e06173cf3: Someone who seeks power for personal gain may gain security but loses freedom; a wild bird works hard for its food yet would not trade that hardship for a cage.
3b7c75694281f60f: A truly wise person's death should not provoke excessive grief, and a teacher who cultivates such attachment in followers has failed them.
7570dade20c6a003: Stirring up strong emotional attachment in others violates nature and burdens them with sorrow. Birth and death arrive in their proper time, so there is nothing to mourn.
20765a50d9762101: The individual body burns away, but what it carries forward passes on and does not end.
73f4912b5e953aa9: A self that is nourished rather than exhausted in worldly striving can outlast the body and return to its source.
0cd577e39ba58271: When a state is badly governed, that is precisely where a capable person should go, since a well-governed one needs no help.
be408032bc272795: Wisdom cannot be handed out to others without being fragmented and losing its coherence. Perfect yourself before attempting to correct anyone else.
455e24a525819bf9: Virtue tends to curdle into a hunger for reputation, and cleverness into competition; both are dangerous tools.
524b60cc380f43e4: Preaching morality to bad people before you have demonstrated it yourself only makes them hate you for your goodness. Those who denounce others invite denunciation in return.
2acbd6c5756c65dd: If you confront a powerful person with moral instruction, they will simply out-argue and overpower you, and your resolve will collapse. Fighting force with force only escalates it.
d320d5a5ec125182: History shows that those who cultivate virtue to help ordinary people are destroyed by rulers who feel shown up by them.
30b6d36c7ba9e610: If the greatest people in history could not defeat the human appetite for fame and victory, an ordinary person has no chance of doing so.
c3597936bb29a972: Displaying your own moral perfection to someone in power only makes them uneasy and defensive; a person who has failed at ordinary decency will not be won over to higher virtue by argument.
8627982eab7b4ad1: Someone who is inwardly aligned with the natural order sees themselves and the most powerful ruler as equals under it, and so is indifferent to praise or harm from people.
5637c7572eafce18: Outwardly conforming to convention protects you from blame, and speaking only in the words of accepted authority means the responsibility for the message is not yours.
9236f318e45d4756: Elaborate strategies may keep you safe, but they will never move someone to change from their own heart. The methods that actually work are the difficult ones, not the easy ones.
1ce1eb697945c3b4: Outward austerity is not the same as inner discipline; what matters is fasting of the mind, not of the body.
fefe26ac4366185b: Stop listening with the ears and thinking with the mind; let awareness become empty and merely responsive, and truth can occupy that emptiness. This emptiness means the disappearance of the separate self.
a030392a7c8e40a3: Real influence comes from doing nothing and pretending nothing — an achievement as strange as walking without touching the ground, or being wise without any wisdom.
51b94ecdc171a585: A room is bright only because it is empty; let your senses carry the world inward but keep the mind unfurnished by cleverness.
4dcf94d2d34a86f8: Emptiness is what makes room for everything else to arrive, and passive rather than active virtue is what actually transforms the world.
5f2f0ff05da272df: Any important undertaking threatens you twice: failure brings punishment from others, and success brings the inner cost of having strained for it.
802d16380176819d: Only someone genuinely indifferent to their own reward or punishment escapes suffering whether they succeed or fail.
5c8963b0aa1eb926: Anxiety about an outcome burns you before you have even begun, so that you are punished from within before the world can punish you from without.
38a62e4d75a61209: When trapped between inner and outer pressure, ask what can actually be done rather than simply enduring.
419a5070e45f0498: Some obligations cannot be escaped — love of one's parents is inborn and duty to one's society is inescapable — so the way through is to serve without conditions rather than to negotiate the terms.
f62a8b3c2b782205: The highest achievement is to govern your own heart so that neither joy nor sorrow disturbs it, and to accept what cannot be changed.
52b333a4d1637372: If you absorb yourself entirely in the work and forget yourself, you have no attention left over for clinging to life or dreading death.
b865a5f40fd2157b: Messages get exaggerated in transmission — goodwill inflated, ill will sharpened — and the exaggeration destroys credibility and endangers the messenger.
f39e7d1ac5d8b0e2: Stick to plain statements of fact and leave out surplus feeling, and you reduce your risk. Everything pushed far enough turns into its opposite: contests become hostility, celebrations become disorder, trust becomes contempt.
b59a8cdd354da651: Words stir emotion the way wind stirs water; once anger rises, dishonest arguments follow and both sides lose themselves. Corner someone hard enough and they will resist for reasons neither of you can name.
177b81c375225974: Neither fall short of nor exceed what you were sent to do, since a bad settlement, once made, cannot be undone. Let yourself be carried along without grasping at either side.
1fb91934b3043564: Restraining a powerful person with bad instincts endangers you, while letting them run free endangers everyone; the difficulty is that they can see everyone's faults but their own.
28f40311e93683bf: People with just enough intelligence to spot others' faults, and not enough to spot their own, are the hardest to correct.
fed2c02f14de43f1: Adapt outwardly to a difficult person while holding your inner standard firm — but never let the outward adaptation seep inward and corrupt you, nor let the inner standard show and provoke them.
cd3aa38a4c171af8: By meeting someone exactly where they are, you can reach them without giving offence.
28a89559e270a2ed: The mantis raising its arms against a chariot has admirable courage and no chance; constantly asserting your superiority over others will destroy you.
c812b126a20cdb4a: Even a tiger is safe if you work with its nature rather than provoke it, and the same is true of people.
9503925b59dcef6d: Good intentions carried out without real attention to what the other actually needs can cause exactly the harm they meant to prevent.
0bb96c8f55dfbcaa: What is most impressive to the untrained eye is often what the expert knows to be worthless.
0403d684fed33e19: The tree survived to enormous age precisely because its wood was useless for every purpose anyone wanted.
4ef45c09b1553058: Fruit trees are stripped and broken because they bear fruit; whatever is valuable is consumed by the world and never completes its natural life. Uselessness, patiently maintained, becomes the greatest usefulness of all.
4ef2c928febb21e5: Someone whose own survival is still uncertain is in no position to judge another's strategy for surviving.
763b64e12f44bace: Becoming sacred was simply the tree's way of escaping the axe; unconventional means of safety cannot be judged by conventional standards.
0577f9d6227af8c7: Great size and long survival often come from being unfit for every use others would put you to.
08e1f2a0e242905b: Qualities that repel others are what keep a thing from being consumed by them.
a5f1b9090d7570b0: A thing grows large by being good for nothing, and a wise person would do well to imitate it.
eeae7437e584ceba: The best trees are cut down first; excellence is exactly what prevents a thing from living out its natural span.
2a8f020027441796: What is judged defective is exempted from sacrifice, so the flaw that condemns you in others' eyes may be the very thing that saves you.
e58bb1f7829f8001: The hunchback's deformity exempted him from conscription and forced labour while still qualifying him for charity; if a bodily defect can preserve a life, a mental or moral one that keeps you out of public affairs can do so even more.
c635d6006a5afef8: When the times are right, capable people can fulfil their calling; when they are wrong, the most they can do is preserve themselves.
f664939fd75e028d: Worldly honours are weightless yet universally overvalued, while misfortunes are enormous yet nobody avoids them. Everyone understands the use of useful things; almost nobody understands the use of being useless.
7f8d1d4ed7966823: A teaching that never needs to be spoken can send people away full.
89a9226c6e15e6ad: A body may be maimed while the mind is whole, and it is the wholeness of the mind that makes a teacher.
947a5f23332d9320: Something in a person is untouched by life and death and would survive the collapse of the world; it changes other things while remaining itself unchanged.
f7396ee82ba9104f: Seen through difference, things divide endlessly; seen through sameness, all things are one. To someone who sees this, the loss of his own feet matters no more than losing a lump of mud.
29a1ce032291009d: Only what is itself still can make other things still, which is why people gather around a settled mind as they look for their reflection in calm rather than running water.
eacb0c29beeacc71: If mere physical courage can eliminate fear enough to face an army alone, how much more can be achieved by someone who knows all things are one. Such a person attracts others without making any effort to.
45fc11422e1f9149: People flock to a person of complete inner virtue; the crowd around someone is only the visible sign of what is inside them.
