a2cb260c090770e8: Rank in the world confers no precedence in learning. A truly bright mirror gathers no dust — if dust settles, the mirror was never bright — and long association with wisdom leaves a person without fault.
16df94647c70996c: Being useless protects a thing from harm, so what is large and apparently useless can be more valuable than what is small and immediately useful.
2ceb85969363618b: A person can let go of the self so completely that body and mind go still, and only in that state can they perceive a level of reality deeper than human or natural sound.
9eb8511a79d31082: A single undifferentiated force produces countless different voices depending only on the shape of whatever it passes through.
443f44f129c0b981: The same stirring force can be observed at work everywhere in the natural world.
a6b0a043adc88bea: Something unnamed gives each thing its own distinct character and its capacity to express that character. Broad understanding takes in the whole, while narrow understanding grasps only a fragment.
0904bd4f67271835: The mind is continually agitated by indecision, concealment, and fear, and from this agitation it darts out to pronounce things right and wrong.
bac6b6f738f3d639: Moods arise and pass through us on their own, and we cannot locate where they come from, let alone what ultimately causes them.
8fc30f0d864db6e8: The self and its emotions depend on each other for their existence, yet what sets them in motion has effects without ever showing a visible form.
04d2e81a93a4c780: The parts of a person do not govern themselves; something unifying directs them all.
2c139a6cde6675d4: To be worn down by ceaseless labor, unable to stop, and then to vanish without ever enjoying the fruit of it, is a genuinely pitiable way to spend a life.
42b0aca1c90aa200: The mind perishes along with the body, and this is the real cause for sorrow. If everyone judges by the standards of their own mind, then even fools have a standard, which shows that private criteria settle nothing.
0d111827b332a30a: To claim to hold no fixed standards while still asserting opposites is a contradiction, like arriving somewhere yesterday on a journey begun today.
47192f420b780284: Words are distinguishable from mere noise only by their meaning. Truth is obscured by our failure to grasp the whole, and speech is obscured by the one-sided conventional senses we attach to words.
ab5a9be5c15f0b3e: Rival schools of thought merely deny what the other affirms, and reconciling them requires dropping fixed criteria and seeing things by natural clarity.
7e058c64a4f50561: Nothing is purely objective or purely subjective; each arises from the other, so that affirming one amounts to denying the other.
7665340edf001673: The wise abandon the distinction between this and that, and instead place themselves in relation to all things as a single whole.
1b355ce33f0dee3d: Since subject and object are ultimately the same thing seen from two sides, it becomes impossible to say that either really exists on its own, and their opposites collapse into each other.
5b836b2c408fcfdc: When opposites no longer have anything to stand against, you occupy the pivot from which all distinctions dissolve into a single infinite unity.
97168194a7c0e4d1: Things are what they are through their own natures, and every name, possibility, and impossibility follows from what a thing is drawn toward and what it excludes.
b2bb45baf2fd878f: Nothing can exist without a particular nature of its own.
9e8d8d33e4778f16: Seen from the standpoint of the whole, all opposites are identical: beauty and ugliness, making and destroying, are the same process viewed differently.
0c2ac5a4c8cf66d1: Real understanding comes from entering the standpoint of the thing observed rather than judging it from your own, and exhausting yourself insisting on the separateness of things is folly.
2c9028459cb1300f: People can be enraged or delighted by identical facts merely rearranged, so the wise accommodate others' preferences without altering what is actually true.
64fd27fb0c43a8f8: The wise treat opposites as one and the same while still going along with the natural order, following both courses at once.
fa4bc32c5b27586a: Distinctions between opposites are a late development, and once they appear, unity is lost and personal bias takes its place.
0eb583ced54aec6f: Rising and falling have no independent existence; they are only side effects of some activity being performed.
76909a29724e06cb: Trying to enlighten people by argument, rather than by the quiet manifestation of one's own clarity, ends in obscurity and establishes nothing.
6f54eda05d9a1aaa: The clarity worth having emerges from stillness, and consists in seeing things from their own position rather than from your own.
91341904a6f508b6: Any claimed beginning implies a time before it, without end, so existence and non-existence cannot be fixed as stable categories.
3f341670be7ac18c: Even a statement that denies all distinctions cannot escape the doubt it casts, including doubt about whether it was said at all.
f92b6f89a4ebe9a5: Great and small, long life and short, are entirely relative, so the tiniest thing may be called the largest. Everything came into being together with me, and all of it is one.
59076a46c7a24109: The moment you speak about unity you have already made it into more than one, and the multiplication never stops; the only escape is to stop standing apart from things.
6cfec6f4c452cde7: Our categories of distinction are conventions that the wise recognize without insisting on or arguing for them.
45478d64c4df72cf: The wise keep their understanding within themselves, while ordinary people set theirs out in argument to convince each other.
8516b069ab35fd88: Anything perfect stops announcing itself: displayed truth, argued speech, calculated kindness, rigid honesty, and unyielding courage all defeat their own purpose. The highest knowledge is knowing where knowledge stops.
2b720f68dbaf51a2: The deepest understanding is inexhaustible, giving without emptying and receiving without filling, and the one who has it cannot explain how.
9efa932c837e97b7: Quiet moral influence reaches further and accomplishes more than force ever can.
c632016cd9ec0e0f: No one can be certain they know anything, and since what kills one creature suits another, no way of living is absolutely correct.
f144eb87c7103ccd: What counts as the right food or true beauty differs entirely by creature, so no standard of either is absolute.
91845d40fc596a3b: Standards of virtue, beauty, and right and wrong are so obscured by perspective that they cannot be known as absolutes.
831ec73d83edb104: Someone who has passed beyond the world's distinctions is untouched by anything that happens within it, including death.
6e78f47de9614893: The wise person seeks no gain and avoids no injury, asks nothing of other people, and can speak without asserting anything at all.
22910b315d358dde: The wise attend to matters early, before they become pressing.
8f4920a2e4bb0835: Do not leap from a premise to the results you expect from it; things need time to develop.
5a45604d35a4b66a: The wise blend everything into one harmonious whole and ignore the rank and precedence that ordinary people prize. Our love of life may itself be a delusion, and our dread of death like a lost child's fear of a home they have simply forgotten.
e769e1be993231bd: We may come to regret having clung to life, just as someone who wept at a change later regrets the weeping; while we are dreaming, we never know that we dream.
3e032bb2d58cc438: This life may itself be a dream from which we have not yet woken, and the person who says so is no more awake than anyone else.
625e7ba1f4b57c15: Winning an argument does not establish that you were right.
e975c917f474ae25: No third party can settle a dispute, since any arbiter either shares one side's view, shares neither, or shares both, and in every case cannot decide.
88b29861f32460b5: If a thing genuinely is what it is, it differs from its opposite in a way no argument can touch; disputes only arise where that genuineness is absent.
3e147fa3ad986dec: Stop attending to time and to right and wrong, and rest instead in the boundless, where opposites exist without antagonism.
ee3e5adbc8eb38dd: Because consciousness always divides the knower from the known, the ultimate reality in which the two are united cannot be known.
35262696d85cfc25: Everything that acts does so because of something it depends on, which depends on something else in turn, so no one can say why they do what they do.
cc1afab99e199caa: After being fully absorbed in another form of existence, one cannot be sure which state was the waking one and which the dream.
bb535a8f4456a8df: Chasing limitless knowledge with a limited life is exhausting and ruinous; take a middle course, avoid both fame and disgrace, and you will preserve your health and live out your span.
cf9ecd0fb293636b: Mastery comes from working along the natural structure of a thing rather than forcing through it, guided by understanding rather than the senses.
85a9f14f986c5e06: Do not force your way through what most strongly resists you.
c335b7fd18951d6b: Those who find the natural gaps in a thing never wear themselves out, while those who hack through it are used up quickly.
d4e6f5693aa66e59: Even a master slows down, concentrates, and works with caution wherever a genuine difficulty appears.
e32c26288e850cd5: A person's unusual condition is the work of nature rather than the doing of other people.
ed6085543557fe75: Criticizing someone else's words may reveal a fault in the critic rather than the speaker.
0c34800cbfa4e89d: Most people hide their faults to avoid punishment, and few accept exposure; accepting what cannot be changed is itself the achievement of virtue.
e20430cb9774b4f0: Mockery from others only wounds you while you still identify with what they mock; sustained inner work makes the insult irrelevant.
071a27a70f52fb9a: A person who comes seeking growth should be met by what is universal in you, not judged by the damage already done to them.
39c3ea835971ba37: If someone burdened by past wrongdoing works this hard to improve, those without such burdens have far less excuse for neglecting it.
86206ccf20f6b2b9: Reputation for cleverness in argument is itself a kind of bondage, and a person bound by it usually cannot be freed by others.
84f07b7415769c63: A person with no power, office, beauty, or teaching to offer can still draw others irresistibly, because what attracts them is inner character rather than anything visible.
5b90a91ba06edb6c: Real attachment forms around a person's inner quality, so their absence is felt as a loss no status or benefit could have produced.
3e6af2fc3e2c7edf: What we love in a person is not their body but the animating quality within it, which is why the body alone commands no attachment once that quality is gone.
cb146449568a2e4e: Things are valued only for the function they serve; once the function is gone the thing is worthless. Anyone serious about inner character will therefore guard it as carefully as others guard their bodies or possessions.
ab890076a350350f: The highest capacity can exist wholly without any outward mark of it.
7eb612c89accc8fa: Life and death, success and failure, wealth and poverty, praise and blame all turn by a logic outside our control, so a complete person refuses to let them disturb inner harmony while still moving easily among others.
80d56a757f37a148: Remaining undisturbed by circumstance is what leaves a person prepared for anything.
94ceb0a38f6106e3: Character should be like still water: perfectly level, contained, not spilling outward; and precisely because it makes no display, others cannot help being drawn to it and evened by it.
9ffefb410366ce97: A ruler who thinks administering people's welfare fulfills his duty has mistaken the task; without inner cultivation he may be wearing himself out while damaging what he governs.
8e0cabd74d3f8044: When inner quality is compelling, physical deformity ceases to register at all. Yet people habitually cling to the body, which should be forgotten, and forget character, which should not.
29fa9db87c14d005: The truly wise need no cleverness because they make no schemes, no loyalty-bonds because they never divide, no virtue because they want nothing, and no shrewdness because they sell nothing. They look human and mix with humans, but nothing in them takes sides for or against.
4870eba22f205bcd: To be without passions does not mean feeling nothing; it means not letting judgments of good and bad disturb your inner workings, accepting what happens, and not adding self-inflicted harm to the harm nature already deals.
03001ba137375723: Spending your intelligence on external puzzles wears out the very faculties that make a life whole.
e5294cb0e37c9a14: Nature has given you a whole living being, and you spend it on abstract quibbles.
bda9360017bb0601: Complete understanding means knowing both what lies beyond you and what belongs to you: resting in what you actually know, waiting on what you don't, and living out your full span.
a8b34595147016f5: All knowing depends on an outcome we never reach, so we can never be sure which part of us is merely human and which is more; genuine knowledge requires first becoming a genuine person.
d8788b3f2c2cddf1: Those who act without calculating outcomes have no regret in failure and no self-congratulation in success, and this freedom lets them pass through danger untouched.
93038b7bfea549f8: Those at peace sleep without dreams and wake without anxiety, taking food without fuss and drawing breath from their whole being rather than shallowly from the throat.
fd67d9c3ea6fee9d: Techniques for prolonging life tend to degrade into superstitious tricks.
87de0204d2c4375a: Crooked people produce speech that comes out like vomit; the deeper someone's cravings run, the shallower their capacity for anything higher.
1193af2334df839d: Those at peace neither cling to life nor dread death, arriving and departing without resistance and letting nothing human be forced onto what is not ours to control.
7d9515c0807b650b: A complete person's feelings arrive and pass like the seasons, in their own time. Such a person can destroy a kingdom without losing the people's hearts and benefit ten thousand generations without acting out of love, because everything they do is spontaneous and therefore right.
77bea8992f73640f: Whoever takes special delight in particular people falls short of universal care, and whoever needs the right opportunity to act well has no real worth, since true worth operates unprompted.
b56697b90da258f1: Anyone still concerned with his own reputation or unable to forget his own existence is unfit to lead others, and those celebrated for high-minded refusal were in fact still serving other people's terms.
4efc3abb3901a1ec: Extreme gestures of purity and withdrawal, however admired, are still lives lived on someone else's terms.
c61e1573b18e207f: Those at peace fulfill their obligations without merging into the crowd, seem deficient without flattering anyone, hold their shape without rigidity, and are independent without extremism. Their outward charm is simply the overflow of what has been built inside.
bbc8913564cb6972: The wise treat law, custom, knowledge, and morality as practical instruments for moving through the world, not as ends deserving reverence — which is why everyone praises them without noticing why.
ed756ea97e4a6729: For such a person what they embrace and what they reject are equally part of one whole, so no conflict arises between their nature and their circumstance.
1bec9663196907e0: Life and death follow one another as inevitably as day and night, beyond any human power to interfere.
8871e936d17a93df: If people revere what stands above them, they should revere still more the underlying principle from which even that derives.
7b8b45e3c5f9a461: Rescuing what has already gone wrong is far inferior to arranging things so the trouble never arises; likewise, arguing about who was good and who was bad matters less than attending to the underlying way.
fd839fc4efa0007c: Whatever created your life and carries you through youth, age, and death is a trustworthy guide in dying as much as in living.
bac281d7512af3ef: Anything hidden inside something larger can still be stolen; only what is identified with the whole has nowhere left to be lost from.
01f2c7bd2e412324: To have received a human form is joy enough, and to face endless further transformation into the infinite is greater joy still — so the wise delight only in what cannot be lost.
4379e72cd3ff7bab: If we can accept early death, old age, beginnings, and endings as given, we can also accept the underlying principle from which all phenomena ultimately follow.
dd7680c272f5b861: The underlying principle of things existed before heaven and earth, acts without acting, has no form, cannot be handed over or seen, and is untouched by height, depth, or the passage of ages.
606b1d63a05970bf: Everything that endures and functions well — the stars, the seasons, the mountains, wise rulers — does so by conforming to this same underlying principle.
5df09d44f74c6d21: Those who align with the underlying principle become fixed points others navigate by.
e31c70944582a83b: Alignment with the underlying way cannot be transmitted by instruction, and the qualifications that make someone look like a sage are neither necessary nor sufficient for it.
82d5a066ab9af713: Learning of this kind proceeds by withholding rather than adding: first the social world of rank and judgment falls away, then the external world, then awareness of oneself.
0e1262586ae23f29: At the end of that stripping away one arrives where life and death no longer differ — where killing takes nothing and longevity adds nothing.
8310b91ce9c7f1c3: Such a person adapts naturally to whatever their situation demands and stays among ordinary people; withdrawal into solitude is not required. They can be battered without being broken.
a9144de0904c8011: Every chain of learning traces back through books, study, investigation, practice, and the desire to know, until it terminates in what is unknown and finally in the infinite.
f8bffff96efa7505: Those who can make inaction the head, life the spine, and death the tail of their existence recognize one another without needing to explain.
b271532d443b6b8a: The body can be wrecked and deranged while the mind's equilibrium remains wholly undisturbed.
b4ed9c04fd65ed99: I received life when it was time and I release it by the same law, so neither joy nor sorrow can touch me; the only real bondage was ever my attachment to material existence.
f1da83176d02b56c: Humans have always yielded to what is greater than themselves, so there is nothing left to fear.
4b77912930f7ed78: Grieving loudly around the dying only obstructs the natural transformation they are undergoing.
67bf28d3d33c7f1d: Nature stands to us as a parent does to a child; to refuse her summons is a kind of ingratitude, and she cannot wrong us.
ae444b4221d15db5: Demanding to be made into some particular thing is as absurd as molten metal insisting on its own shape; go where you are sent, and death is a return to dreamless sleep.
32ee529e648686e8: People who share the same freedom from fear about existence and nonexistence recognize each other immediately.
971c814baf779069: Real propriety is inward, not the outward observance of forms, so those who have it may violate ritual entirely without impropriety.
8cd2ffcaa215257f: Some people live by the rules of ordinary life and some travel outside them; the latter regard life as a swelling that death lances, admit no beginning or end, and take their stand on the unity of everything.
fc2ffd2773bb1355: Those who have stepped outside conventional life ignore their cravings, discount the evidence of their senses, and are indifferent to what anyone thinks of them.
57718b99960fe14b: Just as a fish thrives only in water, a person is at peace only when living in accordance with the natural way of things, regardless of outward ceremony.
2d3f6b323b687a5f: What looks extraordinary by human standards is ordinary from a cosmic perspective, and the two scales invert each other: the least by one measure is the greatest by the other.
c2b6a2f9ac35b797: Someone can perform mourning's outward forms without inward grief and still be the truest mourner, because real wisdom has moved past the need for emotional display.
14bc4543bc5d166c: A wise person does not know where we come from, where we go, or when the end comes, and so simply awaits the change without anxiety. Life and death may be no more knowable than a dream we have not yet woken from.
b7e29fcc92b31ff8: Conform outwardly to custom while keeping your inner self undisturbed, treating death as a homecoming and weeping with others naturally rather than from grief. You cannot even verify that you are what you take yourself to be, since a dream feels as real from inside as waking does.
b38ae0abdca44cc3: Genuine feeling expresses itself spontaneously rather than on cue, and a person who accepts their circumstances and stops tracking every change merges with the undivided whole.
4177ddab03bcb27c: A person conditioned by rigid categories of right and wrong is thereby disabled from perceiving what lies beyond them, the way damaged eyes cannot judge beauty or color.
47c3adf24e1802f1: Conditioning can be unlearned, and people who abandoned their conspicuous gifts did so through deliberate reshaping. The source that sustains all things does so without counting it as duty, charity, skill, or age.
5f6b1d51b21a5622: The source that covers heaven, supports earth, and gives everything its form takes no credit for the doing.
160d27eb30eb59cf: Progress toward wisdom means first shedding moral rules and then shedding cultural forms, but neither step is the final one.
bee0d5aa2b30847c: The last step is letting go of body and intellect entirely and becoming one with the infinite, after which there is no partiality and no beginning or end.
8234fe9f1b0b9c23: Hardship comes to everyone in its turn, and a friend's response is to notice and go to them.
0e2bdbad400cf872: When misfortune can be traced to no one — not your parents, not heaven, not earth, all of which treat everyone alike — it can only be attributed to impersonal fate.
a3b01657dbef92f8: The laws of the world are indifferent to us and often crush us rather than help us, and that indifference is what people mean by fate.
ed1a9c06bf0ef702: Discovering the limits of your knowledge is itself a form of knowing. A ruler who strains to be good remains trapped in artifice, while one who governs without self-conscious effort does not.
5a0797abfd435c7a: A person free of self-consciousness holds no fixed idea of what he is.
f233e3d8fd96118e: When every channel to self-consciousness is closed, a person's wisdom and virtue become genuine rather than performed.
e43f324c31be3d36: Trying to govern people through imposed laws and regulations is as futile as hewing a passage through a river; the wise ruler perfects himself first and accomplishes what he wants without effort. Even animals instinctively evade the traps set for them, so people will evade coercion too.
f53ce7fc3883a125: Letting people become good of their own accord works better than coercing them into goodness.
cedb3989ff96f1e9: Empty the mind, stop striving, and fall in with the natural order without inserting yourself into it, and things will order themselves.
177b59e021f9b09b: A ruler who wears himself out with courage and cleverness is a mere craftsman, since conspicuous ability is exactly what gets a creature trapped and killed.
c3e0811ff5635ac5: The goodness of a wise ruler reaches everything and brings joy to everything while remaining unnoticed, resting on no foundation and located nowhere.
190c7182f7282d8a: Real governance operates invisibly. People are easily dazzled by someone who can predict outcomes precisely, and mistake that power for something deeper than it is.
d3b03179b2bc6d5b: Learning a teaching's outward ornaments is not the same as grasping its essence, and forcing it on others only exposes how little you have.
7e0b1b2d1e5d6bb1: A person who shows only stillness looks lifeless to an observer who cannot see the generative energy held within.
9d98d448bb00567a: What an observer reads in you is only what you allow to show.
b17308c283d449af: The same depth takes many forms — stillness, motion, equilibrium — and no single appearance is the whole of it.
ce456166c473f0f9: Confronted with something formless and prior to all distinctions, someone who reads appearances has nothing to read and flees in confusion. Real learning began only when the student dropped his pretensions and returned to plain, ordinary life.
5bda5da44f0e574d: It is possible to remain unconfounded in the midst of confusion.
3b04377fc62c8065: Through non-action a person becomes the center of others' thought and the arbiter of wisdom. Make full allowance for others while remaining unmoved yourself, and comply with underlying principles without ever displaying that you do.
bc904c96a82e016d: The ideal mind works like a mirror: it grasps nothing, refuses nothing, and keeps nothing, so it can meet the world without being worn down by it.
b19b1d983662a2fc: Well-meant improvement imposed on something whose nature is to be undivided destroys it, which is why whoever rules by force ruins what he holds.
f335acbc24701b62: People's actions habitually outrun their ability to control them.
ac4966265268cddb: Framing a state's deliberations around profit is a mistake; benevolence and justice are the only proper terms.
50d811a3ca8cce49: When everyone from ruler to commoner asks what will profit themselves, they end up preying on one another and the state is endangered.
4f13e36bf55f2b30: Once gain is placed above justice, no share is ever large enough to satisfy people short of taking everything.
d1ec03293d033a24: People trained in benevolence never neglect their parents, and people trained in justice never treat their ruler as an afterthought. Whether a ruler can take pleasure in what he owns depends on whether he is good.
4140c83132c02e5e: A ruler who lacks virtue takes no pleasure in his possessions even while owning them.
fcd918e57ecb682c: When a ruler's project is genuinely for the common good, people take it up willingly and finish it quickly, as children do work for a parent.
35fb3a84252a32eb: A ruler who shares his pleasures with the people can actually enjoy them, while one who hoards them is hated so bitterly that people would accept their own death to see him gone.
dde1f61d2358ba04: Emergency relief during famine does not win a people's loyalty if the underlying conditions of their lives are unchanged.
3531137ec216a1f7: A lesser degree of the same fault is still that fault, so a partial improvement gives no ground to look down on the worse case.
54e1ddb1bd2b6bad: Since your conduct differs from your neighbors' only in degree, you have no reason to expect a different result from theirs.
5cffa50c17d8322b: If people can farm in season and resources are harvested with restraint, there will be more than enough to feed the living and bury the dead without resentment.
e53e49a1dc6e1214: Meeting the people's basic needs for living and dying is only the first step of good government, not its completion.
766c1bff682d410e: Secure land, tended livestock, farming time left uninterrupted, and schooling in family duty let ordinary people live in comfort and grow old with dignity.
f5cc17ba5a4251b8: No ruler who has actually secured his people's comfort has ever failed to attain supreme authority.
c4156a0679eea91e: Letting animals eat while people starve on the roads, then blaming the harvest, is like stabbing someone and blaming the knife; stop shifting the blame and people everywhere will come to you.
728fff46113ce203: Governing so that your beasts are fat while your people starve is a way of killing them, no different from using a sword, and it forfeits any claim to be the people's parent.
68fc22e6de611edd: If merely making human images to bury with the dead deserved condemnation, far worse is a ruler whose policies actually starve people to death.
16764cd57fdb2e23: Rulers who have suffered military defeat and lost territory are driven to wipe out the humiliation by force.
2cb4c9c223725b91: Even a very small territory is sufficient ground from which to win supreme authority.
03fc9f26b881fe93: Light punishments and taxes, time left free for cultivation, and education in family and civic duty produce a people who will fight for their ruler with sticks against armored troops.
73e8be5942e8c600: Rulers who rob their people of working time and let their families starve and scatter leave themselves defenseless, which is why a benevolent ruler has no enemy.
7a065c5db4d2f9fb: The world can only be brought to peace by being unified, and it can only be unified by someone who takes no pleasure in killing.
ce1a28fc7c9c62ef: If a single ruler among many refused to delight in killing, people everywhere would turn to him the way parched grain springs up when the rains finally come.
acad1f173ae9e9e5: People move toward a humane ruler as irresistibly as water rushes downhill, and no one can hold them back.
bb634ad0787a0b65: Lasting authority comes not from studying the exploits of conquerors but from loving and protecting the people.
07a413f667a26907: A person who doubts his own capacity to govern well may already possess the compassion that governing well requires.
49f5c8b039cd02bd: Sparing a frightened animal from slaughter reveals a compassion that could just as well be extended to human beings.
da99584c209dc3bf: Sparing a creature because you cannot bear to watch its fear is an act of compassion, even when onlookers read it as stinginess.
17b2c156a1888b0a: Compassion that spares one victim while substituting another is inconsistent, and outsiders are right to notice the inconsistency.
ed28f51c15cbeb80: Pity is triggered by what we actually witness, not by what we know abstractly, which is why people arrange their lives to stay at a distance from the suffering their comforts require.
f89480926355fa62: People often act from motives they cannot identify in themselves until someone else names them, and a claim to manage the hard thing but not the easy one is incoherent on its face.
6741c43749b32725: When someone displays a capacity in one case and not in another, the gap is a failure to use the capacity, not an absence of it.
19ba091e99015ced: Falling short of what you are capable of is a matter of unwillingness, not inability.
9c62198890fbbe6e: There is a real difference between what is genuinely impossible and what is merely unchosen, and most claimed impossibilities belong to the second kind.
b0b29ee6056335ed: The task you say you cannot do is usually the easy kind, refused rather than beyond you.
4083fc49da3a55de: Extend the respect and care you give your own family outward to everyone else's, and governing becomes effortless.
3445f3e1e9f7b3f2: What made the great figures of the past exceptional was simply that they carried their existing kindness outward to others; a ruler who does that protects an entire realm, and one who does not cannot even protect his own household.
9b90c644afd73756: We measure objects carefully before judging them, yet almost never examine our own minds, which is precisely where measurement is most needed.
c2bf5905d356d545: When someone's ambition cannot be explained by any ordinary appetite, the real desire is something larger that they have not admitted.
a44b0f9e7cc6aac6: Pursuing a goal by means that cannot possibly produce it is futile.
674bf75bfe7f56fa: Using the wrong means toward a harmless end merely fails; using the wrong means toward a dangerous end brings ruin.
635a537d517eb178: The weak cannot defeat the strong or the few the many, so a strategy that depends on doing so is doomed.
25fd6fefd41020c7: When your method guarantees defeat, the fault lies in the method rather than the desire, and you must return to the approach that can actually reach it.
65d64dbee739637e: A government that treats people well draws people to it from everywhere, and no rival force can hold them back.
8566dfd7df2ec6af: Only those trained in reflection can keep their moral bearings without material security; ordinary people without a stable livelihood will eventually do anything.
ed7c321861364cc4: Punishing people for crimes their deprivation forced upon them is a trap laid by the ruler, not justice.
71f3be7e278e9080: Secure people's material needs first, so that they can support their parents and children in good years and survive the bad ones; only then will moral instruction take hold.
2c9a189459e82d1e: People consumed by the struggle to survive have no capacity left over for virtue.
9aa27252d6fa6017: Humane government begins with concrete provision: land, time to work it, and the means for the old and the young to be fed and clothed.
301a8ff3e44b3941: Where the elderly are cared for and no one goes hungry or cold, a ruler's authority follows without being seized.
8fd011f70929b0b2: Judge deliberately, teach widely, and build protective margins around the practices that matter, so they are never breached by accident.
eee35ce2e5aa91b6: The world rests on three things: learning, devotion, and acts of kindness.
32b6e8fa63c7de40: Serve without working for the reward.
7fb974b0db4b87d2: Surround yourself with people wiser than you and absorb their words with real hunger.
399957d775d3fad9: Keep your home open and let the poor belong there, and do not squander in idle talk what should go to serious things.
18f60a981a4e7ec7: Find yourself a teacher and a companion, and judge every person as generously as the facts allow.
794253a3e564285a: Keep away from bad company, and never abandon the conviction that actions carry consequences.
8ab6329f87d84164: A judge must not advocate for either side, should hold both parties suspect while the case is open, and should regard both as honest once they have accepted the verdict.
2349a06717373b2b: Examine witnesses closely, but guard your own phrasing, since a careless question teaches the witness what lie to tell.
58ffdec7777c88e3: Love your work, refuse authority over others, and keep your distance from political power.
46795e0cf5392434: Teachers must be careful what they say, because students absorb their errors wholesale and carry them onward.
82a6764cbc367ecf: Love peace, pursue it actively, and draw people toward what is good. Knowledge that is not growing is shrinking; you must act on your own behalf, yet living only for yourself amounts to nothing, and the time to begin is now.
c9d4963be8469760: Set a fixed time for study, promise little and do much, and meet everyone with a cheerful face.
a376c973f44a0cdf: Get yourself a teacher, resolve your uncertainties rather than living with them, and never estimate where precision is owed.
6d9fb4d155a52aa3: Silence serves a person better than speech, because what finally counts is action rather than study, and multiplying words multiplies wrongdoing.
b5bfe18675df95d1: The world is held together by truth, justice, and peace.
d315109f19c8e1bc: Choose the path that you can take pride in and that others also honor. Treat trivial obligations as seriously as weighty ones, since you cannot know what any of them is worth, and remember that everything you do is seen and recorded.
085a4cd0682ae629: Study combined with ordinary work keeps a person from wrongdoing, while study without work eventually collapses into it. Be wary of the powerful, who approach you only when it serves them.
bacc786b6652558a: The powerful act like friends while it profits them and vanish the moment you need them.
e60f2efe8c7835e4: Stay bound to your community, never assume you are beyond failure while you still live, and withhold judgment on anyone until you have stood where they stand. Speak so you are understood at once, do not defer your work to a leisure that may never arrive, and where no one else is behaving decently, be the one who does.
3810f2edda3c6dcd: Violence returns upon those who commit it, and then upon those who avenge it in turn. Every kind of accumulation brings a matching burden, while learning, counsel, and generosity bring life, understanding, and peace.
0c7d736d9538d45e: A good reputation serves you in this life, while what you have genuinely learned serves you past it.
f72f4f17c7295f00: Take no credit for fulfilling the purpose you were made for.
663063c29375fcdc: Minds are gifted in different ways, some retaining everything they receive and others generating insight endlessly, and both are worth honoring.
a4435b98c83b2e2a: A single exceptional mind can outweigh a great many capable ones. Of all the qualities worth cultivating, a good heart is the one that contains the rest.
1593a8b6508aa516: A bad heart is the flaw beneath every other flaw, and borrowing without repaying is theft whether the lender is a person or God.
86d86f02062cf333: Every particular vice traces back to a corrupted heart.
c730ec3bfd4ebe4b: Hold another person's dignity as precious as your own, resist anger, and repent today, since you cannot know which day is your last. Draw close to the wise but respect their capacity to burn you, and know that envy, appetite, and hatred destroy a person.
fa180f16008cef68: Treat another's property as carefully as your own, and earn your learning rather than assuming it will be handed down to you. Pray as a genuine plea rather than a mechanical duty, and never write yourself off as beyond redemption.
3ce87a6f0acfe8ee: Know who you are working for and what the work is finally worth.
8cd65947a9987427: The work is vast, the time short, and the demand pressing. You are not required to finish it, but neither are you free to walk away from it.
b46363d0cab16963: An abundance of obligations is a gift rather than a burden, because each one is another chance to act well.
141604d245a13688: Keeping in view where you came from, where you are going, and to whom you must finally answer will hold you back from wrongdoing.
4dab6d357a8382e4: Even an imperfect government deserves support, because without the order it enforces people would devour one another.
6e79e72d0afb752a: Time spent together with nothing of substance said is time wasted, while serious conversation makes the gathering itself sacred, and a person studying alone is credited no less than two who study together.
4e1a788760a4f924: Solitary study is worth as much as study undertaken in company.
4952eb6132b763f7: A meal shared with nothing meaningful said is an empty rite; the same meal with real talk becomes a sacred one.
bdbe19abc0c455e6: Filling your solitary hours with empty thoughts wastes your life.
38741b2fa71a0190: Taking on the discipline of serious study frees a person from the burdens of politics and livelihood, while shedding that discipline leaves them at the mercy of both.
559a619a98378cb3: A sacred presence gathers wherever people study together, and the number of them does not matter.
757040c81b2494e2: Wherever a person turns their attention toward the divine, they will meet it there.
e6d81d09b1040572: What you give away was never yours to begin with; you are only returning what was lent to you.
1a9d81516d53e4cc: Breaking off from what matters to admire a passing distraction squanders your life.
2c9a4466804a30c1: Deliberately letting go of what you have learned is a real failing, but forgetting what was always beyond you is not.
5fb5fe7cc6e031c5: Knowledge lasts only when moral seriousness comes before it and action outweighs it. Anyone whom other people cannot bear is not truly good.
45bd324dc63508fa: Oversleeping, drinking through the day, idle chatter, and time spent among the willfully ignorant consume a life.
cec5c8fd5d555845: Contempt for what is sacred, humiliating others in public, and twisting a teaching into what it does not mean cancel out whatever learning and good deeds a person has to their name.
3c039ec45641bfe2: Defer to those above you, be gracious to those below you, and meet everyone with warmth.
cc4c5d667c83f9bf: Everything is known in advance and yet people still choose freely, and they are judged mercifully but strictly according to what they actually did. Life is a loan extended to everyone, and the debt is collected whether or not you are ready to pay.
a44195bc9fd19f5b: Learning and decency, wisdom and reverence, knowledge and understanding, food and study each depend on the other and neither survives alone. Learning that outruns action is a tree with many branches and few roots.
09e1fcce569bd9e5: A person whose deeds exceed their learning stands firm under any pressure, while one whose learning exceeds their deeds is uprooted by the first real storm.
0d26c2a2bca50484: What is deeply rooted keeps bearing fruit even through drought.
26f799c328eb322e: The rules that govern practice are the substance of learning, and the sciences are its refinements. Being given many obligations is a gift rather than a burden.
6b9159a0f1d0b004: The wise learn from everyone, the strong master themselves, the rich are content with what they have, and the honored are the ones who honor others.
5df48f1695146c99: Do even the smallest good act at once, because every act makes the next one of its kind easier, and its own reward is the character it builds. Despise no person and dismiss no thing, since each has its hour and its place.
5f237d453a26b1c4: Hold yourself humbly, because everything a person hopes for ends in the ground.
d9514e3518c844c6: What a person profanes in private is eventually exposed in public, whether they did it knowingly or not.
8af18b3b6c46ef70: Learning in order to put something into practice gives you more than learning in order to pass it on.
0b075470465f762e: Never turn what you have learned into a crown for your own status or a tool for your own profit, because trading on it destroys you. Do not cut yourself off from your community.
d69e54a4bb29fbb9: People honor those who treat what is worth honoring with respect, and they hold in contempt those who degrade it.
b5b0cfe1382373f6: Shrinking from the power to judge others spares you hatred and corruption, while handing down confident verdicts marks a person as arrogant. Never judge alone, and never press others to adopt your view.
a210b0a18858227a: Whoever keeps to their principles in poverty will end up keeping them in wealth, and whoever abandons them in wealth will end up abandoning them in poverty too.
86c628bb65183100: Spend less effort on possessions and more on understanding, stay humble before everyone, and expect that neglect will always find you plenty of excuses.
8d42e7e6cc0a12be: Every good act earns you a defender and every wrong act an accuser, and remorse joined to good deeds shields you from the consequences.
7b9469271c4cb94b: A group united around something larger than itself will endure, and one that is not will fall apart.
810147dc8cf0dfbf: Guard your student's dignity as you guard your own, respect your colleague as you would your teacher, and hold your teacher in the awe you owe to heaven.
51741a102ea16752: Be careful in how you learn, because an error in understanding can do the damage of a deliberate wrong.
5b348119797ce7c9: A good name is worth more than learning, office, or power.
1139efc6e7a0498a: Go to where learning already lives rather than waiting for it to reach you, because it is your companions there who make it stick, and your own understanding alone is not enough to lean on.
6d305ce21b100c11: It is not within our power to explain why the wicked prosper and the good suffer.
72599a56b06bd278: Be the first to greet anyone you meet, and choose to be the least among the excellent rather than the first among the mediocre.
3f5ab51644572b98: This life is the anteroom to what comes after, so prepare yourself here. An hour of repentance and good deeds in this world outweighs the whole of the next, and yet an hour of peace in the next outweighs the whole of this one.
bccf7fe73198564f: Do not try to reach a person while their anger, grief, resolve, or humiliation is still fresh.
9bb5c5f9c6125eea: Do not let yourself take pleasure in an enemy's downfall.
f34fc6001bda4803: What you learn as a child is written on a clean page, while what you learn late in life is written over what is already there.
c3505cad9902d239: The seasoned offer a ripeness of understanding that the inexperienced cannot yet give.
d7c537499e7bd78d: Judge people by what they actually contain rather than by their age or their surface.
0ab715c5d719c2e2: Envy, greed, and ambition take a person out of the world.
b6db6c970d498516: Everyone alive will die and be held to account by a judgment that cannot be bribed, deceived, or outwaited, and the grave offers no refuge from it.
74dacf1db20ea643: You did not choose to be born, to live, or to die, and you will not choose whether to answer for how you lived.
49ec3c0a185d315e: The world was made with great deliberateness, so that destroying it counts as a great crime and preserving it as a great merit.
69f137fd47f3b8e8: The long stretch of generations before any reckoning shows how patient the divine is. Standing firm through repeated trials is what proves the depth of a person's devotion.
a4b8f3a91f6002f9: People keep testing the patience of what sustains them, even after being rescued again and again.
326c2ceea6dc9288: In a place set apart as sacred, the ordinary limits of space, decay, and danger seem to fall away.
fe7c755a146c84b3: Some things in the world were prepared at the edge of creation, made in advance for the moment they would be needed.
b818f6024ecc0a06: A wise person does not speak out of turn, does not interrupt, answers to the point, admits what they do not understand, and concedes the truth; the uncultured person does the opposite in every case.
d7977a760e4ed435: When a community fails to give what it owes, scarcity falls on everyone, including those who did their part.
d45293120099ab7c: Violence enters a society through delayed justice, corrupted justice, and teaching twisted away from its true sense.
ce037fcfc9a70bc1: Disaster arrives precisely at the moments when a community has failed to give the poor what was owed to them.
4f9ac1a722dc7733: A person's character is revealed by how they divide what is theirs from what is others'. A person's temperament is measured by how easily they are angered and how easily they are calmed.
534250d7d235b453: The best temperament is slow to anger and quick to forgive, and the best learner grasps quickly and forgets slowly. Wanting to give while resenting the giving of others is only envy wearing generosity's clothes.
96ad19c5dbaef1bc: Generosity is genuine only when you both give and want others to give as well. Going to learn and actually practicing each have their own worth, but only doing both makes a person good.
4eef3bb09d885de0: People differ in what they retain from what they hear, and the best of them keep what is valuable and let the worthless pass through.
13d38d3f6f28c215: Love that depends on some outside advantage dies when that advantage does, while love that depends on nothing outside itself never dies.
d6eb6975bae19717: An argument conducted for the sake of the truth leaves something lasting behind, while an argument conducted for the sake of the arguers leaves nothing.
55e9492476069c08: Whoever leads others toward good is protected from their own failings, and whoever leads others into wrong is left without the means to recover.
fb4544e37a58f6c6: Generosity, humility, and modesty mark a good person, while envy, arrogance, and pride mark a corrupt one. The generous flourish both in this life and beyond it.
29360a39ce48f3fb: The greedy and deceitful destroy themselves and do not live out their natural span.
eeaa00b741b23cfe: Pursue what is right with boldness, speed, and strength.
bc5c887484268106: Human life moves through natural stages, each with its own fitting task and capacity, from learning in childhood through judgment in middle age to frailty at the end.
e2117e5744d0e315: Return to a great text again and again for your whole life, because everything worth knowing can be found by studying it deeply.
fcda05ebc5b60242: Neglecting learning is a disgrace, and only the person devoted to study is truly free.
580150d82aa4e03c: Sustained effort at learning raises a person step by step.
5b4a97536fcda7cd: Honor anyone who teaches you anything, no matter how small the lesson.
a4fbcabbd350cbcd: Genuine honor and genuine good consist in wisdom itself, not in anything wisdom might get you.
560444c871de1405: Wisdom is worth pursuing even at the cost of comfort, wealth, and physical ease.
29d54a15c00591b2: Do not chase status, honor, or the favor of the powerful; let your actions outrun your knowledge, and trust that good work is eventually repaid.
8cb61584fdb3b7b4: Wisdom is harder to acquire than power or office, because it demands many disciplines at once: attentive study, good character, moderation in appetite and speech, and the company of those who know more.
5f03f4974e436399: Wisdom requires accepting hardship, being content with what you have, restraining your speech, taking no credit, judging others generously, and learning in order to teach and to act rather than to impress.
969b67d2f701b531: Attribute ideas to whoever originated them; giving credit does real good in the world.
796b14cd6b170eab: Wisdom sustains and heals the person who lives by it.
a456958be33a0fdf: Wisdom brings long life, prosperity, honor, and peace.
e0ad468292d78a4a: Beauty, strength, wealth, honor, wisdom, age, and children are genuine goods only when they belong to a person of good character.
35d8c48477381bbf: A single life can embody every quality that makes a person admirable.
8d6c6a2e02481399: No amount of wealth is worth leaving a place where you can learn.
be097eb4ef1108c7: Wealth cannot follow you past death; only what you have understood and the good you have done stay with you.
769f8ce3ab5e8646: What is most precious — wisdom, the world itself, sacred people and places — is not truly owned by anyone but belongs to something greater.
5bb67aae7b0387a1: The holiest things belong to the divine rather than to those who seem to possess them.
64fd9618bd465921: Everything that exists was made for the sake of its maker.
df003533eb0ce6e5: A heart in love reaches toward its beloved through everything it sees, and nothing but union will quiet its restlessness.
f509f00dcfad0d7c: Seek ceaselessly and die to yourself, remembering the divine until the self is forgotten and no separation remains between seeker and sought.
715a2ad872dcad07: Love consumes the body but enlarges the soul, so grieve the outward wasting less than you celebrate the inward growth.
c5973fc6d5a5eae4: Look past the flesh, which corrupts and decays, to the spirit, which stays fresh forever.
7a48f47df40b7ae7: What reaches the soul from the divine surpasses anything imagination or reason can conceive, and only perishable splendors have the power to distract from it.
15d987d10177faac: The love of the divine is worth more than every kingdom and treasure that could be offered in exchange.
25fe5b750b17910e: Union with the eternal is what makes life life; without it, existence is an empty vessel.
313e93892fded29d: Souls arise from a single source and belong to it, and their restlessness in the world is the pull of that source drawing them home.
706745c35be6bff5: All beauty is a faint reflection of the divine and is dimmed the moment the real thing appears, yet the very desire for it sustains the one who desires.
ad6fc20341849dd1: Every particular form is a passing expression of an eternal original, so nothing is really lost when a form perishes and there is no reason to grieve.
b27a6a75b2b2b4f4: A life without love is wasted; only love and the lover endure, so set your heart on what cannot die rather than on what will.
a53a83ed05acb999: The soul takes on the image of what it loves, and beauty overwhelms whoever truly sees it.
abf82e3acf374807: The lover longs for the beloved to be revealed, since every sweetness and every light comes from that source.
a2fcc5a520a6b7d7: Searching the outside world for the divine is futile; it is found only by looking into your own heart.
b036416906309e10: Only like can recognize like, and when the light within meets the light without, the two become indistinguishable.
d19e780372234c96: In the encounter with the divine the seeker loses himself entirely and dissolves into the greater reality.
fc70866b79d75628: Individual beings rise from the one source like foam on a wave and melt back into it.
8a733198e060b64c: Death is not a parting but a homecoming; what looks from here like a setting is actually a rising.
9892dafab084fd91: Do not close yourself off among the corrupt, for the whole world is a mirror in which the beloved appears, and no part can be greater than the whole.
be28db38e442eee8: The divine is always present within you, in joy and in distress alike, and was never actually hidden.
72ca2ee8dbbd00bc: Stop filling your life with worthless things the way a child fills his lap with dirt and pebbles; break out of the body's confinement and grow into the life of the spirit.
61514f43648b35e7: The soul is worth cherishing only because it carries a trace of what made it.
81e5eba2634f61b5: Love is a flight upward that tears through veil after veil: it begins by renouncing your life, ends by leaving the ground entirely, and requires ceasing to see either the visible world or yourself.
9f37a8836bd9f52e: Everything is departing at every moment and life is desperately short, yet most people sleep through it.
e11f8139b055c6ff: Seek what you love, and stay awake.
032c4e9eba10a1c1: You were once dust and are now spirit, once ignorant and now wise; whatever brought you this far will carry you further, and even the pain it costs you is a form of being drawn closer.
09c7ff1f94d0ba82: Stay awake, sober, and silent, and free yourself from the prison of appetite and distraction, so that you can perceive the larger life beyond it.
56b13b621e23f372: Humble yourself and you will reach the goal, but the road is lined with people who longed their whole lives and died without arriving.
8e00bec0eb66a117: Do not look for happiness in the world, because it is not there; find it in service instead. Be silent, so that the source of your speech can speak through you.
e838bcdc9f2ba293: The soul was caged in the world and at death returns to its true home; once you have reached the source, the ornaments and habits of the old life are meaningless.
b28668aebc306119: The heart fearlessly braves what should destroy it, and having found what it sought, it should fall silent and rest.
f01309266460875e: Love overwhelms and ruins the self it enters, breaking open every dark, closed place, until the heart throws itself in and is lost.
795e2512f237119b: Humility carries you to the goal, but the road of love is strewn with those who suffered and died without ever reaching it.
ad1a8536b010d6c9: Being crushed is what makes a life fragrant and useful to others, so the pain is not to be resented. Everything in nature weaves the thing that ends it.
c5e60129797939f7: Love is both the snare that binds you and the only thing that frees you, and it is the harmony running underneath everything. The world is a clear mirror of the divine to anyone whose sight is not clouded from within.
7795f3ea63eb67a6: Souls move toward their source the way rivers move toward the sea, and all of nature's beauty and sorrow is an expression of that longing.
8bc1da74af2bdff8: Once you see the divine in everything, all of creation provokes love. The deepest happiness is two beings sharing a single soul.
d6d2da98584e25bd: Two people in deep union experience ordinary surroundings as transformed, lose their separate identities, and are no longer bound by place.
cf179b767442b6ba: Personal suffering and even the loss of one's days matter little if what is eternal endures.
42df979b90aa913e: Love is a kind of ecstatic madness that heals arrogance and self-importance and lifts a person beyond ordinary physical life.
6cf943d3cafa2787: Without the presence of what one loves, a person loses vitality and the capacity to act.
846069a93ac50d33: Love is the instrument by which the deepest realities are read, and every lesser attachment is really a partial pull toward the ultimate one. Any attempt to explain love in words falls short of the experience itself.
8b6e7a234fc498df: Anything that turns your attention away from the ultimate becomes a false object of worship. Yet the ordinary faculties you have should be tended rather than extinguished, since they light the way toward union.
836bf01977bd958d: The source of life renews the world constantly yet remains hidden from those who suffer and call out for it.
7f19af494f37461a: Woman is a manifestation of the divine rather than an object to be possessed.
c968a25944c92f0c: Absorption in what one loves can suspend ordinary duty and time, and the deepest love is by nature concealed from outside view.
f8ae0746773d408a: Conditions that strip away appearances make the excellent and the worthless look alike to human eyes, but the one who truly knows can still tell them apart.
1fbb40c27a4ef195: Recognizing and admitting your own flaws is what moves you toward perfection; believing you are already perfect halts your growth entirely.
2f6af0001e3eb80a: What you actually love is never the visible form, since you abandon that form the instant its life departs, though the form remains.
a5360009993ed64f: If sensory appearance were what you loved, love would persist as long as the appearance did; in reality visible things merely reflect a light whose source lies elsewhere, and that source is what deserves your attention.
35645c56922c2805: Those who love operate by their own laws and are fearless. Pain is valuable because hardship, darkness, and decline are the concealed conditions from which renewal and elevation emerge.
78a974c6be111604: If inner experience alone were sufficient, the physical world and its outward forms of devotion would have no reason to exist.
64f1926e0f53516e: Approaching what once seemed terrifying transforms it into something nourishing, filling you with new life and revealing what was hidden. Attend to the underlying reality rather than the surface disturbances it throws up.
df433ac27800e499: People drift blindly on the very reality that carries them; beneath every visible motion is an unseen force moving it, and beneath the spirit a deeper spirit still. Once you truly perceive this, you move freely.
266fce25df77905e: The value of a place comes entirely from who is present in it, so that any confinement becomes paradise with the beloved and any paradise becomes torment without.
8eb1cc1190c8a49e: Love is never one-sided; whenever it arises in one heart it is already answered in the other, though it wastes the lover and thrives in the beloved.
f32fd1ef677947a2: The soul is drawn toward life, wisdom, and what is exalted, while the body is drawn toward possessions and comfort. Whatever you seek is already inclining toward you.
18dc071001770d7a: Do not mutilate what is beautiful in yourself; the war people wage against themselves is waged with their own destructive thoughts, which wound the soul like poisoned claws.
9e1df3a889ef231a: Your own hostile thoughts are what disfigure you; the peace you damage is your own.
ed62a07a9a98cc1a: Wealth, power, luxury, and status are weak snares compared to sexual attraction, which most reliably overrides human reason and self-control.
02e6c3cecf792a24: Lovers, beloveds, and their love all pass away; only the underlying reality persists, and it is what animates these transient beings and sets them longing for each other.
accda61e6d2d102e: Envy takes root in a heart that has none, and existence disturbs what has no real being. Hope in a person is itself grounds for their release and the erasure of their failings.
c02e74a3d32ed155: Elaborating on your feelings toward someone in their presence reveals that you love your own emotional experience rather than the person.
17860d35f25b7aa9: Real love makes the other the single object of desire rather than a trigger for your own moods. Total surrender of the self is the price of the deepest union, in which the self vanishes entirely.
d9a863ffe2e345dd: Once direct contact is achieved, intermediaries and teachings become unnecessary. What ordinary people read as punishment appears as blessing to those in that state.
3ba0bdab9765bbff: People imitate teachers and prophets without realizing that the words originate from a source behind them, mistaking the messenger for the origin.
2bb3be5d4e7b1882: Visible forms are only shadows of truth, adequate for beginners but too confining for the mature. Every denial exists in order to make room for the affirmation that follows, and negating the self clears the way to see that only one existence is real.
1c2371cc16111766: Absorption in life's pleasures and busyness obscures reality; the remedy is a different kind of intoxication that carries a person beyond the self.
f1b512a31d690724: The evidence that you have genuinely glimpsed truth is that you withdraw from pride, not that you can talk about it.
3ccf678ab3b21b18: Surrendering ordinary reason to overwhelming beauty is what opens the door to a deeper wisdom. Any particular beauty is only a fragment of the whole, so give yourself to the whole.
17cfcbfd02d0c38d: Ideas received secondhand can be verified by direct perception, and accumulating such ideas eventually guides you to the reality behind them.
d4929f6a946aa309: Denying that anything exists beyond what you can see is like a child denying reason because he has not yet reached it; a higher state is not disproved by the inability of a lower one to perceive it.
fc4fc8af2f159a7f: Conflicting thoughts break a person, and longing can exhaust one of learning and reason entirely, leaving only the desire for what is loved.
98554392bb2a53cd: Whatever power produced beauty out of harshness and height out of lowness can also turn present sorrow into joy.
b36e335b480d40fa: The exchange offered by reality is absurdly generous: it takes your worthless possessions, your grief, and your tears, and returns light, life, and lasting greatness.
273ae9c9e42f130b: The animating source is itself invisible but is known through its effects, in the same way wind is known by moving dust and joy by the laughter it causes.
2cd52309607d2501: Trusting in providence does not excuse you from acting; make full use of practical means and effort while you trust, because those who do not strive for their aims are fools.
58b40d79524a4adf: Wisdom and skill are distributed independently of size, strength, or status, so the seemingly weak often receive insight denied to the powerful.
6c540fe4fd230116: Sleep nightly releases people from the constraints of body, station, and circumstance, so that prisoner and king are alike unaware of their condition.
08a6e96fc6b86b1b: The soul destroys and rebuilds the self in better form, in the way one demolishes a house to reach the treasure beneath it and then rebuilds.
80ccfa361346a2e9: Since deceivers imitate the language and appearance of the genuine in order to trap the credulous, judge people by the effects of their conduct rather than their claims, and be slow to trust everyone.
444521376cb2e2be: Hardship sent by the one you love becomes sweet, and grief itself becomes a thing to cherish because it is the medium through which you remain in relation.
1d01ccf3c6322808: The distinctions between self and other, high and low, are provisional; they exist so that the underlying unity can play through them, and they dissolve when union occurs.
dc5673485a8f99c9: When all separate selves become one, they are absorbed into what they loved.
7d23c0f6fcbc1fa2: Ultimate reality cannot be perceived by the senses or conceived by the mind. Love contains far more than the alternating states of joy and sorrow, and the true lover stands above both, unaffected by fortune's seasons.
06e2fd5af36f6ba4: The deepest ecstatic states liberate a person from both weeping and rejoicing, and cannot be described in words, since they amount to complete immersion in and identification with what is infinite.
292759f3f8dd19de: People pass their lives asleep and veiled, and awakening restores to them a consciousness and joy they did not know they had lost.
36eda64382e478e5: Material possessions and bodily demands work against the soul's good. The senses draw us downward while a different light draws us up, and love, once kindled, needs no external evidence to confirm itself.
31d4febdc7b00817: Nothing contains the infinite except the human heart. The worst spiritual sickness is imagining yourself perfect and superior, a weakness present in everyone. Knowledge not rooted in truth is merely a burden and as impermanent as cosmetics.
064c1bee5d1c3597: Knowledge carried humbly rather than for vanity eventually stops being a burden and becomes an inner resource. Words and elegant phrasing are worthless next to a heart that actually burns.
ee3646328cb58335: Those who love the correct performance of ritual and those whose hearts genuinely burn are two different kinds of people.
f1f14b46fd2ca1d5: Suffering endured involuntarily can count as fully as sacrifice chosen deliberately, and what feels like irrecoverable loss may be preservation in another form.
738bbdb1d515628f: Accept misfortune without doubting that it serves some good, and meet gain and loss alike with equanimity rather than fear.
27d34b89f552c51d: People venerate the outward institution while persecuting the people who embody its spirit, though only the latter matter. Ignorance is a deeper stain than physical impurity and cannot be washed away by ordinary means; the honest response is to acknowledge that even one's devotion is defective.
ecb856c7ae6fab9b: It is right to admit that your best efforts are as flawed as you are, and to ask for good in return for that inadequacy.
4091badcff316eb4: Ask for what is good in this life and the next, and make the ultimate itself, rather than any intermediate reward, the aim.
1788eea74ae69f1f: Because all praise is ultimately directed toward one reality, the differing religions are in substance a single religion wearing borrowed forms.
7509463eb05e960a: Those who are preyed upon by violence and greed have no protector except the moral order itself, and they are right to demand to know why they were made to suffer it.
02f9b38ff29cbd5c: Anything vulnerable needs a guardian appointed for it, one both skilled and vigorous enough to turn back the aggression of the ruthless.
3911d6ff81ebaef0: No ordinary being is both powerful enough to repel cruelty and free of cruelty himself; only the highest, whom people approach through their deeds rather than their pleas, is equal to that.
9e5279140076b850: Only what knows every deed already done and still to be done is fit to judge, and its judgment is what will stand.
3343422f3cedfc60: The honest and the industrious will not be swept away in the destruction that falls on the wicked.
4be8cc409430d3d6: The true leader is not found by the usual search; authority is given to the one who does the patient, productive work of tending the earth.
035f8a916474c9fe: Wisdom may prepare both nourishment and doctrine, but a human voice is still needed to carry the teaching to other people.
e6e6a1a6efc08196: The one who actually listens, and wants to speak what he has heard, is the one given the standing to speak.
820b0d7393d093f3: Suffering resents being answered with a mere voice when it wanted a strong hand, and doubts that a powerless advocate can change anything.
d9dca47ab7846008: What is worth asking for is not victory but the peaceful ordinary goods of home and quiet happiness in place of devastation.
6c7d90719590408f: No one can carry out a great task on his own strength; the power to do it has to arrive from outside him.
2ed114ba1d9daa04: Good thought, good word, and good deed are themselves the blessing.
63a413013ce7255d: Ask first that all your actions be done rightly, and for the understanding needed to relieve the suffering around you.
cb4627a1c10f71c9: Right living is what carries a person into flourishing, and it should be sought for the body and the mind alike.
44ff879157429801: A ready and devoted mind is what makes an imperishable order advance in the world.
beb1978c7e373040: Knowing what right action leads to, one should teach it for as long as one has the strength to.
afb9faefa49abab2: Attentive obedience is the road to the highest good, and words held firmly in the mouth keep corruption at bay.
1705f44fb08b7971: Strength to outlast a tormentor comes from words that give the spirit something to stand on.
3af01c4adb76bb94: The blessing to ask for is a good mind of one's own, and the capacity to hear a true word and understand it.
faf2cc9ea05c13c5: The best gift is one given not for a season but for the whole of a life and beyond it.
159d1d49642fcf8e: What is highest is easily reached and already wishes us well, so sincere effort in its service can never offend it.
c94af7f0ee6138b3: Right instruction is never without effect, even in the daily struggle for food, and is therefore worth wanting.
89377afa5e7729b9: Ask to be taught at the source, so that what you pass on to others is not secondhand.
3f6d8a323e446e48: What is worth proclaiming to seekers is praise, offering, and the meditations that arise from good thinking.

9a19a9d515bccf81: Two opposed impulses, a better and a worse, run through every thought, word, and deed, and the wise choose between them deliberately.
f9b713fd8c3a6eb7: The choice between those two impulses determines the shape of existence itself: the worst life for the wicked, the best state of mind for the good.
f25855e9e791fdd9: Each of the two impulses chose its own realm, and each person likewise reveals which he has chosen by what he actually does.
f3e7337f002cd087: Those who deliberate badly end by choosing the worst mind, and then rush together into rage that poisons the lives of others.
043c254cf600b35e: Steadfast devotion is what gives a body to what would otherwise remain merely spiritual.
c4057682e85e3808: When the long struggle is decided, it is good thinking that wins the victory, delivering falsehood into the hands of truth.
d84598fa0d87eafe: The worthiest ambition is to be among those who renew the world and carry it forward toward its perfection.
69b6e84dcb15f1bc: Falsehood is finally destroyed, and those who walked honestly gather quickly into the best place.
cf24dd7acef3b373: Learn what long injury awaits the wicked and what blessing awaits the honest, because once you know it, deliverance is already beginning.
19ba4e2321f8475d: The same words sound unheard-of to those whose oaths serve deceit and are the best of words to those who are wholeheartedly devoted to truth.
675cea8481a3ea20: When words fail to make the truth visible, the teacher must come in person, backed by real authority.
02145435471134ad: The test that separates the two sides should be declared openly, so that anyone able to see can know it for himself.
fe00d5a26054e074: Devotion joined to good thinking is what one prays for when what is needed is the power to break falsehood.
6355425df87caef6: The knowledge worth asking for is knowledge of what can be and what cannot.
42acb4807c9f4212: The best word ever spoken is the one that promises wholeness and deathlessness, and a realm founded on it will flourish.
00bb57116b63d3c3: The one who first conceived the truths of things is, by that very understanding, the maker of the world's order, and he sustains the good mind in those who hold it.
86f9c8af6970b0ae: The highest is recognized as the father of good thinking, the maker of the moral order, and the lord of what we do with our lives.
b6d185cfee1fe3b5: A path is laid out, but the creature that walks it may go with the one who tends and builds or with the one who never has.
34b041710131e559: Given the choice, what is good chooses the honest, industrious worker over the plunderer, and the plunderer has no share in it.
6872a566d314ee7e: Along with laws, understanding, and bodily life, we were given commands by which a person may make his own choices.
bf7fde5137b7d843: The honest and the lying, the enlightened and the ignorant, all speak with equal conviction, so devotion must go behind their voices to the impulses themselves.
4fcd4958d46de2e7: Whether a question is asked openly or in secret, and whether a person punishes his small sins harshly or not at all, none of it goes unobserved.
e09c48282f40b5ec: It is worth asking what is coming, what the honest owe, what awaits the wicked, and how it all ends.
218be3614e9d5f00: Ask what the man deserves who props up the wicked and can sustain his own life only by lawlessly stealing from the honest worker.
f9619cf5485ad5b2: Ask by what actions a person who has advanced good rule in his house, his region, and his country might come to resemble what is highest.
066892b2a4a896b1: Let the informed speak with the informed and refuse to be deceived by the ignorant; truth is settled at its source, not by whoever shouts loudest.
d4d47de83b0747d0: Refuse to listen to the teaching or the commands of a corrupt man, since he would bring your home and your country to ruin.
39ca102cbdcffd07: Listen instead to the one who conceived the world's order, whose speech is bound to truth and whose light guides those who are struggling.
1cd97ee76c5564f8: Whoever deceives a good person will inherit a long, dark, and degraded existence, and it is his own deeds and his own conscience that bring it on him.
3f8783d32d11abc9: Wholeness and immortality are given to the one who is a friend of the good in both thought and action.
e8a6b0e8c004d72d: The discerning see this clearly, and the one who follows the right order in word and deed is the most useful being alive.
e375c6d9f0ee6406: Even a false ruler has his kinsmen, his laborers, and his peers praying for him, so one must consciously choose whose friendship to hold and stand against those who do harm.
a19983cc89b756fb: The answer that comes back to devotion is that the devotion itself has been accepted and claimed.
ad772804c6ea1f3a: Those who sacrifice most to what is false are themselves the offspring of falsehood, and their deceptions are known throughout the world.
f1f70d3e292a0c7b: False teaching confuses thought until men doing the worst possible deeds believe themselves beloved, having drifted far from good thinking and from wisdom.
dce7b7b534b13290: Those who corrupt other people's thinking rob them of both happiness in this life and immortality beyond it.
392f6bed63e05b66: Even when destructive leaders win fame for their doctrine, the truth outlasts them and can still be re-established by those who hold to it.
ad476567918103c8: The wicked cannot see that the very weapons and victories they are celebrated for are what will eventually destroy them.
fadcc8eb6e696c58: Even a celebrated figure of the past can be counted among the wretched if he violated what is sacred to win people's favor, and the righteous must distinguish themselves from him.
aed192c827fe11ef: A false teacher destroys sound doctrine, perverts people's understanding of life, and strips them of the real wealth that is a good mind.
3209bdd88a115743: The corrupt call the best things the worst, and their teaching ends in a wasted land and violence against the good.
546f69dff5b7c276: Those who take counsel with the powerful and wicked plunder households of what they have inherited and attack the very people whose goodness resists them.
e1878ec44986fe33: Those who bless what they are in fact destroying, and who prefer falsehood and its priests to truth, stand condemned by their own actions.
c7df702de642b4d2: Tyranny works through the worst impulses of the mind, and while it envies the prophet's message, its real work is keeping people from ever seeing the truth.
cf78cf3f2d584805: The impressive deeds of corrupt rulers are only deceptions, and the power they mean to conquer is the very thing that would free people from death.
