9c817be91150dec4: Eloquence and intellectual instruction do not liberate anyone; those sinking deepest often think themselves wisest.
e5f0e0880cb13558: The road ahead is long, so proceed with constant attentiveness.
d19a1bf3b9630fa3: Choose liberation over worldly things, and maintain the body only as an instrument for undoing past consequences.
c882bad325ac1e0f: Attachment to the body and to physical appearances brings suffering.
ef5108c9744c068f: Once you understand what generates your future consequences, live simply and take only what is already available rather than causing anything to be made for you.
f25323ea418c95e7: Accumulate nothing; carry only what you can move freely with, as a bird carries its feathers.
1df313388d90e47d: Take only what is given cleanly, stay unattached to any fixed home, and remain deliberate while everyone around you is careless.
e8a28d251917456d: This teaching comes from one who has attained both perfect knowledge and perfect faith.
0b5d396ca71ed1bf: A man fattens a young ram in his yard, feeding it well in preparation for a guest's arrival.
9b861a8eea445520: Well-fed and grown plump, the animal is now ready for the purpose it was kept for.
da50956037183fa9: The animal lives comfortably only until the moment it is wanted, and then it is killed.
e8d220c8c405c1cd: Comfort and indulgence can be the very thing fattening you for ruin.
eea9e128b714b2f7: The ignorant man kills, lies, robs, steals, and cheats, always calculating who he can plunder next.
49f16d1632bffb43: He chases women, pleasure, business, drink, and meat, and grows strong enough to dominate others.
3ee8949701ae0eb7: Everything he consumes and accumulates buys him nothing but his own destruction, like the ram fattened for slaughter.
34dfc5cbe152b45b: Someone who spends his life on pleasures and wealth, believing only in what he can see, will face death in terror.
19540b4412d07ecc: One who has spent life harming others loses everything at death and passes, unwilling, into darkness.
bcfe1b69093ec82a: People trade something enormous for something trivial, like risking a fortune for pennies or a kingdom for a forbidden piece of fruit.
6fa54b418f488c37: Human pleasures are a thousand times smaller than what is available at a higher level of existence.
6d86c0d81e32f898: Those with real understanding live vastly longer, so the foolish lose an immense amount by spending only a short human life.
1c9de3fada9ad324: Of three merchants who set out with capital, one multiplied it, one broke even, and one lost everything — the same is true of how people spend a life.
81714f17f98bbeee: Human life is the capital and a higher existence is the profit; squander it and you fall to a lower form of being.
b78cbf1e0bb39ca0: Someone enslaved by his appetites has forfeited both a human and a divine future, leaving only two miserable paths.
1e8b62af648542b3: Once a good position is lost, it takes an extremely long time to climb back.
ea541efd4a6374a7: Weigh honestly what a corrupt life costs against what a virtuous one gains; the person who merely breaks even is simply born human again.
828d02f099efc7cb: People who cultivate ordinary virtue return to ordinary life, because everyone reaps exactly what they have done.
c1b7c32058c66d21: The one who multiplies his capital is the one who practices exceptional virtue, and he rises to a higher state.
54f7335ec553f7b5: If you can recognize when someone is gaining, you have no excuse for not recognizing when you yourself are losing.
063c90a650620ebe: Human pleasures compared to divine ones are a dewdrop compared to the ocean.
d85f9a222700225d: Given how brief and small the pleasures of this life are, it is worth almost any effort to secure something more lasting.
721368bef63e3ea7: Anyone who has not given up pleasure will keep straying from the path no matter how well he has been taught.
5c79a3266cbefa3f: Anyone who has renounced pleasure stays on course, understanding that shedding the body is a promotion rather than a loss.
7cb3555ced6ccaf5: Right living leads to a future of wealth, beauty, reputation, long life, and deep happiness.
a51e39c5a07dbf9b: The fool who lives unjustly and turns from the truth descends into misery.
66df76ee78093397: The wise person who turns away from wrongdoing and follows the truth rises to a higher state.
2b881e8e204db8b4: No amount of effort, endurance, or writing could ever adequately express the greatness of the divine.
f980ca5fbeb5250c: There is one God — creator, without fear or hatred, unborn, self-existent, and eternal — who existed before time and will exist after it.
d9d219aac9ed51a5: A finite mind cannot map an infinite reality, yet that reality is present everywhere, closer than we imagine, seeing everything we do and deny.
fc7509b54a570d58: Since the world was made by divine power, whatever divine will decrees should be accepted.
f99d7bbdafbf37bf: Those who sell religion for profit have corrupted the very source of what they claim to offer.
aa5704963dae85a6: To love the divine so completely that you value yourself least and everyone else highly looks like madness to the world.
9b3ee65c8f1a92ed: Truly belonging to a faith is difficult: it requires shedding pride and possessions, releasing the fear of death, effacing yourself, and being kind to everyone.
9f07fcaef5f51036: Kindness, sincerity, justice, modesty, and honest conduct are the real substance of religion, not its outward forms.
daab51158a303411: Treat what belongs to others as absolutely forbidden; you are saved by practicing truth, not by professing it, and no ritual seasoning makes a dishonest life acceptable.
dbf2120c3dd1f9e2: Genuine devotion consists of truth, justice, charity, good intentions, and gratitude — and false people obtain only falsehood.
b243cd9345ad0856: The truly religious person erases his own ego, lives by truth and contentment, and takes nothing that is not his.
41ad458a79b70f83: Countless holy figures, prophets, ascetics, and gods of every tradition stand at the same threshold.
2dc525e3c24d6b27: No quantity of religious credentials or ascetic effort brings peace without genuine inner teaching, because all traditions answer to one immeasurable reality.
a538bf2c5592ed5f: There is one eternal, self-existent Creator, free of fear and enmity, who was and is and always will be.
a854c06741092e72: Every word, act, breath, and perception is accounted for, and attachment to material things is a deception that leaves you with nothing in the end; when you die, no one accompanies you.
da0bd172e62dc2ec: Praise does not make God greater, and the divine gaze rests on the lowest of the low rather than the great.
e6c215f5e7ecbcd9: You carry the consequences of whatever you have accumulated, and only those who acted honestly can face the end without shame.
9c80bdac38948fd0: Wealth, youth, and beauty are temporary guests that wither like leaves; the present life is not your permanent home, and anyone who sleeps through it wakes up robbed.
e7552823e25ddb5a: When your allotted time is complete you are separated from everything, having received exactly what your past actions earned.
94f427b102533c0b: Since we pass through this world as guests and can claim no authority in what follows, pride is absurd and each person fares according to what they did.
eb334f8a4505adb0: Grief spent on lost possessions and status is wasted; only sorrow born of love for the divine has meaning, since everything material is temporary and pride in it is self-deception.
689bb131e59ca383: Death is an appointment already scheduled for every household, so meet it the way a bride meets a wedding — with praise and readiness rather than dread.
8192b1c8bfdc1434: There is one creator, self-existent and free of fear or hatred, who was real before time began and remains real through every age.
c407aef101118557: No amount of thinking, silence, wealth, or clever technique can reach ultimate reality or clear away one's falseness; only living in accordance with the order of things can.
73b64aa8e956aa67: Everything that happens — birth, status, pleasure, pain, reward — unfolds under one indescribable order that nothing escapes, and grasping this dissolves egotism.
ff6594a9521d1df8: No one has the capacity to adequately describe the power or the gifts of the infinite.
b9267a4641bc8c18: Description of the infinite never reaches an end, because it creates and destroys, is simultaneously distant and near, and endless attempts by countless people all fall short.
7dbb705e43fcb0a6: The ultimate is at peace precisely because it is detached from outcomes.
05b55edf5c72cceb: We have nothing to offer the source of everything we already receive, except loving attention and remembrance, which is itself the doorway to freedom.
3a408cc91e4f458e: The ultimate is uncreated and self-existent; through a teacher one learns it is present everywhere, and singing its praise dissolves sorrow — yet words can never actually describe it.
32f792abaf1e538c: The one lesson worth never forgetting is that a single source sustains every living thing.
8bee518c4084b68c: Ritual purification is worthless compared to inner alignment; a single true word from a teacher is worth more than any pilgrimage.
0f157cf4243dd942: Fame, longevity, and universal admiration count for nothing without grace, which can be given to anyone but which no one can bestow upon its source.
b90b29ec9b6f0392: Attentive listening to the divine name grants understanding of the whole cosmos, removes the fear of death, and ends sorrow and wrongdoing.
16fc3c2d5f78e1de: Attentive listening to the divine name raises the lowly to honor and yields the substance of every discipline and scripture without studying them.
7ab5812589badd99: Attentive listening to the divine name confers truth, contentment, and knowledge, steadies the mind, and equals every pilgrimage.
ff90a4658f7b89a6: Attentive listening to the divine name lets the blind find their way and makes the unfathomable fathomable.
24aa8dbd66656f2c: The state of one who lives in accord with the divine cannot be written or spoken; it is known only as an inner pleasure by the person who lives it.
826cefab1b40a257: Living in accord with the divine brings understanding, knowledge of all worlds, and freedom from punishment.
c7b5032117ef2370: Living in accord with the divine clears one's path, brings honor at death, and binds one to virtue — a joy known only from the inside.
414cfc8de586f3b3: Living in accord with the divine liberates not only oneself but one's family and students, and ends all begging and dependence.
67617b2e49fa1e9a: Those chosen by grace are honored everywhere and focus on one teacher; the world is upheld by righteousness born of compassion and sustained by patience, and no one can count the creator's works.
dd66d7b7935099ca: Creation's scale is beyond any accounting, and one who recognizes this feels too small even to make an offering; whatever the formless reality wills is good.
f16e9baabe766605: The devoted — ascetics, scholars, saints, givers, warriors, silent contemplatives — are beyond counting, and the speaker feels too small before the formless reality to describe it.
94e9f14ad5b96dad: The fools, thieves, tyrants, murderers, liars, and slanderers are equally beyond counting, and even these exist within what the formless reality permits.
402d3335691feb7c: The divine has countless names, places, and worlds; language itself is how we praise, know, and record — yet the one who writes each destiny bears no destiny, and no place exists without the divine.
95c1ff8cc32c7932: Recognizing one's smallness before the formless reality, one accepts that whatever it wills is good.
916b49c5e308e625: As water cleans the body and soap cleans cloth, love of the divine name cleans a mind soiled by wrongdoing; calling yourself virtuous or wicked changes nothing, since you reap exactly what you sow.
fa5e8d7eb3ab7998: No priest, jurist, or ascetic knows when or how the world began — only its maker does — so all our confident talk about the divine is guesswork.
f8d18ab2b011ab3b: The proud find no honor when they arrive at the end.
9959c0f390d330eb: Scriptures across traditions agree the divine has no boundary and cannot be captured in writing; the only honest statement is that it alone knows its own greatness.
5cb3c9c30425d1ac: Those who praise the divine still do not comprehend it, as rivers pour into the sea without knowing its extent; and a humble creature who remembers the divine is worth more than an emperor who forgets.
18e85ad30a2917d6: The divine's mercy, perception, creation, and inner secret are all limitless; the more one says, the more remains unsaid, and only an equal could measure it — and there is no equal.
6c5a515c1464e7be: Only the infinite knows its own extent, and it gives to whomever it favors.
db7fa7fa0a2d5231: The source gives endlessly and without greed, to heroes and fools, to those who deny it and those who starve; who receives and who is freed is not ours to arrange, and interfering only brings suffering on oneself.
1ad0798c298e406e: The person granted the capacity to praise the divine is richer than any king.
681b59fe20667ffd: Everything about the divine — its qualities, dealings, justice, mercy, measures — is beyond price, and though gods, sages, scholars, and scriptures all speak of it endlessly, none can state how far beyond price it is.
1f094fdf4d705144: Gods, demons, sages, and mortals all speak of the divine, yet even a creation many times larger could not speak of it adequately; anyone who speaks of it presumptuously reveals only their own ignorance.
90bf6bc28190a898: The whole cosmos — wind, water, fire, gods, judges, sages, heroes — is a single continuous act of praise directed at a dwelling no one can locate.
a26117c420a9ca94: Scholars, celestial beings, warriors, holy places, every world and every form of life sing the same praise, above all those who are steeped in love.
56cf37f9eb72a89b: The praisers are too many to name; what matters is that the maker of this world endures, delights in what it made, answers to no one, and holds all things in its will.
102083b3da0af9cb: Instead of the outward props of asceticism, wear contentment, modesty, self-respect, and faith; conquering your own heart is the only conquest of the world worth making.
e6bd8163fa9c04c4: Let understanding be your food and compassion your steward; leave wealth and occult powers to others, since union and separation are simply the law by which the world runs.
f7c99ca42dd3c07d: Creation, preservation, and destruction are three functions of one process, directed by an origin that sees everything and is seen by nothing.
f3cdc9537ba99b5b: Everything that would ever be needed was placed into creation at once, and the maker watches what it made; the work is sound.
e619499fe40c8a79: If I had countless tongues I would use them all to climb toward union with the one reality; the envious mock what they cannot reach, and their boasting is empty.
9e6491ddf819ca20: I have no power of my own to speak, keep silent, live, die, gain wealth, or even meditate; before the infinite no one is stronger or weaker than anyone else.
c1fbaf718e7fe9eb: The earth was set as a temple in the midst of time and the elements, filled with countless kinds of beings, and each is judged by what it actually did — a distinction that becomes clear only at the end.
23188c18b716d888: In the domain of knowledge one sees the sheer multiplicity of worlds, gods, teachers, suns, moons, languages, and lineages — and finds no end to them.
2b18b8082dbcaa1c: The forms of devotion and understanding are without number.
8b21b9b225e7f6f0: In the domain of knowledge understanding shines and joy pours out as music; in the domain of happiness, beauty is the governing quality and things are made incomparably.
64f582f63ba2fa34: What happens in the higher domain cannot be described, and whoever tries will regret it; there knowledge, wisdom, intellect, and even supernatural skill are forged.
7e8225c9ff22f703: The domain of action is governed by power, and those who hold the divine in their hearts neither die nor lose their way; the realms of creation are so many that describing them never ends.
636c7cecbe6a645a: All things carry out their functions under one order, and the maker contemplates them with pleasure; describing this is beyond us.
9c160134f0e97b2a: Forge yourself as a smith forges metal: self-restraint the furnace, acceptance the craftsman, understanding the anvil, awe the bellows, discipline the fire, and love the crucible in which truth is cast.
621015396f414b89: Air, water, and earth are our teacher and parents and day and night our nurses; in the end we are measured by our deeds, and those who lived attentively brighten the way for many others.
f3eb2513da51d9dd: There is one creator — true, self-existent, unborn, immortal, free of fear and hatred, and generous.
706c7d01d76479a1: A true teacher is worth endless gratitude, for they transform ordinary people into something far greater without delay.
a4243be848723973: Without a teacher, even overwhelming light leaves you in darkness.
93356c721f7950e5: Those too clever to accept teaching are left behind like empty husks — they may flower and fruit outwardly, but are ashes inside.
0b76e4df6d614a4f: A single creator made everything, including the illusion that veils it, and delights in what he made; he gives and takes life at will.
86abd67cef19faef: Only the eternal is real — its order, its power, its authority — while everything that is born and dies is by comparison unreal.
47d7650451f4ffbe: The ultimate is beyond description: it acts on its own will, answers to no one, and knows every thought before it is spoken.
4f2b12eed9b8470b: The world is where the divine actually lives, and who is drawn out of illusion into that awareness is not something anyone can predict.
145dbd124004d9e4: Every life is recorded and every act judged; those attached to wealth and appearance have nothing to show, while those devoted to truth pass.
4fb3f1ed176624e7: Everything about existence is astonishing — the elements, the creatures, hunger and fullness, nearness and distance, meeting and losing.
f16b8874f5bfed5f: Standing in wonder at existence is itself the response; those who truly grasp it are rare and fortunate.
80b67b19b5f723c7: Every capacity we have — perception, speech, appetite, virtue and vice alike — is borrowed from a power we did not generate.
60237151112f4f87: The creative source pervades everything it observes and has no equal.
85e15ffa81c1e889: No amount of wealth or status exempts anyone from death and a reckoning with what they did; a life spent unaware is wasted.
9426585bf8068c57: Everything in existence, from the wind to the stars to the mightiest beings, operates under compulsion; only the unconditioned source is free of fear.
fe84fc4f9082007e: Heroes, scriptures, performers, and jewels all turn to dust; real understanding is not acquired by talk or technique but arrives as a gift.
ad83820a495693f0: Genuine teaching, once received, ends a long wandering — but it becomes available only through grace, and only from a teacher whose own ego is gone.
a479ffb774a9a533: Turning the whole cosmos into stage props for religious performance is a way of being robbed of the understanding you were performing about.
b978b819faad2be6: Religious performance done for a living is entertainment, and the audience laughs and goes home; what actually connects a person to the divine is longing and being met.
b09677e7348a61fc: The world spins people around like machinery, each turned by their own past actions; today's laughter becomes tomorrow's grief, and no trick escapes it.
415ba61389a62ac7: Since everything you have is given, the only sensible response is to act well and stay humble. Death arrives on schedule regardless of what you postpone.
fd162609b7e45016: Every tradition has its own approach to the divine, and generosity produces contentment — but most giving is really a bid for return and recognition.
7efbbaea1e432db0: People consuming credit they didn't earn have nothing left for what comes next, while those who simply praise what sustains them live in lasting joy.
be9ec52feb5e59d8: Burial and cremation both end in the same dust, so the argument over which is correct misses that neither party knows.
ec34cabac6679f7b: Liberation comes through contact with a teacher who has himself let go of worldly attachment, because the divine has made itself present in him.
6ce498bab6d208ba: Ego runs through every human act — giving, earning, sinning, even attaining virtue — and only when it drops away does reality become visible.
67d04636d0f3ee7f: Talking without understanding is self-inflicted torment; you receive from reality what you bring to it.
6b9a5109101ec0ec: Ego is self-reproducing and keeps a person cycling through the same patterns; it is a chronic illness whose cure is already within, released by grace and instruction.
f6b61b32c6e2353a: Those who orient toward truth become content, abstain from harm, live simply and honestly, and thereby cut the ties that bound them.
f6897e7973fe70ed: Whatever creates a world is obliged to sustain it, and does; ritual markers count for nothing without inner reality.
46025abe2d53e347: No quantity of charity, penance, heroism, or study earns liberation — grace does. Actions without it merely continue the cycle.
712aba9038a9efc8: Truth is not achieved but received, and the stubborn person who refuses it has squandered the point of being alive.
bde844ee45823697: You could read for an entire lifetime and it would amount to nothing; one word actually lived is worth more than a library of clever argument.
1a3c5a81c2b6e2e2: Reading, pilgrimage, fasting, nakedness, silence, and self-mortification all just add suffering; without inner realization, the austerities only lose you your dignity.
a4632fab0f3f7aa4: Living among tombs and wilderness is not knowledge; the person who internalizes truth finds peace and is freed from hope and fear alike.
e68fbebebc6a4a18: Those who inflate their own importance don't even know where they came from; caste distinctions mean nothing before what is real.
dbd206d078205a94: Kings, wealth, beauty, marriage, and the body itself are all impermanent, so building a life on them is building on nothing.
05cf7e53f95a3a13: A person is genuine when truth is internal — loving it, learning the way, planting it in oneself — not when the outward forms are correct.
80cc7f877fde11e8: Be merciful to living things, be charitable, and make your own heart the place of pilgrimage; truth is the cure that removes wrongdoing.
57778851a9df57cd: Abandon greed and focus on what is unseen; you will get out what you put in, so don't ruin yourself by half-hearted effort.
ad9924d74003b36d: In a corrupt age, a partial commitment produces nothing, just as unprepared cloth won't take dye; only wholehearted devotion, grounded in awe and humility, holds.
7e368a8527d79d3e: When greed, lust, and falsehood run the institutions, the priests become performers and the scholars become profiteers, each convinced of his own perfection.
c9ee20ae912f9be7: Nobody admits their own deficiency; only when integrity is what gets weighed does a person's true measure appear.
fb535f0383209a23: Wrongdoing is always eventually visible; effort is universal but outcome is not in our hands, and inherited status confers nothing where only conduct is counted.
d104f0f9f784ff88: Whether a person turns toward or away from truth is not within their own power; understanding comes when reality discloses itself through a teacher.
ae8f39141252b76b: Suffering is medicine and pleasure is the disease, because comfort kills longing. Nothing I do accomplishes anything; the doing is not mine.
0599fbbbc1711376: Each social role has its assigned duty, but the highest obligation belongs to no role — it is simply to hold the divine in mind.
689fdea11f8986b4: There is one reality behind all the gods, and understanding the connection between self and that reality is itself a kind of divinity.
77e88584f9abab74: Just as water needs a vessel to hold its shape, the mind needs understanding to be contained — and understanding needs a teacher.
1610b3cbf0203b93: Judge people by their conduct, not their learning: the educated sinner deserves punishment and the unlettered saint does not. Don't play a game you'll lose at the end.
9fc7610b1740a4d4: Each era is driven by a different force — contentment, then continence, then austerity, and now passion steered by falsehood.
34e8c9a9c3b14a74: Each scripture speaks for its own age, and each age names the divine differently while the object of reference stays the same.
8eba79bb00d59154: Sacred texts held to be authoritative yield contradictory doctrines when actually read; what saves is love, devotion, and humility, not the texts.
baa59e5bd5ff9e27: A true teacher is the vessel that carries you across, though few recognize it; those who trade the real for wealth are wrecked.
00eca9629e9c4e92: The tall, impressive tree bears bitter fruit while the low one is sweet; likewise every self-regarding person bows only to himself, and the outward bow of a hypocrite counts for nothing when the heart is impure.
dc77f3c40204c2e6: Scripture-reading, ritual observance, and religious costume amount to a hollow religion if you don't understand what you're worshipping.
cee3d5ccbcfaf971: Beauty, clothes, and power stay behind at death; what follows you is what you did, and you will regret the harm.
825c565854147e8c: A meaningful sacred thread would be woven from mercy, contentment, restraint, and truth — such a thing can't burn or break, whereas the physical thread falls off the corpse.
0d8db6e76b3c468d: People commit endless theft, adultery, and cruelty and then perform the initiation ritual anyway, replacing the thread when it wears out — as if the ceremony were the point.
12c81b05dcf46261: Honor and genuine consecration come from living in devotion, and that is the only kind that doesn't wear out.
f01c46fce7faa35f: There is no sacred thread restraining the genitals, hands, feet, tongue, or eyes — yet the priest who ties threads on necks and charges for weddings is called wise while being blind.
85927be466bf5fbb: Whoever is given the capacity to live in accordance with the true order will do so, and will find both acceptance and what his heart actually wanted.
502a382b003f947a: Practicing one religion at home and another in public, while living off what you claim is forbidden, is hypocrisy; drop the performance.
b4c611269051baba: Priests wearing sacred threads slaughter and profiteer, adopting whatever costume the ruling power rewards; their piety is a business built on lies.
33762e69e7e8955e: A person under threat from hidden malice can appeal to the source of light and life for protection, and ask that same power to fill the body with vigor and guide the hands and breath toward right action.
7a67847032881090: When the power that sustains growth is absent from the world, the land itself fails: the reeds go uncreated, the canals unlifted, the roots carried away.
bc697e813c58e744: A mountain rooted in the deep and crowned in heaven stands as a visible sign of divine magnitude, bright as the sun and legible as a star.
0500db835a93437d: People who maintain elaborate rules about ritual contamination while their conduct and motives are corrupt are not pure; real purity is inward, and comes from holding the truth in mind.
c621a4807242ad4f: Every person is visible to and moved by a power beyond themselves, and greatness is granted rather than achieved; the same power that raises someone can reduce them to a beggar.
473a1507d8326479: Wealth acquired by theft cannot be laundered into merit through religious offerings, and the priest who brokers such offerings shares the guilt; only what a person earns by honest labor carries any value beyond this life.
f7e4d1003de92443: Deceit recurs in a liar as regularly as a bodily cycle, and washing the body does not make a person clean; purity is a matter of what lives in the heart.
ef872cbb49dde8d3: Trust in a benevolent higher power, and ask it to guard your speech, your actions, and the inner life from which both come.
837d92b984fff9e1: A wise person who has been taught the hidden workings of earth and sky can see what others cannot and bring peace to those troubled in mind, and such a person deserves the community's love.
6b3b7786e9317d4b: The great teacher writes down what he knows for generations he will never meet, so that his knowledge outlives both him and the wars of his own time.
d20059ee17c12af5: A community rightly honors the leader who lifted it out of distress and defended it, since his deeds outlast his life.
362afed44458b6f1: Strength that looks like a person's own is really a gift, and it belongs as much to those who benefit from it as to the one who carries it.
786e412a56beee5d: Power given in love produces offspring whose fame outstrips the parent's, and the grief of separation is itself part of how greatness is born.
0fd4cfac4ff42c80: A person raised among animals and in the wild can carry wisdom that settled society never taught him, and be at home in the world in a way civilized people are not.
369f57897722c96a: When you have done wrong and disaster is gathering, do not brace against it; acknowledge the wrong and turn toward what is greater than you.
b0fd826585fb53ba: The prosperity of a ruler and the prosperity of his people are the same thing, so blessing him is not flattery but self-interest.
81a3f84afe06654c: A leader's strength in conflict is drawn from the collective will of those he leads, not from himself alone.
1ed4d51c7a23aa2c: Those loyal to you will follow you even into the unknown, all the way to the seat of the power that made you.
fc8b8a7804521382: Anger has its proper use; when it is rightly aimed at a real enemy, hardness of heart is not a failing.
56fab562e65e5742: Labor done willingly and in company with others turns hard ground into abundance, and there is joy in the work itself.
21e98f2d47f4cd7c: The harvest belongs to those whose land it is, and the moment of ripeness must be seized when it comes.
2f6b065c3212dcf3: The world offers both what nourishes and what destroys, and they grow side by side; wisdom is knowing which to take.
659e13b93f03fca3: There is a kind of good that does not spoil or run out, and its sign is that it ripens rather than decays.
650c985c7cebf08f: A person must sometimes leave those who love him to seek out the one who can answer what no one at home can.
b6312d533f084c9f: The journey's end is a place where the sweet is not mixed with the bitter, which is exactly what makes it not a place on earth.
8af197141ddd0fa6: The same force can be gentle as dawn and violent as a predator, and it is not a contradiction that it is both.
bf93fa01fd4bebd9: What people build for the divine is really built for time itself, marking the turning seasons; and the sacred names itself as the source of what precedes us.
cc9270d26b9b8972: The power that gives light to the world is prior to the world, and to call it your origin is simply to describe what it is.
a7227c6386ddb14c: The same power that turns the sky and shatters mountains is also the shelter that holds them fast, so what is most terrifying is also what is most protective.
600122594461f518: Placating anger means addressing the offended party by every name and aspect they hold, because a being met only partially is not really met.
90105f42c53cdd99: Reverence is completed by naming the object of it fully, not by feeling it vaguely.
7655bc3290f807d1: Real authority is recognized by its constancy: the one who never changes his purposes is the one whose power cannot be opposed.
b30848502f5b1c85: The reward asked for generosity toward the sacred is a long, honored life, an enlarged inheritance, and a good place beyond death.
13274d16e35d3af1: A prayer, once released, is meant to travel beyond the one who spoke it.
d23af5f775711b43: Illness is a person being pulled loose from life, and healing begins when compassion leaves its own comfort to arrive at the sufferer's door.
190cca829b554ad2: Recovery is understood as being made luminous again — the sick person restored to the brightness he had before, and taken back into the care of what is greatest.
d1eb2ea46cc9015e: In the depths of suffering no human hand reaches out, and the plea is not for punishment to be lifted but for the wrong itself to be turned into something right.
b9885ef011044113: There are moments when a person can only say that their failings are beyond counting.
2c125cb961e519c6: Nothing in heaven or earth stands beside the greatest power; everything else falls silent and low before it.
4c3352df8eb0ab60: The words of the highest power cannot be resisted or matched, even by others of its own kind.
47f29f9528265d12: Ask that the one who made you stay near, and that your speech and your actions be kept from doing harm.
270bbf90445810b8: Fire is what makes things what they are — it melts, refines, and purifies — and it both drives back what is evil in the dark and lights the way for anyone acting rightly.
c775a70531405d24: Healing means making a person inhospitable to what harms them and hospitable to what sustains them.
c02e56d86c861d4b: A person can be tormented by a wrong they did not commit — inherited from a parent, a sibling, or someone they never knew — and freedom begins with that wrong being named.
cec9c38aa6717d11: Binding and then loosing, knotting and then washing away — the ritual enacts the release it intends, so that the sufferer's body is taught the freedom his mind cannot yet reach.
4d4805fa5900a3e0: The forces that destroy have no sex, no kin, no place in any order, and hear no appeal — which is precisely what makes them terrible.
1a21bb387d605776: What is truly driven out does not come back.
1d5baffbbcf616ea: A relationship broken by wrongdoing is restored not by the offender's effort but by the offended one choosing to return.
27584da83d1e5961: A person can violate what is sacred to them without meaning to, and afterward live on the taste of their own grief.
9fdb381b3011bca2: It is possible to be punished for a transgression you cannot identify, which is a distinct and particular kind of anguish.
d9f8d1aa9332ee4f: The worst part of affliction is not the pain but that no one takes your hand while you bear it.
85d1e859ab5133bd: Suffering asks 'how long' and receives no answer, because a human being cannot know the reckoning that governs their own life; the terms of the accounting are hidden from the one being accounted for.
1e28b590b1cf51b7: Since no one can tell whether they are cursed or blessed, the only thing left to ask is that judgment, when it comes, be judgment that gives life.
e8830c43f49bb663: Ask that the anger you provoked subside as naturally and completely as the day ends.
0090ec116b6fddfb: The sun rising is the world being unlocked and opened, and its light claims the whole of both sky and earth.
63bb2d2bdeec0c01: The source of light raises those who are bowed down and holds up the weak; even the highest powers turn toward it, and everyone's praise becomes one word.
1ac496221ebd2b8e: The one who nourishes the heavens is still the one whom some refuse to turn toward.
9c9e1e0db728a0ec: The power that gives light is also the power that judges: it knows truth from falsehood, plants truth in the thinking of nations, and cannot be deceived by slander.
e2a39c50ed64cba0: Ask not only that the sufferer be healed and guided afterward, but that the healer be guided too.
6d131eb0b15e2c1a: At the moment light returns, everything above and below turns to face it — the crowds of earth no less than the powers of heaven.
5ab728825bbf68c9: The one who directs the whole human race is also asked to attend to a single suffering body; the request for justice and the request for mercy are made in the same breath.
d576d6db12e32208: The plea is that the wrong be pardoned, the illness lifted, and the one restored to life be surrounded thereafter by what restored him.
4d4033549c3e7300: Light destroys falsehood: it dissolves omens, spells, evil dreams, and malicious designs, and turns them toward a good end — so to take refuge in the truth is to be safe from them.
